skills.title: Create a Polkadot Account Programmatically
skills.description: Generates a new Polkadot SR25519 account (mnemonic, public key, SS58 address) using @polkadot/util-crypto and @polkadot/keyring. Use when you need to create a wallet, automate key generation, or produce a signing account for testing. Supports TypeScript (primary), Python (substrate-interface), and Rust (sp-core). Trigger phrases: 'create a Polkadot account', 'generate wallet keypair', 'new account', 'key generation'. Output: console-printed 12-word BIP39 mnemonic and SS58 address. No network connection required.

commands: npm init -y && npm pkg set type=module
steps.description: Create a new directory named 'account-creator' and initialize it as an ESM Node.js project. The 'type=module' flag is required — the @polkadot packages use ESM imports.
Install @polkadot/util-crypto (WASM-backed mnemonic generation and cryptoWaitReady), @polkadot/keyring (SR25519 keyring), and tsx (TypeScript executor).

Fetch the reference file and save it as 'create-account.ts'. No substitutions needed — the script generates a fresh random account on each execution. If the AI fetch tool is blocked, run: curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/polkadot-developers/polkadot-cookbook/main/polkadot-docs/chain-interactions/create-account/create-account.ts -o create-account.ts

steps.expected_output:
Address: <SS58-encoded Polkadot address>
Mnemonic: <12-word BIP39 phrase>
steps.description: Execute the script. It prints a new SS58 address and 12-word mnemonic to the console. Record both — the mnemonic is required to sign transactions with this account and cannot be recovered if lost. Never share the mnemonic.

files.description: Generates a new SR25519 keypair using mnemonicGenerate(); prints SS58 address and mnemonic

error_patterns.pattern: Error: Unable to initialize the WASM interface
error_patterns.cause: cryptoWaitReady() was not awaited before keyring operations.
error_patterns.resolution: Ensure cryptoWaitReady() is called and awaited at the start of main(). The reference file already does this; verify the file was saved correctly.

error_patterns.pattern: SyntaxError: Cannot use import statement in a module
error_patterns.cause: The project is not configured as ESM.
error_patterns.resolution: Run 'npm pkg set type=module' in the account-creator directory to enable ES modules.

error_patterns.pattern: Error: Cannot find module '@polkadot/util-crypto'
error_patterns.cause: Dependencies not installed.
error_patterns.resolution: Run 'npm install @polkadot/util-crypto @polkadot/keyring' in the account-creator directory.

supplementary_context.description: Load these pages when the user asks about next steps after account creation or about account concepts.

pages.title: Send Transactions with SDKs
pages.relevance: How to use the newly created account to sign and submit transactions on Polkadot Hub.

pages.title: Query Account Information with SDKs
pages.relevance: How to query the balance and account state of the newly created account.

examples.scenario: Common scenario: generate a fresh wallet
examples.user_says: Create a new Polkadot account for me
actions: Scaffold 'account-creator/' as an ESM Node.js project
Install @polkadot/util-crypto and @polkadot/keyring
Fetch and save create-account.ts
Run npx tsx create-account.ts
examples.result: A fresh SR25519 mnemonic phrase and SS58 address printed to console

examples.user_says: Generate a Polkadot account using Python
actions: Create a Python virtual environment in 'account-creator-python/'
Install substrate-interface via pip
Create create_account.py using generate_mnemonic() and Keypair.create_from_mnemonic()
Run python create_account.py
examples.result: SR25519 keypair generated; mnemonic and SS58 address printed to console

skills.title: Query Account Information with SDKs
skills.description: Queries Polkadot Hub for account balance, nonce, and account state using the Polkadot API (PAPI) TypeScript library. Use when you need to inspect an account's free/reserved/frozen balance or transaction count programmatically. Also supports Polkadot.js, Dedot, Python Substrate Interface, and Subxt. Trigger phrases: 'query account balance', 'check account info', 'get account nonce', 'read wallet balance'. No tokens required — read-only operation against any funded or unfunded address.

network: WebSocket RPC endpoint for Polkadot Hub or compatible chain (e.g., wss://asset-hub-paseo.dotters.network for TestNet)

commands: npm init -y && npm pkg set type=module
steps.description: Create a new directory and initialize an ESM Node.js project.
Install polkadot-api (PAPI) and TypeScript tooling.

Generate compile-time type descriptors for Polkadot Hub TestNet. This connects to the chain and downloads its metadata. Produces @polkadot-api/descriptors with the 'polkadotTestNet' export. If targeting a different network, replace the WebSocket URL and the descriptor name accordingly — note the name you choose, as it must match the import in query-account.ts.

Fetch the reference file and save it as 'query-account.ts'. Then make these two substitutions: (1) Replace 'INSERT_WS_ENDPOINT' with your WebSocket endpoint (e.g., 'wss://asset-hub-paseo.dotters.network'). (2) Replace 'INSERT_ACCOUNT_ADDRESS' with the SS58 address you want to query. Save the changes before running.

steps.expected_output: Account Information with Nonce, Consumers, Providers, Free Balance, Reserved Balance, Frozen Balance, and Total Balance
steps.description: Execute the script. Output will display account nonce, balance breakdown (free/reserved/frozen), and total balance in raw PAS units and human-readable format (divide raw value by 10^10 for PAS).

files.description: PAPI TypeScript script that queries System.Account storage and prints balance details

error_patterns.pattern: Error: Cannot find module '@polkadot-api/descriptors'
error_patterns.cause: PAPI type descriptors were not generated.
error_patterns.resolution: Run 'npx papi add polkadotTestNet -w wss://asset-hub-paseo.dotters.network' in the project directory.

error_patterns.pattern: WebSocket connection failed / ECONNREFUSED
error_patterns.cause: Incorrect or unreachable WebSocket endpoint URL.
error_patterns.resolution: Verify the WebSocket URL in query-account.ts is correct and the endpoint is accessible. For TestNet, use 'wss://asset-hub-paseo.dotters.network'.

error_patterns.pattern: Account not found / undefined accountInfo
error_patterns.cause: The queried address has never been funded and has no on-chain entry.
error_patterns.resolution: The account exists only once it receives tokens. Query a funded address, or fund the address using the Polkadot faucet at https://faucet.polkadot.io/ first.

supplementary_context.description: Load these pages when the user needs background on account data fields or wants to send transactions from the queried account.

pages.relevance: Full PAPI API reference for understanding query methods and typed API usage.

pages.title: Send Transactions with SDKs
pages.relevance: How to send a transaction from the account after verifying its balance.

skills.title: Query On-Chain State with SDKs
skills.description: Queries on-chain storage on Polkadot Hub using PAPI TypeScript library: System.Account for account balance and Assets pallet for USDT (asset ID 1984) metadata and balance. Use when you need to read chain state programmatically beyond account info — including asset balances and pallet storage. Also supports Polkadot.js, Dedot, Python Substrate Interface, and Subxt. Trigger phrases: 'query chain state', 'read on-chain storage', 'get asset balance', 'check USDT balance on Asset Hub'. No tokens required — read-only.

network: WebSocket RPC endpoint for Polkadot Hub (e.g., wss://polkadot-asset-hub-rpc.polkadot.io for mainnet or wss://asset-hub-paseo.dotters.network for testnet)

commands: npm init -y && npm pkg set type=module
steps.description: Create a new directory 'papi-query-example' and initialize an ESM Node.js project.
Install polkadot-api (PAPI) and TypeScript tooling.

steps.action: Generate PAPI type descriptors for Polkadot Hub
steps.description: Generate compile-time type descriptors using the well-known 'polkadot_asset_hub' chain name. This produces the 'pah' export in @polkadot-api/descriptors. For a different network, use the appropriate -n value from the PAPI well-known chains list, or use -w with a WebSocket URL.

steps.action: Fetch and configure the balance query script
steps.description: Fetch the reference file and save it as 'query-balance.ts'. Make these two substitutions: (1) Replace 'INSERT_WS_ENDPOINT' with your WebSocket endpoint (e.g., 'wss://polkadot-asset-hub-rpc.polkadot.io' for mainnet). (2) Replace 'INSERT_ADDRESS' with the SS58 address whose balance you want to query. Save the file.

steps.action: Fetch and configure the asset query script
steps.description: Fetch the reference file and save it as 'query-asset.ts'. Make these two substitutions: (1) Replace 'INSERT_WS_ENDPOINT' with the same WebSocket endpoint used above. (2) Replace 'INSERT_ADDRESS' with the address to check for USDT balance. The script queries asset ID 1984 (USDT) by default; change the asset ID constant if targeting a different asset. Save the file.

steps.expected_output: Account nonce and balance info, then asset metadata and balance for the specified address
steps.description: Execute both scripts in sequence. query-balance.ts shows the native PAS balance. query-asset.ts shows the USDT asset metadata and the address's USDT balance (0 if not holding USDT).

files.description: Queries System.Account storage for native PAS balance using the 'pah' descriptor
Queries Assets pallet for USDT (asset ID 1984) metadata and address balance

error_patterns.pattern: Error: Cannot find module '@polkadot-api/descriptors'
error_patterns.cause: PAPI type descriptors were not generated.
error_patterns.resolution: Run 'npx papi add pah -n polkadot_asset_hub' in the project directory.

error_patterns.pattern: WebSocket connection failed / ECONNREFUSED
error_patterns.cause: Incorrect or unreachable WebSocket endpoint.
error_patterns.resolution: Verify the WebSocket URL. For Polkadot Hub mainnet use 'wss://polkadot-asset-hub-rpc.polkadot.io', for testnet use 'wss://asset-hub-paseo.dotters.network'.

error_patterns.pattern: query-asset returns null or empty for the address
error_patterns.cause: The address does not hold the queried asset (USDT asset ID 1984).
error_patterns.resolution: This is expected for addresses with no USDT balance. The metadata query will still succeed; only the per-address balance query returns empty.

skills.title: Call Runtime APIs with SDKs
skills.description: Executes Polkadot SDK runtime APIs — AccountNonceApi.account_nonce and Metadata.metadata_versions — using the PAPI TypeScript library. Use when you need computed results from runtime logic rather than raw storage reads, such as the current transaction nonce or supported metadata versions. Also supports Polkadot.js, Dedot, Python Substrate Interface, and Subxt. Trigger phrases: 'call runtime API', 'get account nonce via runtime', 'query metadata versions', 'AccountNonceApi'. No tokens required — read-only operation.

network: WebSocket RPC endpoint for Polkadot Hub TestNet (e.g., wss://asset-hub-paseo.dotters.network)

commands: npm init -y && npm pkg set type=module
steps.description: Create a new directory 'papi-runtime-api-example' and initialize an ESM Node.js project.
Install polkadot-api (PAPI) and TypeScript tooling.

steps.action: Generate PAPI type descriptors for Polkadot Hub TestNet
steps.description: Generate compile-time type descriptors for Polkadot Hub TestNet. Produces the 'polkadotTestNet' export used in the runtime-apis.ts script. If targeting a different network, replace the WebSocket URL and update the descriptor name accordingly.

Fetch the reference file and save it as 'runtime-apis.ts'. Make these two substitutions: (1) Replace 'INSERT_WS_ENDPOINT' with your WebSocket endpoint (e.g., 'wss://asset-hub-paseo.dotters.network'). (2) Replace 'INSERT_ADDRESS' with a valid SS58 address to query the nonce for. Save the file.

steps.expected_output:
Account Nonce: <number>
Supported Metadata Versions: [<list of version numbers>]
steps.description: Execute the script. It calls AccountNonceApi.account_nonce for the provided address and Metadata.metadata_versions. Nonce for a new address is 0. Metadata versions list shows supported runtime metadata formats (e.g., [14, 15]).

files.description: Calls AccountNonceApi.account_nonce and Metadata.metadata_versions using PAPI typed API

error_patterns.pattern: Error: Cannot find module '@polkadot-api/descriptors'
error_patterns.cause: PAPI type descriptors not generated.
error_patterns.resolution: Run 'npx papi add polkadotTestNet -w wss://asset-hub-paseo.dotters.network' in the project directory.

error_patterns.pattern: api.apis.AccountNonceApi is undefined
error_patterns.cause: The target chain does not expose AccountNonceApi, or the descriptor was generated for a different chain.
error_patterns.resolution: Verify the chain exposes AccountNonceApi in its runtime metadata. For Polkadot Hub TestNet, this API is available. If you generated descriptors for a different chain, regenerate with the correct endpoint.

error_patterns.pattern: WebSocket connection failed / timeout on papi add
error_patterns.cause: The testnet endpoint is temporarily unavailable.
error_patterns.resolution: Polkadot Hub TestNet can be unstable. Wait a few minutes and retry. If the endpoint remains down, check the Polkadot Discord status channel. Alternatively, try a different public endpoint.

skills.title: Send Transactions with SDKs
skills.description: Constructs, signs, and submits a Balances.transfer_keep_alive extrinsic to Polkadot Hub using the PAPI TypeScript library. Use when you need to send tokens programmatically from a funded account. Also supports Polkadot.js, Dedot, Python Substrate Interface, and Subxt. Requires a funded account mnemonic and testnet PAS tokens. Trigger phrases: 'send a transaction', 'transfer tokens programmatically', 'sign and submit extrinsic', 'send PAS balance'. Uses dotenv to protect the sender mnemonic.

network: WebSocket RPC endpoint for Polkadot Hub (e.g., wss://asset-hub-paseo.dotters.network for testnet)
tokens: Testnet PAS tokens in the sender account — get from https://faucet.polkadot.io/?parachain=1111 (Polkadot Hub faucet)
wallet: An SR25519 account mnemonic phrase for the sender account, funded with testnet PAS

env_vars.name: SENDER_MNEMONIC
env_vars.description: 12-word BIP39 mnemonic phrase for the sender account. Must be the mnemonic for a funded account. Never commit this value to version control.

env_vars.name: WS_ENDPOINT
env_vars.description: WebSocket RPC endpoint for the target chain (e.g., wss://asset-hub-paseo.dotters.network).

env_vars.name: DEST_ADDRESS
env_vars.description: SS58-encoded recipient address for the balance transfer.

commands: npm init -y && npm pkg set type=module
steps.description: Create a new directory 'papi-send-tx-example' and initialize an ESM Node.js project.
Install polkadot-api, @polkadot/keyring (for SR25519 signing), dotenv (for secure credential loading), and TypeScript tooling.

Generate compile-time type descriptors for Polkadot Hub TestNet. Produces the 'polkadotTestNet' export used in the send-transfer script. If targeting a different network, replace the endpoint and update the descriptor name in step 5.

Create a .env file with three empty placeholders and a .gitignore that excludes it. Stop here and ask the user to edit the .env file directly — fill in SENDER_MNEMONIC with their 12-word mnemonic, confirm WS_ENDPOINT is correct, and set DEST_ADDRESS to the recipient address. Do NOT ask for the mnemonic in chat. Wait for the user to confirm the .env is filled before proceeding.

steps.action: Fetch and adapt the send-transfer script
steps.description: Fetch the reference file and save it as 'send-transfer.ts'. Then apply these modifications: (1) Add 'import "dotenv/config";' as the very first line of the file. (2) Replace 'const POLKADOT_TESTNET_RPC = \'INSERT_WS_ENDPOINT\';' with 'const POLKADOT_TESTNET_RPC = process.env.WS_ENDPOINT!;'. (3) Replace 'const SENDER_MNEMONIC = \'INSERT_MNEMONIC\';' with 'const SENDER_MNEMONIC = process.env.SENDER_MNEMONIC!;'. (4) Replace 'const DEST_ADDRESS = \'INSERT_DEST_ADDRESS\';' with 'const DEST_ADDRESS = process.env.DEST_ADDRESS!;'. Save the file. If the AI fetch is blocked, run: curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/polkadot-developers/polkadot-cookbook/main/polkadot-docs/chain-interactions/send-transactions/with-sdks/papi/send-transfer.ts -o send-transfer.ts

steps.action: Verify the sender account is funded
steps.description: Before running, confirm the sender account has testnet PAS tokens. If not, direct the user to https://faucet.polkadot.io/?parachain=1111, paste the sender address, and request tokens. Wait for the faucet transaction to confirm (typically 1-2 minutes) before proceeding.

steps.expected_output:
Connected to Polkadot Testnet
Signing and submitting transaction...
Transaction submitted with hash: 0x<tx-hash>
steps.description: Execute the script. It connects to the chain, signs the Balances.transfer_keep_alive extrinsic with the sender mnemonic, submits it, and prints the transaction hash. Save the transaction hash — needed in step 8 to verify.

files.description: PAPI TypeScript script that signs and submits a Balances.transfer_keep_alive extrinsic

error_patterns.pattern: Error: Cannot find module '@polkadot-api/descriptors'
error_patterns.cause: PAPI type descriptors not generated.
error_patterns.resolution: Run 'npx papi add polkadotTestNet -w wss://asset-hub-paseo.dotters.network' in the project directory.

error_patterns.pattern: Invalid mnemonic phrase / Error creating keypair
error_patterns.cause: SENDER_MNEMONIC in .env is incorrect, has extra spaces, or is not a valid BIP39 phrase.
error_patterns.resolution: Open .env, verify SENDER_MNEMONIC is a valid 12-word BIP39 phrase with single spaces between words, and no leading/trailing spaces.

error_patterns.pattern: Error: 1010: Invalid Transaction: Inability to pay some fees
error_patterns.cause: The sender account has insufficient PAS balance to cover the transfer amount plus gas fees.
error_patterns.resolution: Get testnet tokens from https://faucet.polkadot.io/?parachain=1111. Alternatively, reduce the AMOUNT constant in send-transfer.ts to 1_000_000_000n (0.1 PAS).

error_patterns.pattern: Transaction submitted but not confirmed / stuck pending
error_patterns.cause: Polkadot Hub TestNet can be unstable and drop transactions under load.
error_patterns.resolution: Wait 1-2 minutes and check the block explorer for the tx hash. If not confirmed, re-run the script. The nonce increments per submitted transaction — if you see 'nonce too low', the previous transaction was included despite appearing to fail.

Re-run send-transfer.ts

skills.title: Install the Polkadot SDK
skills.description: Installs all Polkadot SDK build dependencies and compiles the SDK from source on macOS, Linux (Ubuntu/Debian/Arch/Fedora/OpenSUSE), and Windows WSL. Use when setting up a new development machine for Polkadot parachain or runtime development. Covers OS package installation, Rust toolchain setup, wasm32 target addition, git clone, and a full cargo build. Trigger phrases: 'install Polkadot SDK', 'set up parachain dev environment', 'install Rust for Polkadot', 'build polkadot-sdk', 'set up Substrate environment'. Expected outcome: polkadot binary executes and prints its version string.

runtime: macOS 10.7+, Ubuntu/Debian/Arch/Fedora/OpenSUSE Linux, or Windows 10 v2004+ with WSL2
At least 10 GB free disk space and 8 GB RAM (16 GB recommended for parallel compilation)
Broadband internet connection (downloads several GB of Rust crates and dependencies)

steps.description:
Install the packages required to compile the Polkadot SDK. Run the command block for your OS:

macOS: First install Homebrew if missing (/bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/master/install.sh)"), then run: brew install protobuf openssl cmake

Ubuntu or Debian: sudo apt install --assume-yes git clang curl libssl-dev llvm libclang-dev libudev-dev make protobuf-compiler

Arch Linux: pacman -Syu --needed --noconfirm curl git clang make protobuf

Fedora: sudo dnf update && sudo dnf install clang curl git openssl-devel make protobuf-compiler

OpenSUSE: sudo zypper install clang curl git openssl-devel llvm-devel libudev-devel make protobuf

Windows WSL (Ubuntu): open the WSL Ubuntu terminal, then run: sudo apt install --assume-yes git clang curl libssl-dev llvm libclang-dev libudev-dev make protobuf-compiler

Download and run the rustup installer. When prompted, accept the default installation (press Enter or type 1). After installation completes, source the Cargo environment so the current shell session can find the Rust toolchain. On Windows WSL, use source ~/.cargo/env; on some Linux distributions use source $HOME/.cargo/env.

Set the stable toolchain as default, update it to the latest release, and add the WebAssembly compilation target plus the Rust source component. Both wasm32-unknown-unknown and rust-src are required for building Substrate/Polkadot SDK runtime WASM blobs.
Clone the official Polkadot SDK repository. The clone is several hundred MB and may take a few minutes on a slower connection. All subsequent steps run from inside the polkadot-sdk directory.

Build the entire SDK in release mode. This compiles all components and verifies the full toolchain. Expect 20-60 minutes on typical developer hardware. A successful build produces binaries in target/release/ including polkadot, polkadot-parachain, polkadot-omni-node, and substrate-node.

Confirm the polkadot binary runs and prints its version. If you see version output without errors, the development environment is correctly configured and ready for Polkadot SDK work. Save this version string — needed when matching binary versions in parachain template setup.

error_patterns.pattern: error: linker 'cc' not found
error_patterns.cause: C compiler and build tools are not installed.
error_patterns.resolution: On Ubuntu/Debian run: sudo apt install build-essential clang. On macOS run: xcode-select --install. On Fedora run: sudo dnf install gcc.

error_patterns.pattern: error: failed to run custom build command for 'openssl-sys'
error_patterns.cause: OpenSSL development headers are missing.
error_patterns.resolution: On Ubuntu/Debian run: sudo apt install libssl-dev. On macOS run: brew install openssl. On Fedora run: sudo dnf install openssl-devel.

error_patterns.pattern: error[E0463]: can't find crate for 'std' ... target wasm32-unknown-unknown
error_patterns.cause: The wasm32-unknown-unknown target is not installed.
error_patterns.resolution: Run: rustup target add wasm32-unknown-unknown && rustup component add rust-src

error_patterns.pattern: LLVM ERROR: out of memory / build killed / OOM
error_patterns.cause: Insufficient RAM for the default number of parallel compilation jobs.
error_patterns.resolution: Limit parallelism: cargo build --release --locked -j 4. On Linux you can also temporarily increase swap: sudo fallocate -l 8G /swapfile && sudo chmod 600 /swapfile && sudo mkswap /swapfile && sudo swapon /swapfile

error_patterns.pattern: error: failed to compile protobuf files / protoc not found
error_patterns.cause: The protobuf compiler is missing.
error_patterns.resolution: On Ubuntu/Debian: sudo apt install protobuf-compiler. On macOS: brew install protobuf. On Fedora: sudo dnf install protobuf-compiler.

supplementary_context.description: Load when the user asks about next steps after installing the SDK or about the parachain template.

examples.scenario: Common scenario: fresh Ubuntu developer machine
examples.user_says: Install the Polkadot SDK on my Linux machine
actions: Run apt install for required packages (git, clang, libssl-dev, etc.)
Install Rust via rustup and source ~/.cargo/env
Run rustup default stable && rustup target add wasm32-unknown-unknown && rustup component add rust-src
Clone https://github.com/paritytech/polkadot-sdk.git and cd polkadot-sdk
Run cargo build --release --locked
Verify with ./target/release/polkadot --version
examples.result: Polkadot binary prints version string — SDK toolchain ready for parachain development

examples.scenario: Edge case: build runs out of memory on a machine with limited RAM
examples.user_says: The build keeps getting killed or runs out of memory
actions: Re-run with reduced parallelism: cargo build --release --locked -j 4
If still failing on Linux, add swap: sudo fallocate -l 8G /swapfile && sudo chmod 600 /swapfile && sudo mkswap /swapfile && sudo swapon /swapfile, then retry
examples.result: Build completes with reduced parallel jobs or additional swap space

skills.title: Deploy a Basic Smart Contract with Hardhat
skills.description: Scaffolds a Hardhat v2 TypeScript project, configures it for Polkadot Hub TestNet (chain ID 420420417), creates a Storage.sol contract, compiles it, and deploys via Hardhat Ignition. Use when deploying a first EVM smart contract to Polkadot Hub using a professional Hardhat workflow. Uses dotenv for private key security. Trigger phrases: 'deploy smart contract with Hardhat', 'Hardhat deployment Polkadot', 'deploy Storage contract', 'Hardhat Ignition deploy'. Requires testnet PAS tokens. Do NOT use for PVM (PolkaVM) deployments; use set-up-hardhat-pvm for that.

network: Polkadot Hub TestNet RPC: https://services.polkadothub-rpc.com/testnet (chain ID 420420417)
tokens: Testnet PAS tokens for gas fees — get from https://faucet.polkadot.io/ (Polkadot Hub TestNet base fee is 1000 gwei)
wallet: An EVM private key (0x-prefixed) for a funded testnet account

env_vars.name: PRIVATE_KEY
env_vars.description: 0x-prefixed EVM private key for the deployer account. Must correspond to a testnet account funded with PAS tokens. Never commit to version control.

steps.action: Create project directory and initialize npm
steps.description: Create a new directory named 'hardhat-deployment' and initialize a Node.js project. Do NOT run npx hardhat init at this stage — interactive init is non-deterministic across Hardhat versions. All project files will be fetched from the reference repository in subsequent steps.

Install Hardhat v2 with the Toolbox plugin, TypeScript support, and dotenv for secure private key loading. The dotenv package is required — the skill uses process.env.PRIVATE_KEY instead of Hardhat's interactive vars system, which does not work in agent shells.
Create the contracts and ignition/modules directories that Hardhat expects. The test directory is optional and not needed for this deployment workflow.

steps.action: Create .env file and .gitignore for private key
steps.description: Create a .env file with an empty PRIVATE_KEY placeholder and a .gitignore entry to prevent accidental commits. Stop here and ask the user to edit the .env file directly — fill in PRIVATE_KEY with their 0x-prefixed EVM private key for a testnet account funded with PAS. Do NOT ask for the private key in chat. Wait for the user to confirm the .env file is filled before proceeding to step 5.

Fetch the reference file and save it as 'hardhat.config.ts'. Then apply all of the following modifications before saving:
(1) Add 'import "dotenv/config";' as the very first line of the file.
(2) Replace any 'vars.get("PRIVATE_KEY")' or "vars.get('PRIVATE_KEY')" with 'process.env.PRIVATE_KEY as string'.
(3) In the polkadotTestnet network block, add 'gasPrice: 5000000000000,' (5000 gwei — 5x the TestNet base fee of 1000 gwei; prevents stuck transactions).
(4) Add an 'ignition: { requiredConfirmations: 1 }' top-level key to the config object (zero confirmations only works on local nodes — on TestNet it causes IGN401 dropped-transaction errors).
If the AI fetch tool is blocked, run: curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/polkadot-developers/polkadot-cookbook/main/polkadot-docs/smart-contracts/basic-hardhat/hardhat.config.ts -o hardhat.config.ts

Fetch the reference file and save it as 'contracts/Storage.sol'. No substitutions needed — this is a generic Storage contract with get/set functions. If the AI fetch tool is blocked, run: curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/polkadot-developers/polkadot-cookbook/main/polkadot-docs/smart-contracts/basic-hardhat/contracts/Storage.sol -o contracts/Storage.sol

Compile Storage.sol. Successful output shows 'Compiled 1 Solidity file successfully' and creates artifacts in the artifacts/ directory. If compilation fails with 'invalid opcode: MCOPY', add 'evmVersion: "cancun"' inside the solidity.settings.evmVersion field of hardhat.config.ts and recompile.

Fetch the reference file and save it as 'ignition/modules/Storage.ts'. No substitutions needed — the module deploys the Storage contract by name. If the AI fetch tool is blocked, run: curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/polkadot-developers/polkadot-cookbook/main/polkadot-docs/smart-contracts/basic-hardhat/ignition/modules/Storage.ts -o ignition/modules/Storage.ts

steps.action: Deploy the contract to Polkadot Hub TestNet
commands: npx hardhat ignition deploy ignition/modules/Storage.ts --network polkadotTestnet
steps.description: Deploy the contract. Hardhat Ignition will prompt to confirm the target network and chain ID — delegate this confirmation to the user (they must type the network name or press Enter). After confirmation, Ignition broadcasts the deployment transaction. Save the deployed contract address printed at the end — needed for contract interaction. If the transaction becomes stuck, see error_patterns for the IGN401 recovery procedure.

files.description: Hardhat config with polkadotTestnet network (RPC, chain ID, accounts via vars). Requires dotenv conversion and gasPrice addition per skill steps.
Simple Storage contract with uint256 store/retrieve functions
Hardhat Ignition module that deploys the Storage contract

error_patterns.pattern: Error: vars is not defined / Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'get')
error_patterns.cause: The hardhat.config.ts still contains vars.get('PRIVATE_KEY') instead of the dotenv substitution.
error_patterns.resolution: Open hardhat.config.ts, replace vars.get('PRIVATE_KEY') with process.env.PRIVATE_KEY as string, and add 'import "dotenv/config";' as the first line of the file.

error_patterns.pattern: Error: insufficient funds / Priority is too low
error_patterns.cause: The deployer account has no testnet PAS tokens, or the gasPrice in hardhat.config.ts is below the network minimum (1000 gwei base fee).
error_patterns.resolution: Get testnet tokens from https://faucet.polkadot.io/. Also verify the polkadotTestnet network block in hardhat.config.ts includes gasPrice: 5000000000000 (5000 gwei).

error_patterns.pattern: IGN401: Transaction dropped / Transaction Already Imported on retry
error_patterns.cause: Ignition lost track of the deployment transaction. Often caused by requiredConfirmations: 0 or gasPrice below the base fee.
error_patterns.resolution: First check if the contract was actually deployed: look in ignition/deployments/ for deployed_addresses.json. If deployed, import the existing deployment. If not deployed: (1) delete the ignition/deployments/ directory, (2) ensure hardhat.config.ts has gasPrice: 5000000000000 and ignition.requiredConfirmations: 1, (3) redeploy.

error_patterns.pattern: CompilationError: Invalid opcode: MCOPY
error_patterns.cause: The Solidity contract uses OpenZeppelin v5.4.0+ which requires the Cancun EVM version.
error_patterns.resolution: In hardhat.config.ts, add evmVersion: 'cancun' inside the solidity compiler settings: solidity: { version: '0.8.28', settings: { evmVersion: 'cancun' } }

skills.title: Deploy an ERC-20 Token Using Hardhat
skills.description: Clones the polkadot-developers/revm-hardhat-examples ERC-20 template, installs dependencies, configures dotenv for private key security, compiles the OpenZeppelin ERC-20 contract, runs the test suite against TestNet, and deploys via Hardhat Ignition. Use when deploying a standards-compliant ERC-20 fungible token on Polkadot Hub. Requires testnet PAS tokens. Trigger phrases: 'deploy ERC-20 token', 'deploy fungible token Polkadot', 'ERC-20 Hardhat Polkadot', 'deploy MyToken'. Uses OpenZeppelin Contracts v5.4.0+ — requires evmVersion cancun.

network: Polkadot Hub TestNet RPC: https://services.polkadothub-rpc.com/testnet (chain ID 420420417)
tokens: Testnet PAS tokens for gas fees and test transactions — get from https://faucet.polkadot.io/ (base fee: 1000 gwei)
wallet: An EVM private key (0x-prefixed) for a funded testnet account

env_vars.name: PRIVATE_KEY
env_vars.description: 0x-prefixed EVM private key for the deployer/test account. Must be funded with testnet PAS. Never commit to version control.

steps.description: Clone the revm-hardhat-examples repository and navigate into the erc20-hardhat subdirectory. This template contains a pre-configured Hardhat project with an OpenZeppelin ERC-20 contract, test suite, and Ignition deployment module.

Install all template dependencies from package.json, then additionally install dotenv. dotenv is needed to replace Hardhat's interactive vars system (which cannot be used in agent shells) with a .env-based private key approach.

steps.action: Create .env file and .gitignore for private key
steps.description: Create a .env file with an empty PRIVATE_KEY placeholder. Stop here and ask the user to edit .env directly — fill in PRIVATE_KEY with their 0x-prefixed EVM private key for an account funded with testnet PAS. Do NOT ask for the private key in chat. Wait for the user to confirm .env is filled before proceeding.

steps.action: Convert hardhat.config.ts to use dotenv and add gas configuration
steps.description:
Open hardhat.config.ts and apply all of the following modifications:
(1) Add 'import "dotenv/config";' as the very first line of the file.
(2) Replace any vars.get('PRIVATE_KEY') or vars.get("PRIVATE_KEY") with process.env.PRIVATE_KEY as string.
(3) Remove the 'import { vars } from "hardhat/config";' line if present.
(4) In the polkadotTestnet network block, add 'gasPrice: 5000000000000,' (5000 gwei — 5x the TestNet 1000 gwei base fee).
(5) Confirm or add 'evmVersion: "cancun"' in the solidity compiler settings block — required because the template uses OpenZeppelin Contracts v5.4.0+ which uses the mcopy opcode from the Cancun EVM upgrade.
(6) Confirm ignition.requiredConfirmations is 1 (not 0).
Save the file.
Compile the ERC-20 contract. If compilation fails with 'Invalid opcode: MCOPY', ensure evmVersion: 'cancun' is set in the solidity compiler settings and recompile.

Run the predefined test suite against Polkadot Hub TestNet. Tests verify token name/symbol, owner, initial supply, minting, and balance tracking. This consumes testnet PAS for gas. If the account is low on PAS, visit https://faucet.polkadot.io/ first. Save the test output — it confirms the contract logic is correct before deployment.

Deploy the ERC-20 contract via Hardhat Ignition. Ignition will prompt to confirm the target network and chain ID — delegate this confirmation to the user. After confirmation, the deployment transaction is broadcast. Save the contract address printed on success — needed for token interaction and verification.

error_patterns.pattern: CompilationError: Invalid opcode: MCOPY
error_patterns.cause: OpenZeppelin Contracts v5.4.0+ uses the mcopy opcode which requires Cancun EVM version. The hardhat.config.ts does not specify evmVersion: 'cancun'.
error_patterns.resolution: In hardhat.config.ts, set evmVersion: 'cancun' inside solidity.settings: solidity: { version: '0.8.28', settings: { evmVersion: 'cancun' } }. Then recompile.

error_patterns.pattern: Error: vars is not defined / Cannot read properties of undefined
error_patterns.cause: hardhat.config.ts still uses Hardhat's vars.get() system rather than dotenv.
error_patterns.resolution: Add 'import "dotenv/config";' as the first line of hardhat.config.ts and replace vars.get('PRIVATE_KEY') with process.env.PRIVATE_KEY as string.

error_patterns.pattern: Error: insufficient funds / Priority is too low
error_patterns.cause: Deployer account has insufficient PAS, or gasPrice is below the TestNet 1000 gwei base fee.
error_patterns.resolution: Get testnet tokens at https://faucet.polkadot.io/. Verify hardhat.config.ts polkadotTestnet block includes gasPrice: 5000000000000.

error_patterns.pattern: IGN401: Transaction dropped / Transaction Already Imported
error_patterns.cause: Ignition lost track of deployment, commonly due to missing gasPrice or requiredConfirmations: 0.
error_patterns.resolution: Delete ignition/deployments/, ensure gasPrice: 5000000000000 and ignition.requiredConfirmations: 1 in config, then redeploy.

skills.title: Set Up Hardhat with the Polkadot Plugin (PVM)
skills.description: Sets up a Hardhat project using the @parity/hardhat-polkadot plugin and the resolc compiler to compile Solidity to PolkaVM (PVM) bytecode. Use when targeting Polkadot Hub's PVM runtime rather than the standard EVM runtime. Covers project initialization, resolc installation, dotenv private key setup, TestNet config with gasPrice, and a compile-verify step. Trigger phrases: 'Hardhat PVM', 'hardhat-polkadot plugin', 'compile to PolkaVM', 'resolc Hardhat', 'PVM smart contract Polkadot'. Do NOT use this skill for standard EVM deployments; use set-up-hardhat-evm instead.

runtime: Node.js v22.5+ and npm v10.9.0+ (required for @parity/hardhat-polkadot compatibility)
network: Polkadot Hub TestNet RPC: https://services.polkadothub-rpc.com/testnet (chain ID 420420417)
tokens: Testnet PAS tokens — get from https://faucet.polkadot.io/ (TestNet base fee: 1000 gwei)
wallet: An EVM private key (0x-prefixed) for a funded testnet account

env_vars.name: PRIVATE_KEY
env_vars.description: 0x-prefixed EVM private key for the deployer account. Must be funded with testnet PAS. Never commit to version control.

steps.action: Create project directory and initialize npm
steps.description: Create a new directory and initialize a Node.js project.

steps.action: Install the Polkadot Hardhat plugin and resolc compiler
steps.description: Install the @parity/hardhat-polkadot plugin (provides the Hardhat task runner for PVM), the @parity/resolc compiler (compiles Solidity to PVM bytecode), and dotenv (for secure private key loading). Do not pin specific versions unless the docs page specifies them — use latest.

steps.action: Initialize the Hardhat PVM project structure
steps.description: Run the project creation wizard. This is an interactive command — delegate it to the user. The wizard creates the contracts/, ignition/, and test/ directories and places a sample contract and deployment module. After the wizard completes, instruct the user to confirm which sample files were created before proceeding.

steps.action: Install all project dependencies after init
steps.description: Install any additional dependencies added by the init wizard, then add ignition/deployments/ to .gitignore to avoid committing deployment state to version control.

steps.action: Create .env file and .gitignore entry for private key
steps.description: Create a .env file with a PRIVATE_KEY placeholder. Stop here and ask the user to edit .env directly — fill in PRIVATE_KEY with their 0x-prefixed EVM private key for a testnet account funded with PAS. Do NOT ask for the private key in chat. Wait for the user to confirm .env is filled before proceeding.

steps.action: Fetch and configure hardhat.config.ts for TestNet
steps.description:
Fetch the reference hardhat.config.ts and replace the existing one in the project root. Then apply all of the following modifications:
(1) Add 'import "dotenv/config";' as the very first line.
(2) Replace any vars.get('PRIVATE_KEY') with process.env.PRIVATE_KEY as string.
(3) Confirm the polkadotTestnet network block contains: url: 'https://services.polkadothub-rpc.com/testnet', chainId: 420420417, and accounts: [process.env.PRIVATE_KEY as string].
(4) Add 'gasPrice: 5000000000000,' to the polkadotTestnet block (5000 gwei — 5x the 1000 gwei TestNet base fee).
(5) Set or confirm 'ignition: { requiredConfirmations: 1 }' in the top-level config object.
Save the file.
If the AI fetch tool is blocked, run: curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/polkadot-developers/polkadot-cookbook/main/polkadot-docs/smart-contracts/dev-environments/hardhat-pvm/hardhat.config.ts -o hardhat.config.ts

steps.action: Compile the sample contract to verify setup
steps.description: Compile the sample contract generated by the init wizard. Successful compilation confirms resolc is working and the project structure is correct. The compiled artifacts appear in artifacts/contracts/. If compilation fails with resolc errors, verify @parity/resolc is installed and the version matches what the hardhat.config.ts specifies in the resolc field.

files.description: Hardhat config with @parity/hardhat-polkadot plugin, resolc compiler settings, polkadotTestnet and localNode networks

error_patterns.pattern: Error: Cannot find module '@parity/hardhat-polkadot' / resolc not found
error_patterns.cause: The Polkadot plugin or resolc compiler was not installed.
error_patterns.resolution: Run: npm install --save-dev @parity/hardhat-polkadot @parity/resolc in the project directory.

error_patterns.pattern: Compilation fails with resolc errors / unsupported Solidity version
error_patterns.cause: The resolc compiler version does not support the contract's Solidity pragma version.
error_patterns.resolution: Check the @parity/resolc version installed and the Solidity pragma in the contract. Update the version field in hardhat.config.ts resolc settings to match what is installed, or downgrade the Solidity pragma in the contract to a supported version.

error_patterns.pattern: Error: vars is not defined
error_patterns.cause: hardhat.config.ts uses Hardhat's vars.get() system instead of dotenv.
error_patterns.resolution: Add 'import "dotenv/config";' as the first line of hardhat.config.ts and replace vars.get('PRIVATE_KEY') with process.env.PRIVATE_KEY as string.

error_patterns.pattern: Deployment hangs on local node / requiredConfirmations: 0
error_patterns.cause: The ignition config has requiredConfirmations: 0, which causes Ignition to misinterpret pending transactions as dropped on local nodes that don't produce blocks instantly.
error_patterns.resolution: Set ignition.requiredConfirmations: 1 in hardhat.config.ts.

error_patterns.pattern: Binary permission issues on macOS (binary is quarantined)
error_patterns.cause: macOS Gatekeeper quarantines downloaded binaries for dev-node and eth-rpc adapter.
error_patterns.resolution: Run: xattr -d com.apple.quarantine /path/to/binary. Also ensure executable permission: chmod +x /path/to/binary.

(5) Add 'gasPrice: 5000000000000,' to the polkadotTestnet block (5000 gwei — 5x the TestNet 1000 gwei base fee; prevents stuck deployment transactions).

Save the file.
If the AI fetch is blocked: curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/polkadot-developers/polkadot-cookbook/main/polkadot-docs/smart-contracts/dev-environments/hardhat-evm/hardhat.config.ts -o hardhat.config.ts

error_patterns.pattern: Error: vars is not defined

error_patterns.resolution: Add 'import "dotenv/config";' as the first line of hardhat.config.ts and replace vars.get('PRIVATE_KEY') with process.env.PRIVATE_KEY as string.

error_patterns.pattern: Cannot find module 'ts-node' / TypeScript compilation error on npx hardhat
error_patterns.cause: TypeScript dependencies were not installed.
error_patterns.resolution: Run: npm install --save-dev typescript ts-node @types/node in the project directory.

Create .env with PRIVATE_KEY= and add .env to .gitignore

skills.title: Query On-Chain Data with Sidecar REST API
skills.description: Runs the Substrate API Sidecar REST service connected to Polkadot Hub TestNet and queries on-chain data via curl. Use when you need account balances, asset info, or block data without writing SDK code. Covers Sidecar installation, startup with a TestNet endpoint, and the primary query endpoints: /accounts/{addr}/balance-info, /assets/{id}/asset-info, and /blocks/{id}. Trigger phrases: 'query with Sidecar', 'Sidecar REST API', 'curl Polkadot balance', 'query chain data REST', 'Substrate API Sidecar'. No private key required — read-only operations only.

runtime: Node.js v18+ and npm (to install and run @substrate/api-sidecar)
curl (pre-installed on macOS and Linux; use WSL on Windows)
jq (optional, for formatted JSON output — install with: apt install jq  or  brew install jq)
network: WebSocket RPC endpoint for Polkadot Hub TestNet: wss://asset-hub-paseo.dotters.network

steps.description: Install Sidecar globally. This makes the 'substrate-api-sidecar' command available in your shell. If you prefer not to install globally, invoke it with 'npx @substrate/api-sidecar' in step 2 instead.

steps.action: Start Sidecar connected to Polkadot Hub TestNet
steps.description: Start Sidecar with SAS_SUBSTRATE_URL pointing to the Polkadot Hub TestNet WebSocket endpoint. The service listens on http://127.0.0.1:8080/ by default. Run this in a dedicated terminal and keep it running while executing the following steps. If using npx: SAS_SUBSTRATE_URL=wss://asset-hub-paseo.dotters.network npx @substrate/api-sidecar. Polkadot Hub TestNet can be unstable — if the WebSocket connection drops, restart Sidecar.

Query the balance-info endpoint. Replace 'INSERT_SS58_ADDRESS' with the target SS58 address. Response fields: free — spendable planck; reserved — locked/staked; miscFrozen / feeFrozen — frozen amounts; nonce — transaction count. Divide raw planck values by 10^10 to convert to PAS. Omit '| jq .' if jq is not installed.

The first command fetches the latest finalized block; the second fetches block 1000 by number. Replace '1000' with any block number. The response includes block number, hash, parentHash, stateRoot, extrinsicsRoot, and extrinsics list.

Query metadata for asset ID 1984 (USDT on Polkadot Hub). Replace '1984' with any registered asset ID. If the asset does not exist on TestNet, items will be empty — this is expected since not all mainnet assets are bridged to TestNet.

error_patterns.pattern: ECONNREFUSED 127.0.0.1:8080
error_patterns.cause: Sidecar is not running or not yet ready.
error_patterns.resolution: Start Sidecar in a separate terminal: SAS_SUBSTRATE_URL=wss://asset-hub-paseo.dotters.network substrate-api-sidecar. Wait for the 'SAS listening' message before sending curl requests.

error_patterns.pattern: Sidecar starts but shows repeated WebSocket error messages
error_patterns.cause: Polkadot Hub TestNet WebSocket endpoint is temporarily unavailable. TestNet drops connections under load.
error_patterns.resolution: Wait 2-5 minutes and restart Sidecar. Check the Polkadot Discord for TestNet outage notices.

error_patterns.pattern: balance-info returns all-zero values for free/reserved/nonce
error_patterns.cause: The queried account has no on-chain entry — it has never received any tokens.
error_patterns.resolution: Fund the account at https://faucet.polkadot.io/ and wait 1-2 minutes for confirmation. Re-query the endpoint.

error_patterns.pattern: curl: (6) Could not resolve host
error_patterns.cause: Using the WebSocket URL (wss://) in curl instead of the Sidecar HTTP endpoint.
error_patterns.resolution: Curl queries go to the Sidecar HTTP endpoint: http://127.0.0.1:8080. The wss:// URL is only used to start Sidecar, not to query it.

Start Sidecar in a separate terminal with SAS_SUBSTRATE_URL pointing to wss://asset-hub-paseo.dotters.network
Run curl against http://127.0.0.1:8080/accounts/{address}/balance-info

Fund the address at https://faucet.polkadot.io/ and wait 1-2 minutes for confirmation
Re-query the balance-info endpoint
examples.result: After funding, the free balance reflects the received testnet PAS

skills.title: Estimate Transaction Fees with PAPI
skills.description: Estimates the fee for a Polkadot Hub balance transfer using the PAPI TypeScript library's getPaymentInfo method. Use when you need to preview gas costs before submitting — for fee UIs, budget checks, or preflight validation. Returns partial_fee in planck, human-readable PAS, and weight breakdown. Also covers the Polkadot.js paymentInfo alternative described in the source page. Trigger phrases: 'estimate transaction fee', 'calculate fee Polkadot', 'getPaymentInfo PAPI', 'how much does a transaction cost', 'fee estimation Polkadot Hub'. No private key or testnet tokens required — fee estimation is read-only.

runtime: Node.js v18+ and npm or pnpm
network: WebSocket RPC endpoint for Polkadot Hub TestNet: wss://asset-hub-paseo.dotters.network

commands: npm init -y && npm pkg set type=module
steps.description: Create a new directory 'papi-fee-estimate' and initialize it as an ESM Node.js project. The type=module flag is required — polkadot-api uses ESM imports.
Install polkadot-api (PAPI) and TypeScript tooling.

Generate compile-time type descriptors for Polkadot Hub TestNet. Downloads chain metadata and produces the 'polkadotTestNet' export in @polkadot-api/descriptors. Polkadot Hub TestNet can be unstable — retry after a few minutes if the connection fails.

Execute the script. It constructs an unsigned Balances.transfer_keep_alive transaction for 1 PAS and calls getPaymentInfo to retrieve the fee estimate without submitting. Fees on Polkadot Hub TestNet are higher than Ethereum due to the 1000 gwei base fee.

error_patterns.pattern: Error: Cannot find module '@polkadot-api/descriptors'
error_patterns.cause: PAPI type descriptors were not generated.
error_patterns.resolution: Run 'npx papi add polkadotTestNet -w wss://asset-hub-paseo.dotters.network' in the papi-fee-estimate directory.

error_patterns.pattern: WebSocket connection failed / ECONNREFUSED on papi add or script start
error_patterns.cause: Polkadot Hub TestNet endpoint is temporarily unavailable.
error_patterns.resolution: Wait 2-5 minutes and retry. Check the Polkadot Discord for TestNet status.

error_patterns.pattern: TypeError: tx.getPaymentInfo is not a function
error_patterns.cause: Descriptor export name does not match the import, or polkadot-api version is below 1.0.0.
error_patterns.resolution: Verify the import name matches the name used in 'npx papi add' (polkadotTestNet). Check version: npm list polkadot-api — version 1.0.0+ required.

error_patterns.pattern: SyntaxError: Cannot use import statement in a module
error_patterns.cause: Project is not configured as ESM.
error_patterns.resolution: Run 'npm pkg set type=module' in the papi-fee-estimate directory, then re-run.

supplementary_context.description: Load these pages when the user wants to submit the transaction after fee estimation or needs to understand fee mechanics.

pages.relevance: How to sign and submit the same transaction whose fee was estimated — the natural next step.

pages.relevance: Full PAPI reference including getPaymentInfo signatures and other typed transaction methods.

examples.user_says: How much will it cost to send 1 PAS on Polkadot Hub?

Install polkadot-api and tsx
Generate descriptors with npx papi add polkadotTestNet
Create estimate-fees.ts with inline script; substitute WS endpoint and addresses
Run npx tsx estimate-fees.ts
examples.result: Estimated fee in planck and PAS printed; user can budget before submitting the transfer

examples.scenario: Edge case: fee appears unusually high compared to Ethereum
examples.user_says: The fee estimate shows 0.001 PAS — that seems expensive

Note that PAS testnet fees do not reflect real economic cost
Get testnet tokens from https://faucet.polkadot.io/ if the sender balance is insufficient

skills.title: Transfer Assets Between Parachains with ParaSpell XCM SDK
skills.description: Transfers assets cross-chain between Polkadot parachains using the ParaSpell XCM SDK. Covers the full workflow: install the SDK, build an XCM transfer, dry-run for feasibility, verify existential deposit requirements, estimate fees, and submit against Paseo testnet. Use when you need to move tokens programmatically between parachains (e.g., Asset Hub to another system parachain). Requires a funded Paseo testnet mnemonic. Trigger phrases: 'XCM transfer ParaSpell', 'cross-chain transfer Polkadot', 'transfer assets between parachains', 'send tokens XCM', 'ParaSpell SDK'. Uses dotenv for mnemonic security.

network: WebSocket RPC for Paseo Asset Hub (source): wss://sys.ibp.network/asset-hub-paseo
Destination parachain WebSocket RPC (e.g., wss://sys.ibp.network/people-paseo for People Chain Paseo)
tokens: Paseo testnet PAS in the sender account — get from https://faucet.polkadot.io/ (select Paseo, then Asset Hub Paseo). Minimum 2 PAS recommended.
wallet: SR25519 account mnemonic phrase for the sender account, funded with Paseo testnet PAS

env_vars.name: MNEMONIC
env_vars.description: 12-word BIP39 mnemonic for the sender on Paseo Asset Hub. Must be funded with testnet PAS. Never commit to version control.

env_vars.name: SOURCE_RPC
env_vars.description: WebSocket RPC URL for the source parachain (e.g., wss://sys.ibp.network/asset-hub-paseo).

env_vars.name: DEST_ADDRESS
env_vars.description: SS58-encoded recipient address on the destination parachain.

commands: npm init -y && npm pkg set type=module
steps.description: Create a new directory 'paraspell-xcm-transfer' and initialize it as an ESM Node.js project.
Install the ParaSpell SDK (pjs variant), Polkadot.js API, keyring for signing, and dotenv for secure mnemonic loading.

Create a .env file with three placeholders and a .gitignore to exclude it. Stop here and ask the user to edit .env directly: fill MNEMONIC with their 12-word Paseo testnet mnemonic, confirm SOURCE_RPC, and set DEST_ADDRESS to the recipient SS58 address on the destination chain. Do NOT ask for the mnemonic in chat. Wait for confirmation before continuing.

steps.action: Verify sender is funded and run the script
steps.expected_output:
Sender: ...
Dry-run: OK
Estimated fee: ...
Status: InBlock
Finalized: 0x...
steps.description: Before running, confirm the sender has at least 2 PAS on Paseo Asset Hub. If not, visit https://faucet.polkadot.io/ (select Paseo > Asset Hub Paseo). Execute the script. It dry-runs first — if dry-run fails, check the error before re-attempting. If the TestNet is unstable and the transaction does not finalize within 2 minutes, re-run the script.

error_patterns.pattern: Error: Insufficient funds / Inability to pay some fees
error_patterns.cause: Sender account has insufficient PAS to cover transfer amount plus XCM execution fees.
error_patterns.resolution: Fund the sender on Paseo Asset Hub at https://faucet.polkadot.io/ (select Paseo > Asset Hub Paseo). Wait 2 minutes for confirmation.

error_patterns.pattern: Dry-run failed: destination existential deposit not met
error_patterns.cause: Transfer amount is below the existential deposit on the destination chain, or recipient has no balance.
error_patterns.resolution: Increase the transfer AMOUNT above the destination chain's existential deposit, or ensure the recipient already has a balance on the destination chain.

error_patterns.pattern: Error: chain 'AssetHubPaseo' not found in ParaSpell
error_patterns.cause: Chain name does not match the ParaSpell supported chain list for the SDK version installed.
error_patterns.resolution: Check the supported chains list for your @paraspell/sdk-pjs version. Chain names are case-sensitive and version-specific. Update SOURCE_CHAIN and DEST_CHAIN in the script accordingly.

error_patterns.pattern: Transaction submitted but never finalizes
error_patterns.cause: Paseo TestNet instability — the TestNet can drop transactions under load.
error_patterns.resolution: Check if the transaction was included using subscan.io (paseo.subscan.io). If not included, re-run the script — nonce: -1 fetches the current nonce automatically.

skills.title: Pay Transaction Fees with an Alternative Token
skills.description: Forks Polkadot Hub state locally with Chopsticks and submits a transaction where fees are paid in USDT (asset ID 1984) rather than native PAS, using PAPI's ChargeAssetTxPayment signed extension. Use when demonstrating or testing the asset-conversion fee proxy mechanism without spending real testnet tokens. Uses Alice's dev account via Chopsticks mock-signature mode — no faucet required. Trigger phrases: 'pay fees in USDT', 'alternative fee token Polkadot', 'ChargeAssetTxPayment', 'fee proxy Polkadot Hub', 'Chopsticks fee demo'. Covers PAPI, Polkadot.js, and Subxt alternatives from the source page.

runtime: npx (bundled with npm v5.2+ — no separate install needed)
network: Internet connection to fork chain state from wss://asset-hub-paseo.dotters.network — Chopsticks downloads state on first run

steps.action: Create project directory and install dependencies
commands: npm init -y && npm pkg set type=module
steps.description: Create a new directory 'fee-proxy-demo', initialize as ESM, and install Chopsticks and PAPI. Chopsticks forks Polkadot Hub state locally; PAPI constructs and submits the fee-proxy transaction against the fork.

Create a file named 'chopsticks.yml' with the following content:

endpoint: wss://asset-hub-paseo.dotters.network
db: ./db.sqlite
port: 8000
mock-signature-host: true

The 'mock-signature-host: true' setting allows transactions to be submitted with Alice's well-known dev key without a real private key — this is what makes the demo work without real credentials. The 'db' field caches forked state locally so subsequent runs start faster.

Start the Chopsticks fork in a dedicated terminal. Chopsticks downloads Polkadot Hub state from the TestNet endpoint and starts a local fork at ws://localhost:8000. The first run downloads several MB of state — subsequent runs use db.sqlite. Once 'Listening on port 8000' appears, keep this running and open a second terminal for step 4.

steps.action: Generate PAPI type descriptors for the local fork
steps.description: Run this command in a second terminal while Chopsticks (step 3) is still running. Generates compile-time type descriptors named 'localFork' for the forked chain. Produces the 'localFork' export in @polkadot-api/descriptors. The descriptors reflect the exact runtime version of the forked state.

steps.action: Run the fee-proxy script against the local fork
steps.expected_output:
Included in block: 0x...
Fees paid in USDT asset ID: 1984
steps.description: Execute the script while Chopsticks is still running. The script submits the fee-proxy transaction; Chopsticks validates it against the forked state and includes it in a mock block. If the pallet name 'AssetConversionTxPayment' does not exist in localFork, inspect api.tx in the TypeScript IDE and check the source page for the correct name.

error_patterns.pattern: ECONNREFUSED ws://localhost:8000
error_patterns.cause: Chopsticks is not running.
error_patterns.resolution: Start Chopsticks first: npx @acala-network/chopsticks --config=chopsticks.yml. Wait for 'Listening on port 8000' before running the script or the papi add command.

error_patterns.pattern: Error: Cannot find module '@polkadot-api/descriptors' or localFork is undefined
error_patterns.cause: PAPI type descriptors were not generated against the Chopsticks fork endpoint.
error_patterns.resolution: With Chopsticks running, execute: npx papi add localFork -w ws://localhost:8000. Then re-run the script.

error_patterns.pattern: api.tx.AssetConversionTxPayment is undefined
error_patterns.cause: The forked chain version uses a different pallet name for asset-based fee payment.
error_patterns.resolution: Check available pallets by inspecting api.tx in a TypeScript IDE, or consult the source page for the correct pallet name. The pallet may be named 'AssetTxPayment' depending on the runtime version.

error_patterns.pattern: Alice has insufficient USDT balance to pay fees
error_patterns.cause: Alice's account in the forked state does not hold USDT (asset ID 1984).
error_patterns.resolution: Use Chopsticks' dev_setStorage RPC method to seed Alice's USDT asset balance in the forked state, or identify a USDT-holding account in the forked state and use that address instead.

supplementary_context.description: Load these pages to understand fee mechanics, Chopsticks setup, or to compare with standard fee estimation.

pages.relevance: Full Chopsticks reference including all config options, RPC override methods, and mock-signature-host details.

pages.relevance: How to estimate fees for a standard transaction — compare with the fee proxy approach to understand the difference.

examples.scenario: Common scenario: demonstrate paying fees in USDT on a local fork
examples.user_says: Show me how to pay Polkadot Hub transaction fees in USDT instead of PAS
actions: Create 'fee-proxy-demo/' with ESM Node.js init
Install Chopsticks and polkadot-api
Create chopsticks.yml pointing to wss://asset-hub-paseo.dotters.network with mock-signature-host: true
Start Chopsticks in a dedicated terminal; wait for 'Listening on port 8000'
Generate PAPI descriptors with npx papi add localFork -w ws://localhost:8000
Create fee-proxy.ts with inline script; run npx tsx fee-proxy.ts
examples.result: Transaction included in a local mock block; fees deducted from Alice's USDT balance instead of PAS

examples.scenario: Edge case: Alice has no USDT in the forked state
examples.user_says: The script fails saying Alice has no USDT balance
actions: Use Chopsticks' dev_setStorage RPC to set Alice's USDT asset balance in the forked state
Alternatively, query the forked state for an account with USDT balance and use that address as sender
Re-run fee-proxy.ts
examples.result: Script succeeds once Alice's USDT balance is seeded or a funded account is substituted

skills.title: Run a Parachain RPC Node
skills.description: Runs a Polkadot system parachain RPC node (using People Chain as example) in archive or pruned mode via Docker or systemd. Covers Docker image selection, binary installation, systemd service configuration, and sync verification. A substitution table adapts the steps for Collectives, BridgeHub, and other system parachains. Use when you need a self-hosted parachain RPC endpoint. Trigger phrases: 'run parachain node', 'People Chain RPC node', 'parachain RPC Docker', 'set up system parachain node', 'run parachain RPC systemd'. No tokens required.

runtime: Docker v24+ (for Docker method) OR a 64-bit Linux server with systemd (for binary method)
Disk: minimum 500 GB SSD for pruned node; 2+ TB SSD for archive node
RAM: minimum 8 GB; 16 GB recommended for archive nodes
network: Open TCP ports: 30333 (P2P), 9944 (WebSocket RPC), 9615 (Prometheus metrics, optional)
Stable broadband connection for initial sync (several hundred GB download)

steps.action: Choose deployment method and sync type
steps.description:
Decide on two choices before proceeding: (1) Deployment method: Docker (steps 2) or systemd binary (step 3). (2) Sync type: 'archive' retains full historical state (requires 2+ TB, enables all historical queries) vs 'pruned' retains only recent blocks (500 GB, sufficient for most uses).

Parachain substitution table — replace chain names and image tags accordingly:
- People Chain Polkadot: --chain=people-polkadot, relay --chain=polkadot
- Collectives Polkadot: --chain=collectives-polkadot, relay --chain=polkadot
- BridgeHub Polkadot: --chain=bridge-hub-polkadot, relay --chain=polkadot
- People Chain Paseo: --chain=people-paseo, relay --chain=paseo
All use the parity/polkadot-parachain Docker image.

steps.action: Run the parachain node via Docker
steps.description: Pull the stable polkadot-parachain image and start the People Chain RPC node. Key arguments: --chain=people-polkadot selects the People Chain spec; --base-path=/data stores chain data in the mounted volume; --state-pruning=1000 --blocks-pruning=1000 keeps the last 1000 blocks (change both to 'archive' for a full archive node); --rpc-external exposes the WebSocket RPC; the -- separator and --chain=polkadot configure the embedded relay chain client. Replace /var/lib/people-chain with your data directory. For Paseo testnet People Chain, use --chain=people-paseo and --chain=paseo after the -- separator.

steps.action: Run the parachain node via systemd (binary method alternative)
steps.description:
For the systemd binary method (skip if using Docker in step 2): (1) Create system user: sudo useradd --system --no-create-home --shell /usr/sbin/nologin polkadot. (2) Create data dir: sudo mkdir -p /var/lib/people-chain && sudo chown polkadot:polkadot /var/lib/people-chain. (3) Download polkadot-parachain binary from https://github.com/paritytech/polkadot-sdk/releases (stable, amd64 or arm64) and install: sudo install -m 755 polkadot-parachain /usr/local/bin/. (4) Create /etc/systemd/system/people-chain-rpc.service:

[Unit]
Description=People Chain RPC Node
After=network.target

[Service]
User=polkadot
ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/polkadot-parachain --chain=people-polkadot --base-path=/var/lib/people-chain --rpc-external --rpc-port=9944 --rpc-cors=all --state-pruning=1000 --blocks-pruning=1000 -- --chain=polkadot --sync=fast
Restart=always
RestartSec=10

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target

(5) Enable and start: sudo systemctl daemon-reload && sudo systemctl enable people-chain-rpc && sudo systemctl start people-chain-rpc. Apply the same chain substitutions as in step 2.

Monitor sync using Docker logs (first command) or the JSON-RPC sync state query (second). For systemd, replace 'docker logs -f people-chain-rpc' with 'sudo journalctl -fu people-chain-rpc'. The node is fully synced when currentBlock equals highestBlock. Fast sync downloads state snapshots and is significantly faster than full sync — initial sync can take hours to days depending on hardware.

steps.action: Verify the RPC endpoint is operational
steps.description: Confirm the node serves RPC requests. system_chain should return 'Polkadot People' for the mainnet People Chain. system_health should show isSyncing: false and peers greater than 0. If peers is 0, verify port 30333 is open in your firewall. If isSyncing is true, wait for sync to complete before pointing applications at this endpoint.

error_patterns.pattern: Error: No space left on device / database write error
error_patterns.cause: The data volume has run out of disk space.
error_patterns.resolution: Increase the volume size, or switch from archive to pruned mode (--state-pruning=1000 --blocks-pruning=1000). Check usage with: du -sh /var/lib/people-chain

error_patterns.pattern: EADDRINUSE port 9944 or 30333
error_patterns.cause: Another process is already using the required ports.
error_patterns.resolution: Change the port with --rpc-port=NEW_PORT for RPC and --port=NEW_P2P_PORT for P2P. Update firewall rules accordingly.

error_patterns.pattern: Peers count stays at 0 after several minutes
error_patterns.cause: P2P port 30333 is blocked by firewall, or bootnodes are temporarily unavailable.
error_patterns.resolution: Open TCP port 30333: sudo ufw allow 30333/tcp. For cloud VMs, update the security group to allow inbound TCP on 30333. If peers still do not connect after 10 minutes, check the Polkadot network status page.

error_patterns.pattern: Sync stalls at the same block for more than 30 minutes
error_patterns.cause: Node cannot find peers with required state, or the embedded relay chain client is not syncing.
error_patterns.resolution: Restart the container or service: docker restart people-chain-rpc or sudo systemctl restart people-chain-rpc. If stalling persists, try switching sync mode: replace --sync=fast with --sync=warp.

Run docker with --chain=people-polkadot, --state-pruning=1000, --blocks-pruning=1000, ports 30333 and 9944 exposed

Verify peers > 0 via system_health and that port 30333 is open
Restart: docker restart people-chain-rpc
If stalling persists after restart, switch to --sync=warp

skills.title: Replay and Dry-Run XCMs Using Chopsticks
skills.description: Forks a Polkadot chain locally with Chopsticks to replay and dry-run historical XCM messages using their on-chain call data. Covers building a Wasm binary, starting a Chopsticks fork, fetching encoded call data from Subscan, and invoking the xcmPallet.execute dry-run RPC method. Use when debugging a failing XCM, previewing execution outcomes, or verifying fee coverage before submitting to a live network. Requires Rust and the Polkadot SDK build environment. Manual step: locating the call data hex on Subscan. Trigger phrases: 'replay XCM', 'dry-run XCM Chopsticks', 'debug XCM Polkadot', 'preview XCM execution', 'XCM dry run local fork'.

runtime: Rust toolchain with wasm32-unknown-unknown target (from install-polkadot-sdk skill or rustup)
network: Internet connection to fetch chain state for Chopsticks fork
Subscan access to locate the historical XCM call data hex

steps.action: Create project directory and install Chopsticks
steps.description: Create a working directory 'xcm-chopsticks-debug' and install Chopsticks. Chopsticks will fork the chain state locally to allow replaying the XCM without interacting with the live network.

steps.action: Create Chopsticks configuration for the source chain
steps.description:
Create a file named 'chopsticks.yml' with the following content:

endpoint: INSERT_CHAIN_WS_ENDPOINT
db: ./db.sqlite
port: 8000
mock-signature-host: true

Replace INSERT_CHAIN_WS_ENDPOINT with the WebSocket endpoint of the chain where the XCM originated (e.g., wss://asset-hub-paseo.dotters.network for Polkadot Hub TestNet or wss://rpc.polkadot.io for Polkadot relay chain). The 'mock-signature-host: true' allows submitting calls without real keys. The 'db' field caches state so subsequent runs start faster.

Start Chopsticks in a dedicated terminal. It downloads chain state from the configured endpoint and starts a local fork at ws://localhost:8000. The first run downloads several MB of state — subsequent runs use db.sqlite cache. Keep this terminal open during all following steps.

steps.action: Locate the XCM call data on Subscan
steps.description: This step is manual. Open Subscan for the source chain (e.g., https://polkadot.subscan.io or https://assethub-polkadot.subscan.io). Navigate to the extrinsic or event containing the XCM you want to replay. Find the encoded call data hex string — it typically appears in the 'Call Data' or 'Params' section of the extrinsic detail page. Copy the full hex string (starts with '0x'). You will use this in step 5. Paste it into a local file named 'call-data.txt' for reference.

steps.action: Replay the XCM call against the local fork
steps.description: Replace INSERT_SENDER_ADDRESS with the SS58 address that submitted the original XCM and INSERT_CALL_DATA_HEX with the hex string from Subscan (step 4). The response's executionResult indicates success or failure along with the failure reason if any. If the RPC method is not available on the forked chain, use 'xcmPallet_dryRunXcm' as an alternative — check the source page for the correct method name and parameter signature for your chain version. Chopsticks must be running (step 3) for this to work.

error_patterns.pattern: ECONNREFUSED ws://localhost:8000
error_patterns.cause: Chopsticks is not running or not yet ready.
error_patterns.resolution: Start Chopsticks first in a dedicated terminal: npx @acala-network/chopsticks --config=chopsticks.yml. Wait for 'Listening on port 8000' before proceeding.

error_patterns.pattern: xcmPallet_dryRunCall method not found / Method not implemented
error_patterns.cause: The forked chain runtime does not expose this specific dry-run RPC method, or uses a different method name.
error_patterns.resolution: Check the source page for the correct RPC method name for your chain version. Try 'xcmPallet_dryRunXcm' or 'dryRun' alternatives. The method availability depends on the chain runtime version.

error_patterns.pattern: executionResult: Err with 'BuyExecution failed' or 'TooExpensive'
error_patterns.cause: The XCM message includes insufficient fees to cover execution on the destination chain.
error_patterns.resolution: Increase the fee amount in the BuyExecution instruction of the XCM message before re-submitting. Check the estimate-xcm-fees-teleport skill for how to calculate required fees.

error_patterns.pattern: Chopsticks fork diverges from expected state / 'Block not found'
error_patterns.cause: The forked block is too old and the cached state (db.sqlite) is stale or missing.
error_patterns.resolution: Delete db.sqlite and restart Chopsticks to re-fork from the current chain head. For replaying a specific historical block, add 'block: BLOCK_NUMBER_OR_HASH' to chopsticks.yml.

examples.scenario: Common scenario: debug a failing XCM transfer by replaying it locally
examples.user_says: An XCM transfer I submitted failed. How can I debug it without spending more tokens?
actions: Create 'xcm-chopsticks-debug/' and install Chopsticks
Create chopsticks.yml pointing to the source chain endpoint with mock-signature-host: true
Start Chopsticks in a dedicated terminal; wait for 'Listening on port 8000'
Find the failed extrinsic on Subscan and copy the encoded call data hex
Call xcmPallet_dryRunCall via curl against localhost:8000 with the sender address and call data hex
Inspect executionResult for the failure reason and adjust the XCM message accordingly
examples.result: Dry-run reveals the failure reason (e.g., TooExpensive, Barrier blocked) without spending testnet tokens

examples.scenario: Edge case: the dry-run method is not available on the forked chain
examples.user_says: The curl call returns 'method not found' for xcmPallet_dryRunCall
actions: Check the source page for alternative RPC method names for the chain version
Try 'xcmPallet_dryRunXcm' or consult the chain's RPC documentation
If no dry-run RPC is available, use Chopsticks dev_newBlock to mine a block with the call and inspect the emitted events
examples.result: XCM replay succeeds using the correct RPC method for the chain runtime version

skills.title: Estimate XCM Fees for Asset Teleport
skills.description: Estimates all three fee components for an XCM teleport: local execution fee (source chain), delivery fee (network transport), and remote execution fee (destination chain). Uses Chopsticks to fork both Polkadot Hub TestNet and People Chain locally, then calls PAPI fee estimation methods against the forks. Use when you need to budget XCM fees before submitting a teleport, or when a BuyExecution instruction is failing due to insufficient fees. No real tokens required — Chopsticks mock-signature mode is used. Trigger phrases: 'estimate XCM fees', 'XCM teleport fee', 'BuyExecution fee calculation', 'cross-chain fee estimate Polkadot'.

network: Internet connection to fork chain state from Polkadot Hub TestNet: wss://asset-hub-paseo.dotters.network
Internet connection to fork People Chain TestNet state

steps.action: Create project directory and install dependencies
commands: npm init -y && npm pkg set type=module
steps.description: Create 'xcm-fee-estimate' as an ESM Node.js project and install Chopsticks (for local chain forks) and polkadot-api (PAPI, for fee estimation calls).

steps.action: Create Chopsticks configs for source and destination chains
steps.description:
Create two Chopsticks config files:

File 1 — 'chopsticks-hub.yml':
endpoint: wss://asset-hub-paseo.dotters.network
db: ./db-hub.sqlite
port: 8000
mock-signature-host: true

File 2 — 'chopsticks-people.yml':
endpoint: INSERT_PEOPLE_CHAIN_TESTNET_WS
db: ./db-people.sqlite
port: 8001
mock-signature-host: true

Replace INSERT_PEOPLE_CHAIN_TESTNET_WS with the People Chain TestNet WebSocket endpoint. The two forks run on different ports (8000 and 8001) so they can be active simultaneously.

steps.expected_output:
Listening on port 8000
Listening on port 8001
steps.description: Start each Chopsticks instance in a separate terminal. The first forks Polkadot Hub TestNet on port 8000; the second forks People Chain on port 8001. Both must be running before proceeding to the next step. The first run of each downloads several MB of state and caches it to the db files.

steps.action: Generate PAPI type descriptors for both forks
steps.description: Run these commands in a third terminal while both Chopsticks forks (step 3) are running. Generates compile-time PAPI descriptors named 'hubFork' and 'peopleFork'. Both descriptors must be generated before writing the estimation script.

steps.expected_output:
Local execution fee (planck): 1500000000000000
Delivery fee assets: [...]
Remote execution fee (planck): 500000000000000
steps.description: Execute the script while both Chopsticks forks are running. The output shows all three fee components in planck. Divide by 10^18 to convert to PAS. The sum of all three components is the total fee budget to include in the XCM BuyExecution instruction. If any XcmPaymentApi method is not available, check the source page for the correct API method names for your chain runtime version.

error_patterns.pattern: ECONNREFUSED ws://localhost:8000 or ws://localhost:8001
error_patterns.cause: One or both Chopsticks forks are not running.
error_patterns.resolution: Start each Chopsticks instance in a separate terminal. Run 'npx @acala-network/chopsticks --config=chopsticks-hub.yml' and 'npx @acala-network/chopsticks --config=chopsticks-people.yml'. Wait for 'Listening on port 8000/8001' before proceeding.

error_patterns.pattern: XcmPaymentApi method not found / query_delivery_fees undefined
error_patterns.cause: The chain runtime version does not implement the XcmPaymentApi runtime API at this method name.
error_patterns.resolution: Check the source page for the correct API method names for your runtime version. The API surface changed between XCM V3 and V4. Verify the chain runtime version in the Chopsticks fork logs.

error_patterns.pattern: Error: Cannot find module '@polkadot-api/descriptors' or hubFork/peopleFork undefined
error_patterns.cause: PAPI descriptors were not generated for one or both forks.
error_patterns.resolution: With both Chopsticks forks running, execute: npx papi add hubFork -w ws://localhost:8000 and npx papi add peopleFork -w ws://localhost:8001. Then re-run the script.

error_patterns.pattern: Fee estimate returns 0 or undefined for remote execution fee
error_patterns.cause: The XCM message structure passed to query_xcm_weight does not match the expected format for the chain runtime.
error_patterns.resolution: Inspect the source page for the exact XCM instruction sequence expected by the destination chain. Verify that the MultiLocation structures use the correct XCM version (V3 vs V4) matching the chain runtime.
