examples.scenario: Common scenario: calculate the total fee budget needed for a PAS teleport to People Chain
examples.user_says: How much PAS do I need to include in the BuyExecution for a teleport from Polkadot Hub to People Chain?
actions: Create 'xcm-fee-estimate/' as ESM Node.js project and install Chopsticks and polkadot-api
Create chopsticks-hub.yml (port 8000) and chopsticks-people.yml (port 8001) with mock-signature-host: true
Start both Chopsticks forks in separate terminals
Generate PAPI descriptors: npx papi add hubFork and npx papi add peopleFork
Create estimate-xcm-fees.ts with the inline script; substitute sender and recipient addresses
Run npx tsx estimate-xcm-fees.ts and sum the three fee components
examples.result: All three fee components printed in planck; sum used as BuyExecution fee budget for the teleport

examples.scenario: Edge case: remote execution fee query fails with XcmPaymentApi not found
examples.user_says: The script errors with 'query_xcm_weight is not a function' on the People Chain fork
actions: Check the People Chain runtime version in Chopsticks logs
Consult the source page for the XcmPaymentApi method names for that runtime version
Update the API call in estimate-xcm-fees.ts to match the correct method signature
examples.result: Remote execution fee estimated using the correct XcmPaymentApi method for the chain runtime

skills.title: Set Up the Polkadot SDK Parachain Template
skills.description: Clones the Polkadot SDK Parachain Template, compiles it in release mode, and runs a local development node to verify the build and environment. Use as the first step in the parachain launch workflow before registering on Paseo testnet or a local relay chain. Requires the Polkadot SDK build environment from the install-polkadot-sdk skill. The template provides a minimal parachain runtime with Aura consensus, FRAME pallets, and a pre-configured Cumulus integration. Trigger phrases: 'parachain template', 'set up parachain Polkadot SDK', 'clone parachain template', 'build parachain template', 'Polkadot SDK parachain starter'. First step in the three-page parachain launch series.

runtime: Rust toolchain with wasm32-unknown-unknown target — complete the install-polkadot-sdk skill first
Disk: at least 5 GB free for the build artifacts
RAM: 8 GB minimum; 16 GB recommended for parallel compilation

steps.action: Clone the Polkadot SDK Parachain Template
steps.description: Clone the official Polkadot SDK Parachain Template repository. The template contains a minimal parachain runtime with Aura consensus, standard FRAME pallets, and Cumulus integration for relay chain connectivity. It includes both a node binary and a runtime Wasm blob.

Familiarize yourself with the key directories before building: 'node/' contains the node binary crate (CLI, RPC, service); 'runtime/' contains the parachain runtime (pallets, weights, Wasm); 'pallets/' contains a sample custom pallet. The 'Cargo.toml' at the root defines the workspace. No files need to be created or modified for the initial setup — the default configuration targets a local development network.

steps.action: Build the parachain node in release mode
steps.expected_output:
Compiling parachain-template-node v0.1.0
...
Finished release [optimized] target(s)
steps.description: Compile the parachain node and runtime in release mode. This step is time-consuming (20-60 minutes on first build) because it compiles the entire Polkadot SDK dependency tree. Subsequent builds use the Rust incremental cache and are significantly faster. If compilation fails with 'linker not found', install the build-essential package (Linux) or Xcode command line tools (macOS). On low-memory machines (less than 8 GB RAM), reduce parallel jobs: CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=2 cargo build --release.

Confirm the binary exists and reports its version. If the command fails with 'No such file or directory', the build did not complete successfully — review the cargo build output for compilation errors.

steps.action: Run the node in local development mode
steps.description: Start the node in development mode. In --dev mode the node runs as a single collator without a relay chain connection, using a local chain spec and instant block production. This verifies the binary works correctly. The node will not produce blocks in this mode without a connected relay chain — the 'Idle' output is expected. Press Ctrl+C to stop. The next step in the parachain launch series covers connecting to a relay chain.

error_patterns.pattern: error[E0463]: can't find crate for 'std' / wasm32 target missing
error_patterns.cause: The wasm32-unknown-unknown target is not installed in the Rust toolchain.
error_patterns.resolution: Run: rustup target add wasm32-unknown-unknown. If using a specific toolchain version, prefix with rustup target add wasm32-unknown-unknown --toolchain stable.

error_patterns.pattern: error: linking with 'cc' failed / linker not found
error_patterns.cause: C/C++ build tools are not installed on the system.
error_patterns.resolution: On Ubuntu/Debian: sudo apt install build-essential clang. On macOS: xcode-select --install. Then re-run cargo build --release.

error_patterns.pattern: Killed / process killed during compilation / OOM
error_patterns.cause: Insufficient RAM for parallel Rust compilation — cargo uses one thread per CPU core by default.
error_patterns.resolution: Reduce parallel compilation jobs: CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=2 cargo build --release. Consider adding swap space on Linux: sudo fallocate -l 4G /swapfile && sudo mkswap /swapfile && sudo swapon /swapfile.

error_patterns.pattern: error: package 'polkadot-sdk' not found / version mismatch in Cargo.lock
error_patterns.cause: Cargo.lock is out of sync with the current Polkadot SDK release pinned in Cargo.toml.
error_patterns.resolution: Run cargo update to refresh the lock file to the latest compatible versions, then retry cargo build --release.

pages.relevance: Prerequisite: install Rust, system dependencies, and the Polkadot SDK build environment before building the template.

examples.scenario: Common scenario: first-time parachain developer setting up the template
examples.user_says: How do I get the Polkadot SDK Parachain Template running on my machine?
actions: Confirm Rust toolchain with wasm32 target is installed (install-polkadot-sdk skill if not)
Clone polkadot-sdk-parachain-template from GitHub
Run cargo build --release in the cloned directory (allow 20-60 minutes)
Verify with ./target/release/parachain-template-node --version
Start in dev mode with ./target/release/parachain-template-node --dev to confirm it works
examples.result: Template node built and running in dev mode; environment confirmed ready for the next parachain launch step

examples.scenario: Edge case: build is killed due to out-of-memory on a low-spec machine
examples.user_says: The cargo build process gets killed partway through on my 4 GB RAM machine
actions: Set CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=2 to limit parallel compilation threads
Add at least 4 GB of swap space: sudo fallocate -l 4G /swapfile && sudo mkswap /swapfile && sudo swapon /swapfile
Re-run CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=2 cargo build --release
examples.result: Build completes successfully with reduced memory pressure from limited parallel jobs

skills.title: Run a Block-Producing Collator for a System Parachain
skills.description: Configures a full collator node for a Polkadot system parachain (e.g., Collectives, BridgeHub) via Docker or a systemd binary. Covers binary installation, node key generation, chain spec retrieval, collator startup, session key generation via RPC, and on-chain session key registration. Governance approval is required for system parachain collator registration. Use when you need to run a block-producing node for a system parachain. No testnet tokens required for setup; on-chain registration requires native token for extrinsic fees. Trigger phrases: 'run collator', 'system parachain collator', 'set up collator Polkadot', 'collator node setup', 'register collator session keys'.

runtime: Docker v24+ (for Docker method) OR a 64-bit Linux server with systemd (for binary method)
Disk: minimum 500 GB SSD; 2+ TB for archive collator
network: Open TCP ports: 30333 (parachain P2P), 30334 (relay chain P2P), 9944 (RPC, restrict to localhost for collators)
Stable broadband connection for initial sync
tokens: Small amount of native parachain token for submitting the session key registration extrinsic

steps.action: Choose deployment method and prepare the server
steps.description:
Decide on the deployment method: Docker (step 2) or systemd binary (step 3). A collator must maintain high uptime — choose a dedicated server or cloud VM. The collator's RPC port (9944) should NOT be exposed publicly; restrict it to localhost. The P2P ports (30333 for the parachain, 30334 for the embedded relay chain client) must be open for peer discovery.

Parachain substitution table:
- Collectives Polkadot: --chain=collectives-polkadot, relay --chain=polkadot
- BridgeHub Polkadot: --chain=bridge-hub-polkadot, relay --chain=polkadot
- People Chain Polkadot: --chain=people-polkadot, relay --chain=polkadot
- Paseo testnet equivalents: append -paseo to the chain names and use --chain=paseo for relay

Pull the stable polkadot-parachain image and start the collator. Key flags: --collator enables block production mode; the chain flag selects the parachain spec; the -- separator and --chain=polkadot configure the embedded relay chain client. RPC port 9944 is NOT mapped to the host — collators should not expose RPC publicly. Replace /var/lib/collator-data with your data directory and adjust chain names per the substitution table.

steps.action: Install and run the collator via systemd (binary method alternative)
steps.description:
For the systemd binary method (skip if using Docker in step 2): (1) Create system user: sudo useradd --system --no-create-home --shell /usr/sbin/nologin polkadot. (2) Create data dir: sudo mkdir -p /var/lib/collator-data && sudo chown polkadot:polkadot /var/lib/collator-data. (3) Download polkadot-parachain binary from https://github.com/paritytech/polkadot-sdk/releases (stable, amd64 or arm64) and install: sudo install -m 755 polkadot-parachain /usr/local/bin/. (4) Create /etc/systemd/system/parachain-collator.service:

[Unit]
Description=Parachain Collator
After=network.target

[Service]
User=polkadot
ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/polkadot-parachain --collator --chain=collectives-polkadot --base-path=/var/lib/collator-data --rpc-port=9944 --state-pruning=1000 --blocks-pruning=1000 -- --chain=polkadot --sync=fast
Restart=always
RestartSec=10

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target

(5) Enable and start: sudo systemctl daemon-reload && sudo systemctl enable parachain-collator && sudo systemctl start parachain-collator.

steps.action: Generate a node key for the collator
commands: docker exec parachain-collator polkadot-parachain key generate-node-key --file /data/node-key
steps.description: Generate a persistent libp2p node key for the collator. For systemd, run: polkadot-parachain key generate-node-key --file /var/lib/collator-data/node-key. The node key is the collator's peer identity on the P2P network. Keep this key backed up — losing it changes the node's peer ID. Add --node-key-file=/data/node-key to the container startup command and restart the collator.

steps.action: Monitor sync and wait for the node to be fully synced
steps.description: Monitor sync progress via Docker logs or the system_syncState RPC call. For systemd, use: sudo journalctl -fu parachain-collator. The node must be fully synced (currentBlock equals highestBlock) before generating session keys. Initial sync can take hours to days depending on hardware and network speed.

Call author_rotateKeys to generate and store new session keys in the collator keystore. The result is a hex-encoded concatenation of all session keys (Aura key for parachain collators). Copy the full hex string from 'result' — you will use it in step 7 to register on-chain. This RPC call must be made against the local node (localhost:9944), not a public endpoint.

This step requires a governance action for system parachains. Using Polkadot.js Apps (https://polkadot.js.org/apps): (1) Connect to the parachain's RPC endpoint. (2) Navigate to Developer > Extrinsics. (3) Select 'session' pallet and 'setKeys' extrinsic. (4) Paste the hex session key from step 6 into the 'keys' field. (5) Set 'proof' to '0x'. (6) Sign and submit using your collator account. For system parachains, collator set changes require an on-chain governance proposal — a simple setKeys call is not sufficient to immediately start collating. Follow the parachain's governance process to have your collator added to the invulnerable set or active collator list. Confirm the key is registered by querying the 'session.nextKeys' storage on-chain.

error_patterns.pattern: No space left on device / database write error
error_patterns.cause: The data volume has run out of disk space during sync.
error_patterns.resolution: Increase the volume size, or switch from archive to pruned mode by adding --state-pruning=1000 --blocks-pruning=1000. Check disk usage: du -sh /var/lib/collator-data.

error_patterns.pattern: Peers count stays at 0 after several minutes
error_patterns.cause: P2P ports 30333 or 30334 are blocked by a firewall or security group.
error_patterns.resolution: Open TCP ports 30333 and 30334: sudo ufw allow 30333/tcp && sudo ufw allow 30334/tcp. For cloud VMs, update security group rules. Check peer count via system_health RPC.

error_patterns.pattern: author_rotateKeys returns empty result or connection refused
error_patterns.cause: The collator RPC port 9944 is not accessible from the host, or the node is not yet fully synced.
error_patterns.resolution: Verify the collator is synced (step 5). For Docker, confirm port 9944 is mapped: docker inspect parachain-collator. If not mapped, add -p 127.0.0.1:9944:9944 to the docker run command and restart.

error_patterns.pattern: Collator node syncs but does not produce blocks after session key registration
error_patterns.cause: System parachain collator registration requires governance approval — simple setKeys does not add a collator to the active set.
error_patterns.resolution: Submit a governance proposal to add the collator to the invulnerables list via the parachainSystem or collatorSelection pallet. Follow the target parachain's governance process on Polkassembly or Subsquare.

examples.scenario: Common scenario: set up a Collectives parachain collator using Docker
examples.user_says: How do I run a collator node for the Collectives parachain on Polkadot?
actions: Pull parity/polkadot-parachain:stable
Create data directory /var/lib/collator-data
Start collator with --collator --chain=collectives-polkadot and embedded --chain=polkadot relay
Generate node key with polkadot-parachain key generate-node-key and restart with --node-key-file
Wait for full sync; monitor with docker logs and system_syncState RPC
Generate session keys with author_rotateKeys and submit governance proposal to register
examples.result: Collator node synced and running; session keys registered pending governance approval for block production

examples.scenario: Edge case: collator produces no blocks after governance approval of session keys
examples.user_says: My collator session keys were approved by governance but the node still is not producing blocks
actions: Verify the session keys registered on-chain match the output of author_rotateKeys: query session.nextKeys storage
Check that the node has peers and is fully synced via system_health and system_syncState
Confirm the collator is in the active invulnerables list by querying collatorSelection.invulnerables storage
Wait for the next session rotation — key changes take effect at the start of the next session
examples.result: Collator begins producing blocks at the next session rotation once all keys and peer connections are confirmed

skills.title: Run an RPC Node for Polkadot Hub
skills.description: Sets up a Polkadot Hub (Asset Hub) RPC node in archive or pruned mode via Docker or systemd, with an optional Ethereum-compatible JSON-RPC adapter (eth-rpc) for EVM tooling. Covers Docker image selection, binary installation, snapshot download for faster sync, systemd service configuration, and sync verification. Use when you need a self-hosted Polkadot Hub RPC endpoint for Substrate or Ethereum-compatible tools. Trigger phrases: 'run Polkadot Hub node', 'Asset Hub RPC node', 'Polkadot Hub Docker node', 'eth-rpc Polkadot Hub', 'Polkadot Hub archive node'. No tokens required.

runtime: Docker v24+ (for Docker method) OR a 64-bit Linux server with systemd (for binary method)
Disk: minimum 500 GB SSD for pruned node; 2+ TB SSD for archive node
RAM: minimum 8 GB; 16 GB recommended for archive nodes
network: Open TCP ports: 30333 (P2P), 9944 (WebSocket RPC), 9615 (Prometheus metrics, optional), 8545 (Ethereum RPC if using eth-rpc adapter)
Stable broadband connection for initial sync (several hundred GB download)

steps.action: Choose deployment method and sync type
steps.description:
Decide on two choices before proceeding: (1) Deployment method: Docker (step 2) or systemd binary (step 3). (2) Sync type: 'archive' retains full historical state (2+ TB, enables all historical queries) vs 'pruned' retains only recent blocks (500 GB, sufficient for most uses).

Polkadot Hub uses the parity/polkadot-parachain Docker image with --chain=asset-hub-polkadot. For the Paseo TestNet (chain ID: 420420417), use --chain=asset-hub-paseo and relay --chain=paseo. Optionally download a database snapshot from the Polkadot ecosystem snapshot providers to accelerate initial sync — check https://snapshots.polkadot.io or community providers for current snapshots.

steps.action: Run the Polkadot Hub node via Docker
steps.description: Pull the stable polkadot-parachain image and start the Polkadot Hub RPC node. Key arguments: --chain=asset-hub-polkadot selects the Polkadot Hub spec; --base-path=/data stores chain data in the mounted volume; --state-pruning=1000 --blocks-pruning=1000 keeps the last 1000 blocks (change both to 'archive' for a full archive node); --rpc-external exposes the WebSocket RPC on 9944; the -- separator and --chain=polkadot configure the embedded relay chain client. Replace /var/lib/polkadot-hub with your data directory. For Paseo TestNet, use --chain=asset-hub-paseo and --chain=paseo after the -- separator.

steps.action: Run the Polkadot Hub node via systemd (binary method alternative)
steps.description:
For the systemd binary method (skip if using Docker in step 2): (1) Create system user: sudo useradd --system --no-create-home --shell /usr/sbin/nologin polkadot. (2) Create data dir: sudo mkdir -p /var/lib/polkadot-hub && sudo chown polkadot:polkadot /var/lib/polkadot-hub. (3) Download polkadot-parachain binary from https://github.com/paritytech/polkadot-sdk/releases (stable, amd64 or arm64) and install: sudo install -m 755 polkadot-parachain /usr/local/bin/. (4) Create /etc/systemd/system/polkadot-hub-rpc.service:

[Unit]
Description=Polkadot Hub RPC Node
After=network.target

[Service]
User=polkadot
ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/polkadot-parachain --chain=asset-hub-polkadot --base-path=/var/lib/polkadot-hub --rpc-external --rpc-port=9944 --rpc-cors=all --state-pruning=1000 --blocks-pruning=1000 -- --chain=polkadot --sync=fast
Restart=always
RestartSec=10

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target

(5) Enable and start: sudo systemctl daemon-reload && sudo systemctl enable polkadot-hub-rpc && sudo systemctl start polkadot-hub-rpc. For archive mode, omit --state-pruning and --blocks-pruning flags.

steps.action: Optionally install the Ethereum RPC adapter (eth-rpc)
commands: docker run -d --name polkadot-hub-eth-rpc --restart unless-stopped -p 8545:8545 --network host paritytech/frontier-node:latest --chain=asset-hub-polkadot --endpoint=ws://localhost:9944 --listen-addr=0.0.0.0:8545
steps.description: This step is optional. The Ethereum RPC adapter (eth-rpc / Frontier) exposes an Ethereum-compatible JSON-RPC endpoint on port 8545, allowing Ethereum tooling (MetaMask, ethers.js, Hardhat) to connect to Polkadot Hub. The adapter proxies Ethereum RPC calls to the Substrate node. Run it after the main node (step 2 or 3) is fully synced and serving on ws://localhost:9944. For Paseo TestNet (chain ID: 420420417), use --chain=asset-hub-paseo. Skip this step if you only need the native Substrate WebSocket RPC endpoint.

Monitor sync using Docker logs (first command) or the JSON-RPC sync state query (second). For systemd, replace 'docker logs -f polkadot-hub-rpc' with 'sudo journalctl -fu polkadot-hub-rpc'. The node is fully synced when currentBlock equals highestBlock. Fast sync downloads state snapshots and is significantly faster than full sync — initial sync can take hours to days depending on hardware.

steps.action: Verify the RPC endpoint is operational
steps.description: Confirm the node serves RPC requests. system_chain should return 'Polkadot Asset Hub'. system_health should show isSyncing: false and peers greater than 0. If peers is 0, verify port 30333 is open in your firewall. If isSyncing is true, wait for sync to complete. If the eth-rpc adapter is running, also verify it: curl -s -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"eth_chainId","params":[]}' http://localhost:8545 — should return '0x18FF5B01' (420420417 in hex for Paseo TestNet).

error_patterns.pattern: Error: No space left on device / database write error
error_patterns.cause: The data volume has run out of disk space.
error_patterns.resolution: Increase the volume size, or switch from archive to pruned mode (--state-pruning=1000 --blocks-pruning=1000). Check usage with: du -sh /var/lib/polkadot-hub.

error_patterns.pattern: EADDRINUSE port 9944 or 30333
error_patterns.cause: Another process is already using the required ports.
error_patterns.resolution: Change the port with --rpc-port=NEW_PORT for RPC and --port=NEW_P2P_PORT for P2P. Update firewall rules accordingly.

error_patterns.pattern: Peers count stays at 0 after several minutes
error_patterns.cause: P2P port 30333 is blocked by firewall or the bootnodes are temporarily unavailable.
error_patterns.resolution: Open TCP port 30333: sudo ufw allow 30333/tcp. For cloud VMs, update the security group. If peers still do not connect after 10 minutes, check the Polkadot network status.

error_patterns.pattern: eth_chainId returns unexpected value or eth-rpc adapter cannot connect to node
error_patterns.cause: The eth-rpc adapter endpoint does not match the running node's WebSocket address, or the node is not yet fully synced.
error_patterns.resolution: Verify the node is fully synced (step 5). Ensure the --endpoint flag for the eth-rpc adapter points to ws://localhost:9944 and the node's --rpc-external flag is active. Restart the eth-rpc adapter after the node is synced.

examples.scenario: Common scenario: run a pruned Polkadot Hub RPC node via Docker
examples.user_says: Run a Polkadot Hub RPC node using Docker
actions: Pull parity/polkadot-parachain:stable
Create data directory /var/lib/polkadot-hub
Run docker with --chain=asset-hub-polkadot, --state-pruning=1000, --blocks-pruning=1000, ports 30333 and 9944 exposed
Monitor sync with docker logs and system_syncState RPC
Verify with system_chain and system_health once currentBlock equals highestBlock
examples.result: Polkadot Hub RPC node running, synced, serving requests on ws://localhost:9944

examples.scenario: Edge case: Ethereum tooling cannot connect — eth_chainId returns wrong value
examples.user_says: MetaMask connects to port 8545 but shows the wrong chain ID
actions: Verify the eth-rpc adapter is configured with the correct --chain flag matching the running node
For Paseo TestNet, chain ID is 420420417 (0x18FF5B01 in hex) — confirm eth_chainId returns this value
If chain ID is wrong, restart the eth-rpc adapter with --chain=asset-hub-paseo for TestNet or --chain=asset-hub-polkadot for mainnet
Update MetaMask network settings with the correct chain ID and RPC URL http://localhost:8545
examples.result: MetaMask connects successfully with the correct Polkadot Hub chain ID and eth-rpc adapter

skills.title: Set Up a Relay Chain Bootnode
skills.description: Configures a Polkadot relay chain bootnode exposing P2P, WebSocket, and secure WSS endpoints for peer discovery. Covers node key generation, polkadot startup flags, nginx SSL reverse-proxy configuration for WSS, and three-protocol connection testing. Use when you need to run a stable, publicly accessible bootnode for a Polkadot relay chain. Trigger phrases: 'set up a bootnode', 'run Polkadot bootnode', 'configure bootnode nginx', 'P2P WSS bootnode'. Prerequisites: polkadot binary in PATH and nginx installed. Output: bootnode reachable on P2P (30310), WS (30311), and WSS (30312).

runtime: Linux VPS or dedicated server with a public IP and DNS name
polkadot binary installed and available in $PATH
network: Open TCP ports: 30310 (P2P), 30311 (P2P/WS), 30312 (P2P/WSS)
A valid SSL certificate and DNS name for the WSS endpoint (e.g., from Let's Encrypt)

steps.expected_output: <64-character hex public key printed to stdout>
steps.description: Generate a persistent ED25519 node key for the bootnode. The command prints the public key (peer ID) to stdout and stores the secret key at chains/<CHAIN>/network/secret_ed25519 under the --base-path directory. Record the public key — it is used in the bootnode multiaddress (e.g., /dns/your-host/tcp/30310/p2p/<PUBLIC_KEY>). Back up the secret key file immediately; if lost, the peer ID changes and chain specs referencing the old ID must be updated. Save the public key output — it is needed in step 4.

steps.action: Start the bootnode with P2P and WS listeners
steps.description: Start the Polkadot bootnode. Replace 'polkadot' in --chain with your target chain name if running a different relay chain. --listen-addr /ip4/0.0.0.0/tcp/30310 binds the raw P2P port; --listen-addr /ip4/0.0.0.0/tcp/30311/ws binds the WebSocket P2P port. The WSS port (30312) is handled by the nginx proxy configured in the next step — do not pass a /wss listen-addr here. To run as a background service, wrap this command in a systemd unit file.

steps.action: Configure nginx as a WSS reverse proxy
Test the bootnode on all three protocols
steps.description:
Verify the bootnode is reachable on each protocol. Replace INSERT_DNS_NAME with your server DNS name and INSERT_NODE_ID with the public key from step 1.

P2P:
  polkadot --chain polkadot --base-path /tmp/node --name 'Bootnode testnode' --reserved-only --reserved-nodes '/dns/INSERT_DNS_NAME/tcp/30310/p2p/INSERT_NODE_ID' --no-hardware-benchmarks

P2P/WS:
  polkadot --chain polkadot --base-path /tmp/node --name 'Bootnode testnode' --reserved-only --reserved-nodes '/dns/INSERT_DNS_NAME/tcp/30311/ws/p2p/INSERT_NODE_ID' --no-hardware-benchmarks

P2P/WSS:
  polkadot --chain polkadot --base-path /tmp/node --name 'Bootnode testnode' --reserved-only --reserved-nodes '/dns/INSERT_DNS_NAME/tcp/30312/wss/p2p/INSERT_NODE_ID' --no-hardware-benchmarks

Each should log 'syncing 1 peers' within a few seconds. Append -lsub-libp2p=trace for detailed libp2p debug output if troubleshooting.

error_patterns.pattern: Address already in use / EADDRINUSE on port 30310, 30311, or 30312
error_patterns.cause: Another process is bound to the port.
error_patterns.resolution: Run 'ss -tlnp | grep 303' to identify the process. Stop it or change the polkadot --listen-addr port and the nginx listen directive to an unused port.

error_patterns.pattern: polkadot: command not found
error_patterns.cause: The polkadot binary is not installed or not in $PATH.
error_patterns.resolution: Install the binary following https://docs.polkadot.com/node-infrastructure/run-a-validator/onboarding-and-offboarding/set-up-validator.md. Verify the installation directory is in $PATH: echo $PATH.

error_patterns.pattern: nginx: configuration file test failed
error_patterns.cause: Syntax error in the nginx site config or the SSL certificate path does not exist.
error_patterns.resolution: Run 'sudo nginx -t' to see the specific error line. Verify that /etc/letsencrypt/live/INSERT_DNS_NAME/fullchain.pem and privkey.pem exist. Renew with certbot if expired: sudo certbot renew.

error_patterns.pattern: syncing 0 peers after several seconds
error_patterns.cause: The bootnode is unreachable — port blocked, wrong DNS name, mismatched node ID, or the bootnode process exited.
error_patterns.resolution: Confirm the bootnode process is running: ps aux | grep polkadot. Check firewall rules allow inbound on 30310/30311/30312. Verify DNS resolves to the correct server IP: nslookup INSERT_DNS_NAME. Double-check the node ID in the --reserved-nodes multiaddress matches the public key from step 1.

examples.scenario: Common scenario: start a bootnode for the Polkadot mainnet
examples.user_says: Set up a Polkadot relay chain bootnode with P2P and WSS support
actions: Run 'polkadot key generate-node-key' and record the public key
Start polkadot with --listen-addr for ports 30310 (P2P) and 30311 (WS)
Create nginx site config proxying port 30312 (WSS) to 30311 (WS) with Let's Encrypt SSL
Test by running a temporary polkadot node with --reserved-only --reserved-nodes for each of the three protocols
Confirm each test logs 'syncing 1 peers'
examples.result: Bootnode running; accepting P2P on 30310, WS on 30311, and WSS on 30312; test node reports syncing 1 peers on all three protocols

examples.scenario: Edge case: peer ID changes after server rebuild
examples.user_says: My bootnode restarted after a server migration and other nodes can no longer connect
actions: Locate the backed-up secret_ed25519 file and restore it to chains/<chain>/network/ under the --base-path
If the file was not backed up, generate a new key with 'polkadot key generate-node-key' and update the multiaddress in the chain spec and any hardcoded bootnode lists
Restart the bootnode and confirm the public key matches the multiaddress in use
examples.result: Bootnode restored with its original peer ID, or chain spec updated with the new peer ID; other nodes reconnect

skills.title: Set Up a Polkadot Validator Node
skills.description: Installs the three Polkadot validator binaries (polkadot, polkadot-prepare-worker, polkadot-execute-worker) needed to run a validator node, and prepares the server with NTP time synchronization and Landlock security. Covers four installation paths: GPG-verified curl download, APT package manager (Ubuntu/Debian), Docker image, or building from source. Use when provisioning a new validator server or upgrading binaries on an existing one. Trigger phrases: 'install Polkadot validator binaries', 'set up validator node', 'install polkadot-prepare-worker', 'configure validator server'. First step in the full validator onboarding flow before key management and bonding.

runtime: Linux server with kernel 5.16 or later (Ubuntu 22.04 LTS or newer recommended)

steps.action: Verify or install NTP time synchronization
steps.description:
Validators need accurate system time to avoid missing block authorship. Check NTP status with 'timedatectl'. If the output does not show 'System clock synchronized: yes', install and start NTP:

  sudo apt-get install ntp
  sudo ntpq -p

The 'ntpq -p' output should list one or more active peers with a '+' or '*' status prefix. Skipping NTP can cause the validator to miss blocks due to minor clock drift.

Landlock (Linux kernel 5.13+) is required by the Polkadot validator for sandboxing. Run the command above as root. If Landlock is active, you will see kernel log entries referencing 'landlock'. If there is no output, your kernel does not have Landlock enabled. Most modern Ubuntu/Debian kernels (5.16+) include it by default. If Landlock is absent, upgrade to a supported kernel or rebuild with Landlock enabled (see https://docs.kernel.org/userspace-api/landlock.html#kernel-support). Polkadot validators can run without Landlock but will operate without the sandboxing protection.

steps.action: Install the Polkadot binaries via curl with GPG verification (recommended method)
steps.description:
Download all three binaries for release polkadot-stable2512-2 and verify each with GPG. The release tag is polkadot-stable2512-2 — check https://github.com/paritytech/polkadot-sdk/releases for a newer stable release and replace all occurrences of 'polkadot-stable2512-2' in the commands if you want a different version. GPG key 90BD75EBBB8E95CB3DA6078F94A4029AB4B35DAE is the ParityReleases key for current releases. A 'Good signature' message after each --verify confirms the binary is authentic. If GPG verification fails with 'BAD signature', discard the binary and re-download.

ALTERNATIVE — APT package manager (Ubuntu/Debian):
  gpg --keyserver hkps://keyserver.ubuntu.com --receive-keys 90BD75EBBB8E95CB3DA6078F94A4029AB4B35DAE
  gpg --export 90BD75EBBB8E95CB3DA6078F94A4029AB4B35DAE > /usr/share/keyrings/parity.gpg
  echo 'deb [signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/parity.gpg] https://releases.parity.io/deb release main' > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/parity.list
  apt update && apt install parity-keyring polkadot

ALTERNATIVE — Docker:
  docker pull parity/polkadot:stable2512-2

steps.action: Verify all three binaries are installed and versions match
steps.expected_output: All three commands print the same version string, e.g. 'polkadot stable2512-2-...'
steps.description: Run all three version commands and confirm the version strings are identical. If one binary is missing ('command not found'), ensure it was moved to /usr/local/bin/ in step 3 and that /usr/local/bin is in $PATH (echo $PATH). All three binaries must be in the same directory for the validator to function. If versions differ, re-download the mismatched binary from the same release tag used in step 3.

error_patterns.pattern: gpg: BAD signature
error_patterns.cause: The downloaded binary was corrupted or tampered with during download.
error_patterns.resolution: Delete all downloaded files and repeat step 3 from a trusted network connection. Do not use any binary that fails GPG verification.

error_patterns.pattern: polkadot: command not found after installation
error_patterns.cause: The binary was not moved to a directory in $PATH, or the directory is not in $PATH.
error_patterns.resolution: Run 'echo $PATH' and verify /usr/local/bin is listed. If not, run 'export PATH=$PATH:/usr/local/bin'. For persistence, add this export to /etc/environment or ~/.bashrc.

error_patterns.pattern: version mismatch between polkadot and worker binaries
error_patterns.cause: The three binaries were downloaded from different releases.
error_patterns.resolution: Remove all three binaries and re-download all from the same release tag (e.g., polkadot-stable2512-2) from https://github.com/paritytech/polkadot-sdk/releases. All three must share the same version to interoperate.

error_patterns.pattern: Landlock not in dmesg output
error_patterns.cause: The running kernel is older than 5.13 or was compiled without Landlock.
error_patterns.resolution: Upgrade the kernel: on Ubuntu, run 'sudo apt-get install linux-generic-hwe-22.04' then reboot. Verify with 'uname -r' that the new kernel is 5.16 or later. Alternatively, consult https://docs.kernel.org/userspace-api/landlock.html#kernel-support.

examples.scenario: Common scenario: install on a fresh Ubuntu 22.04 server
examples.user_says: Install the Polkadot validator binaries on a new Ubuntu server
actions: Run 'timedatectl' to check NTP sync; install ntp if not synced
Run 'dmesg | grep landlock' to verify Landlock is active
Download polkadot, polkadot-prepare-worker, and polkadot-execute-worker from release polkadot-stable2512-2
Verify each binary with GPG key 90BD75EBBB8E95CB3DA6078F94A4029AB4B35DAE
Move all three to /usr/local/bin and run --version on each to confirm matching versions
examples.result: All three binaries installed at polkadot-stable2512-2, GPG verified, and accessible system-wide; ready for key management

examples.scenario: Edge case: APT install on a Debian-based server without curl
examples.user_says: Install Polkadot on Ubuntu using apt instead of downloading manually
actions: Import the Parity GPG key into /usr/share/keyrings/parity.gpg
Add the Parity APT repository to /etc/apt/sources.list.d/parity.list
Run 'apt update && apt install parity-keyring polkadot'
Verify with 'polkadot --version', 'polkadot-execute-worker --version', 'polkadot-prepare-worker --version'
examples.result: Polkadot and both worker binaries installed via APT with automatic future upgrades via apt upgrade

skills.title: Set Up and Use Chopsticks for Chain Forking
skills.description: Installs Chopsticks and creates a local fork of any Polkadot SDK-based chain for testing, debugging, and XCM simulation without touching live state. Covers global and local npm installation, YAML config file setup, fork creation via CLI flags or config, Polkadot.js interaction, block replay, and multi-chain XCM testing. Use when you need to test extrinsics, replay historical blocks, override chain storage, or simulate XCM messages locally. Trigger phrases: 'fork a chain with Chopsticks', 'local chain fork', 'replay blocks Chopsticks', 'test XCM locally', 'Chopsticks install'. Version: 1.2.7. Note: Chopsticks uses the Smoldot light client and does not support Ethereum JSON-RPC — MetaMask cannot connect to a Chopsticks fork.

runtime: npm (included with Node.js) or yarn or pnpm
network: Access to a WebSocket RPC endpoint for the chain to fork (e.g., wss://polkadot-rpc.dwellir.com)

steps.description: Install Chopsticks 1.2.7 globally. After installation, 'chopsticks' is available system-wide. For a project-local installation instead: mkdir my-chopsticks-project && cd my-chopsticks-project && npm init -y && npm i @acala-network/chopsticks@1.2.7. With local installation, run via 'npx @acala-network/chopsticks' instead of 'chopsticks' in subsequent steps.

Create a YAML configuration file (e.g., polkadot.yml) describing the chain to fork. Pre-built configs for common Polkadot SDK chains are available at https://github.com/AcalaNetwork/chopsticks/tree/master/configs — download one with:
  curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/AcalaNetwork/chopsticks/master/configs/polkadot.yml -o polkadot.yml

A minimal config for Polkadot looks like:
  endpoint: wss://polkadot-rpc.dwellir.com
  mock-signature-host: true
  block: 1000  # optional: fork from this block number

The 'import-storage' section (optional) lets you override storage values before the fork starts — useful for funding test accounts:
  import-storage:
    System:
      Account:
        - - - 5GrwvaEF5zXb26Fz9rcQpDWS57CtERHpNehXCPcNoHGKutQY  # Alice
          - data:
              free: '100000000000000000'

Replace the endpoint with the WSS RPC URL for your target chain.

Start the fork using the YAML config from step 2. Alternatively, fork without a config file using CLI flags:
  npx @acala-network/chopsticks --endpoint wss://polkadot-rpc.dwellir.com --block 100

Choose the block number to fork from with --block (omit to use the chain head). Chopsticks caches state locally in an SQLite DB (set --db ./chopsticks.db to reuse between runs). When the fork is ready, you will see 'Running on port 8000'. The local fork is now accessible at ws://localhost:8000.

Replay block 1000 from Polkadot and save the state diff to a JSON file. Replace the endpoint and block number as needed. The output includes detailed storage changes and runtime logs. Add --html to generate an HTML visualization of the storage diff. This is useful for debugging why a specific transaction produced unexpected state changes.

Fork multiple chains simultaneously to test XCM message flow. The --r flag specifies the relay chain config (by name or file path) and --p specifies parachain configs. After startup, each chain runs on a different port (printed in the output). Send XCM messages between the forked chains using the Polkadot.js API or dev_* WebSocket commands. Note: XCM testing requires config files for each chain; use pre-built configs from https://github.com/AcalaNetwork/chopsticks/tree/master/configs.

error_patterns.pattern: Cannot connect to endpoint / WebSocket connection failed
error_patterns.cause: The RPC endpoint URL is incorrect, the chain is temporarily unavailable, or the WSS endpoint requires authentication.
error_patterns.resolution: Verify the endpoint URL by connecting with wscat: wscat -c wss://your-endpoint. Try a public endpoint such as wss://polkadot-rpc.dwellir.com for Polkadot. Check the chain's status page or Discord for outages.

error_patterns.pattern: chopsticks: command not found
error_patterns.cause: Global npm installation did not add the binary to PATH, or Chopsticks was installed locally without npx.
error_patterns.resolution: For global installs, run 'npm root -g' to find the global bin directory and ensure it is in $PATH. For local installs, use 'npx @acala-network/chopsticks' instead of 'chopsticks'.

error_patterns.pattern: MetaMask / Ethereum tooling cannot connect to the fork
error_patterns.cause: Chopsticks uses the Smoldot light client which only supports the native Polkadot SDK API, not Ethereum JSON-RPC.
error_patterns.resolution: This is a known limitation. Chopsticks forks cannot be used with MetaMask or ethers.js. For EVM-compatible local testing, use a local Polkadot Hub development node instead.

error_patterns.pattern: Error replaying block: state root mismatch
error_patterns.cause: The local fork has diverged from the live chain state, often because storage was manually modified before replay.
error_patterns.resolution: Restart Chopsticks without --db to use a fresh state (or delete the SQLite db file). Avoid modifying storage with dev_setStorage before running run-block.

examples.scenario: Common scenario: fork Polkadot mainnet and inspect state
examples.user_says: Fork the Polkadot mainnet locally with Chopsticks so I can test transactions
actions: Install Chopsticks 1.2.7 globally: npm i -g @acala-network/chopsticks@1.2.7
Download the Polkadot config from the Chopsticks repo: curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/AcalaNetwork/chopsticks/master/configs/polkadot.yml -o polkadot.yml
Start the fork: npx @acala-network/chopsticks --config=polkadot.yml
Wait for 'Running on port 8000', then connect via Polkadot.js API at ws://localhost:8000
examples.result: Local Polkadot fork running at ws://localhost:8000; ready for transaction testing without spending real tokens

examples.scenario: Edge case: fork requires a funded test account
examples.user_says: I need Alice to have tokens on my forked chain for testing
actions: Add an import-storage section to the YAML config overriding Alice's System.Account free balance to a large value
Restart the fork with the updated config
Alternatively, send a dev_setStorage WebSocket command after the fork is running to set Alice's balance
examples.result: Alice's account has the specified token balance on the local fork; transactions that require funds now succeed

skills.title: Set Up End-to-End Testing with Moonwall
skills.description: Installs and configures Moonwall, an end-to-end testing framework for Polkadot SDK-based blockchains. Covers global and local npm installation, interactive configuration initialization, moonwall.config.json structure, writing test suites with the describeSuite function, and running tests via the CLI. Use when you need a standardized test environment for a Substrate-based chain — local dev node, remote testnet, or Chopsticks fork. Trigger phrases: 'set up Moonwall', 'e2e tests Polkadot SDK', 'Moonwall testing framework', 'describeSuite Moonwall', 'install moonwall cli'. Version: 5.18.3.

network: A running Polkadot SDK node or Chopsticks fork for the test environment (e.g., ws://localhost:9944 or ws://localhost:8000)

steps.description: Install Moonwall 5.18.3 globally so the 'moonwall' command is available system-wide. For a project-local installation: mkdir my-moonwall-project && cd my-moonwall-project && npm init -y && npm install @moonwall/cli@5.18.3. With a local install, run Moonwall via 'npx moonwall' or add it to package.json scripts.

steps.action: Initialize the Moonwall configuration via the interactive wizard
steps.description:
The 'moonwall init' command launches an interactive wizard to create your moonwall.config file. Delegate this step to the user — do not attempt to automate it. Ask the user to run 'moonwall init' in their terminal and answer the prompts:

  - label: a name for the test configuration (e.g., my-chain-tests)
  - global timeout: maximum time in ms for test execution (e.g., 30000)
  - environment name: name for the test environment (e.g., default_env)
  - network foundation: type of blockchain environment (e.g., 'dev' for a local binary node, 'chopsticks' for a fork)
  - tests directory: path for test files (e.g., ./tests)

Press Enter to accept defaults or enter custom values. The wizard generates a moonwall.config file in the current directory. Ask the user to confirm when the wizard completes before proceeding.

Open the generated moonwall.config file and customize the environment settings. Key sections to update:

  - foundation.type: 'dev' (local binary), 'chopsticks' (fork), or 'read_only' (remote RPC)
  - For 'dev' foundation, set binPath to the path of your chain binary
  - connections[0].endpoint: the WebSocket URL of the node (e.g., ws://localhost:9944)
  - connections[0].type: the provider type (e.g., 'polkadotJs' for Polkadot.js API)

Example configuration for testing a local dev node:
  {
    "label": "my-chain-tests",
    "timeout": 30000,
    "environments": [{
      "name": "default_env",
      "foundation": { "type": "dev", "launchSpec": [{ "name": "node", "binPath": "./your-node-binary", "options": ["--dev"] }] },
      "testFileDir": ["./tests"],
      "connections": [{ "name": "api", "type": "polkadotJs", "endpoints": ["ws://localhost:9944"] }]
    }]
  }

Run the tests against the 'default_env' environment defined in moonwall.config. Replace 'default_env' with the environment name you chose in step 2. The test runner outputs:
  - Test suite execution status (pass/fail per suite)
  - Individual test case results
  - Execution time per test
  - Detailed error logs for failed tests

If the foundation type is 'dev', Moonwall launches the node binary automatically and tears it down after tests complete. If using 'read_only' or 'chopsticks', ensure the endpoint is reachable before running tests.

error_patterns.pattern: moonwall: command not found
error_patterns.cause: Global npm installation did not add the binary to PATH.
error_patterns.resolution: Run 'npm root -g' to find the global bin directory and verify it is in $PATH. Alternatively, use 'npx moonwall' for a local installation.

error_patterns.pattern: Cannot find module '@moonwall/cli'
error_patterns.cause: Moonwall was not installed or the local installation is missing.
error_patterns.resolution: Run 'npm install @moonwall/cli@5.18.3' in the project directory. For global, run 'npm install -g @moonwall/cli@5.18.3'.

error_patterns.pattern: Error: Cannot connect to endpoint ws://localhost:9944
error_patterns.cause: The node specified in the moonwall.config foundation is not running, or the endpoint URL is incorrect.
error_patterns.resolution: Start the node manually and verify it is listening on the configured port before running Moonwall. For 'dev' foundation, ensure the binPath points to an executable binary and the --dev flag is appropriate for your chain.

error_patterns.pattern: Timeout exceeded for test suite
error_patterns.cause: The test environment took too long to start or a test operation exceeded the global timeout.
error_patterns.resolution: Increase the 'timeout' value in moonwall.config (e.g., from 30000 to 60000 ms). For slow chain operations like block finalization, add explicit wait-for-block assertions rather than fixed sleeps.

examples.user_says: Set up Moonwall to run e2e tests for my Polkadot SDK chain
actions: Install Moonwall 5.18.3 globally: npm install -g @moonwall/cli@5.18.3
Ask the user to run 'moonwall init' and complete the interactive wizard, choosing 'dev' foundation
Update moonwall.config with the correct node binPath and ws://localhost:9944 connection
Create a test file in ./tests using describeSuite with a foundationMethods of 'dev'
Run 'moonwall test default_env -c moonwall.config'
examples.result: Moonwall launches the node binary, runs all test suites, and reports pass/fail results per test case

examples.scenario: Edge case: test environment fails to start
examples.user_says: Moonwall says it cannot connect to the node endpoint
actions: Check the foundation type in moonwall.config — for 'dev', verify binPath is correct and the binary has execute permission
For 'read_only' or 'chopsticks', start the node or Chopsticks fork manually and confirm it is listening on the configured endpoint
Increase the connection timeout in moonwall.config if the node starts slowly
examples.result: Node is reachable; Moonwall connects and runs tests

skills.title: Fork a Parachain Using Chopsticks
skills.description: Installs Chopsticks and forks a live Polkadot SDK parachain locally for testing and development. Covers global npm installation, YAML configuration, fork startup, and WebSocket dev commands (dev_newBlock, dev_setStorage, dev_setHead, dev_timeTravel, dev_setRuntimeLogLevel). Use when testing parachain-specific logic, extrinsics, or storage mutations without deploying to a live network. Trigger phrases: 'fork a parachain', 'Chopsticks parachain fork', 'dev_setStorage chopsticks', 'local parachain test environment'. Version: 1.2.7. Note: does not support Ethereum JSON-RPC — MetaMask cannot connect.

network: WebSocket RPC endpoint for the parachain to fork (e.g., wss://moonbeam.public.blastapi.io or wss://polkadot-rpc.dwellir.com)

steps.description: Install Chopsticks 1.2.7 globally so the 'chopsticks' command is available system-wide. To install locally in a project instead: mkdir my-chopsticks-project && cd my-chopsticks-project && npm init -y && npm i @acala-network/chopsticks@1.2.7. With a local install, use 'npx @acala-network/chopsticks' in place of 'chopsticks' for all subsequent commands. Verify installation: npx @acala-network/chopsticks --help

steps.action: Create or download a fork configuration file
steps.description:
Create a YAML configuration file for the parachain to fork. Pre-built configs for common Polkadot ecosystem chains are available at https://github.com/AcalaNetwork/chopsticks/tree/master/configs. Download one with:
  curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/AcalaNetwork/chopsticks/master/configs/moonbeam.yml -o parachain.yml

Or write one manually. Minimal example:
  endpoint: wss://moonbeam.public.blastapi.io
  mock-signature-host: true

Optional: override storage in the config to pre-fund test accounts:
  import-storage:
    System:
      Account:
        - - - 5GrwvaEF5zXb26Fz9rcQpDWS57CtERHpNehXCPcNoHGKutQY  # Alice address
          - data:
              free: '100000000000000'

Replace the endpoint with the WSS URL for your target parachain. All CLI config options (genesis, timestamp, endpoint, block, db, port, build-block-mode, import-storage, allow-unresolved-imports, html, mock-signature-host) can be set in this file or passed as CLI flags.

Start the Chopsticks fork using the YAML config file. Alternatively, start directly from the CLI without a config file:
  npx @acala-network/chopsticks --endpoint wss://moonbeam.public.blastapi.io

Choose a specific block to fork from with --block <number>. Chopsticks will fetch state from the live chain and start an RPC server. When you see 'Running on port 8000', the fork is ready. Connect any Polkadot SDK tooling to ws://localhost:8000.

steps.action: Interact with the fork via WebSocket dev commands

files.description: CI-backed Chopsticks integration test verifying fork startup and WebSocket command behavior

error_patterns.pattern: Cannot connect to endpoint / WebSocket connection failed on startup
error_patterns.cause: The parachain RPC endpoint URL is incorrect or the chain is temporarily offline.
error_patterns.resolution: Test the endpoint with wscat: wscat -c wss://your-parachain-endpoint. Check the parachain's status page or community Discord for outages. Substitute an alternate public endpoint if available.

error_patterns.pattern: chopsticks: command not found
error_patterns.cause: Global npm install did not add to PATH, or Chopsticks was installed locally.
error_patterns.resolution: For global installs, run 'npm root -g' to locate the bin directory and add it to $PATH. For local installs, use 'npx @acala-network/chopsticks' instead.

error_patterns.pattern: MetaMask or Ethereum tools cannot connect to the fork
error_patterns.cause: Chopsticks uses the Smoldot light client which does not implement Ethereum JSON-RPC.
error_patterns.resolution: This is a known limitation of Chopsticks. For EVM testing, use a local Polkadot Hub development node instead of a Chopsticks fork.

error_patterns.pattern: dev_setStorage has no effect / storage value unchanged after call
error_patterns.cause: The storage key path or value format is incorrect, or the block build mode is set to Batch and no new block has been produced.
error_patterns.resolution: After calling dev_setStorage, trigger a new block with dev_newBlock to commit the storage change. Verify the storage key path by querying the current state first (e.g., api.query.system.account). Use dev_setBlockBuildMode with 'Instant' mode to automatically produce a block per transaction during testing.

examples.scenario: Common scenario: fork Moonbeam and test a storage mutation
examples.user_says: Fork Moonbeam locally with Chopsticks so I can test an extrinsic
actions: Install Chopsticks 1.2.7: npm i -g @acala-network/chopsticks@1.2.7
Download the Moonbeam config: curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/AcalaNetwork/chopsticks/master/configs/moonbeam.yml -o parachain.yml
Start the fork: npx @acala-network/chopsticks --config=parachain.yml
Once 'Running on port 8000' appears, connect via Polkadot.js API at ws://localhost:8000
Use dev_setStorage to set a test account balance, then submit and verify the extrinsic
examples.result: Local Moonbeam fork running at ws://localhost:8000; storage mutations applied; extrinsic tested without real tokens

examples.scenario: Edge case: storage override not visible after dev_setStorage
examples.user_says: I called dev_setStorage to fund an account but the balance is still zero
actions: After calling dev_setStorage, send a dev_newBlock request with count: 1 to commit the change
If using Batch block build mode (default), storage changes are applied at next block production
Switch to Instant mode with dev_setBlockBuildMode to get automatic per-transaction block production during testing
examples.result: Storage override committed after block production; account shows updated balance

skills.title: Build a Zero-to-Hero Smart Contract DApp with Viem and Next.js
skills.description: Scaffolds a full-stack Polkadot Hub dApp: deploys a Solidity Storage contract via Hardhat Ignition to TestNet, then builds a Next.js 14 frontend with Viem wallet connect, read, and write components. Use when building a complete end-to-end smart contract application on Polkadot Hub from scratch. Trigger phrases: 'build a dapp polkadot', 'zero to hero tutorial', 'smart contract with frontend viem', 'deploy and connect next.js'. Covers dotenv private key security, 5000 gwei gas config, evmVersion cancun, Ignition recovery, and Next.js/Viem component wiring. Output: running dApp at localhost:3000.

network: Polkadot Hub TestNet RPC: https://services.polkadothub-rpc.com/testnet (chain ID 420420417, base fee 1000 gwei)
tokens: Testnet PAS tokens from https://faucet.polkadot.io/ — required for contract deployment gas
wallet: MetaMask configured for Polkadot Hub TestNet (chainId 420420417)
0x-prefixed EVM private key for a funded testnet account

env_vars.name: PRIVATE_KEY
env_vars.description: 0x-prefixed EVM private key for the deployer wallet. Must be funded with testnet PAS. Edit .env directly — never paste in chat.

steps.description: Clone revm-hardhat-examples. This repo contains the complete zero-to-hero-dapp with both the Hardhat contract project and the Next.js frontend.
Install Hardhat and contract dependencies, then add dotenv. dotenv replaces Hardhat's interactive vars system, which cannot be used in agent shells.

steps.action: Create .env file and protect it with .gitignore
steps.description: Create a .env file with an empty PRIVATE_KEY placeholder. Stop and ask the user to edit .env directly, filling in PRIVATE_KEY=0x<their_key>. Do NOT ask for the private key in chat. Wait for the user to confirm .env is filled before proceeding.

steps.action: Update hardhat.config.ts for TestNet and security
steps.description:
Open hardhat.config.ts and apply ALL of the following changes:
1. Add 'import "dotenv/config";' as the very first line.
2. Replace any vars.get('PRIVATE_KEY') with process.env.PRIVATE_KEY as string — Hardhat interactive vars do not work in agent shells.
3. Remove 'import { vars } from "hardhat/config";' if present.
4. In the polkadotTestnet network block, set url: 'https://services.polkadothub-rpc.com/testnet' and chainId: 420420417.
5. Add gasPrice: 5000000000000 to the polkadotTestnet network block (5000 gwei = 5x the 1000 gwei TestNet base fee).
6. Add or confirm evmVersion: 'cancun' in the Solidity compiler settings — required for OpenZeppelin v5.4.0+ (mcopy opcode).
7. Ensure requiredConfirmations is 1 in any ignition config block (never 0 — zero causes IGN401 errors on TestNet).
Save the file.
Compile Storage.sol. If you see 'invalid opcode: MCOPY', verify evmVersion: 'cancun' is set in the Solidity compiler settings in hardhat.config.ts and recompile.

steps.action: Deploy the contract to Polkadot Hub TestNet
commands: npx hardhat ignition deploy ignition/modules/Storage.ts --network polkadotTestnet
steps.description:
Deploy via Hardhat Ignition. When prompted 'Do you want to deploy this?', answer 'yes'. Save the deployed contract address — needed in step 9.

Recovery guidance:
- 'priority is too low': verify gasPrice: 5000000000000 is in hardhat.config.ts, then redeploy.
- IGN401 (transaction dropped): first check ignition/deployments/<chainId>/deployed_addresses.json — if the address is listed, deployment succeeded despite the error; use that address. If absent, delete ignition/deployments/ and redeploy.
- 'Transaction Already Imported': do NOT retry at the same gas price. Delete ignition/deployments/ and add a higher gasPrice, then redeploy.

Scaffold Next.js 14 TypeScript app named 'frontend'. Piped 'n' answers handle any unexpected interactive prompts from create-next-app. If this fails due to prompt changes, run: npx create-next-app@14 frontend and answer No to all questions except TypeScript (Yes) and App Router (Yes).
Install viem for contract interaction, wagmi for React hooks, and React Query (required by wagmi v2).

steps.action: Update contract address in contract config
steps.description: Open src/contracts/contract.ts (or the equivalent contract config file in the frontend). Replace the CONTRACT_ADDRESS placeholder or any hardcoded example address with the deployed contract address saved in step 6. Save the file.

Start the Next.js dev server. Open http://localhost:3000 in a browser. Click 'Connect Wallet' and approve the MetaMask popup. Read the current stored value, then write a new value and confirm the MetaMask transaction. Verify the displayed value updates after the transaction is mined.

error_patterns.pattern: priority is too low
error_patterns.cause: Transaction gas price is below the 1000 gwei base fee on Polkadot Hub TestNet. Default Hardhat gas estimation produces values too low for this network.
error_patterns.resolution: Set gasPrice: 5000000000000 in the polkadotTestnet network block in hardhat.config.ts. Delete ignition/deployments/ if a prior attempt was made, then redeploy.

error_patterns.pattern: IGN401 / Transaction dropped
error_patterns.cause: Ignition lost track of the transaction. The transaction may have succeeded or may be stuck in the mempool.
error_patterns.resolution: Check ignition/deployments/<chainId>/deployed_addresses.json. If the contract address is present, deployment succeeded — use that address. If absent: send a zero-value replacement transaction at the same nonce with gasPrice 2000000000000 (2x base fee), wait for it to mine, then delete ignition/deployments/ and redeploy.

error_patterns.pattern: invalid opcode: MCOPY
error_patterns.cause: OpenZeppelin v5.4.0+ uses the mcopy opcode (Cancun EVM upgrade). Solidity defaults to an older EVM version.
error_patterns.resolution: Add evmVersion: 'cancun' to the Solidity compiler settings in hardhat.config.ts. Recompile with 'npx hardhat compile'.

error_patterns.pattern: Error: could not detect network
error_patterns.cause: Incorrect RPC URL or chain ID in hardhat.config.ts.
error_patterns.resolution: Set url: 'https://services.polkadothub-rpc.com/testnet' and chainId: 420420417 in the polkadotTestnet network block.

error_patterns.pattern: Module not found: viem or wagmi
error_patterns.cause: Frontend dependencies were not installed.
error_patterns.resolution: Run 'npm install viem wagmi @tanstack/react-query' in the zero-to-hero-dapp/frontend directory.

actions: Clone revm-hardhat-examples and navigate to zero-to-hero-dapp/storage-contract

If absent: delete ignition/deployments/ and redeploy with gasPrice: 5000000000000 confirmed in config

skills.title: Deploy Uniswap V2 on Polkadot Hub
skills.description: Clones and deploys Uniswap V2 (Factory and Pair contracts) to Polkadot Hub TestNet using Hardhat. Use when you need a Uniswap V2-compatible AMM deployment on Polkadot Hub for testing or integration. Trigger phrases: 'deploy uniswap v2 polkadot', 'uniswap v2 asset hub', 'AMM factory polkadot hub'. Covers dotenv credential setup for local and TestNet keys, 5000 gwei gas configuration, evmVersion cancun, Ignition deployment of Factory and Pair, and stuck-transaction recovery. Requires testnet PAS for gas.

network: Polkadot Hub TestNet RPC: https://services.polkadothub-rpc.com/testnet (chain ID 420420417, base fee 1000 gwei)
tokens: Testnet PAS tokens from https://faucet.polkadot.io/ — required for deployment gas
wallet: MetaMask configured for Polkadot Hub TestNet (chainId 420420417)
0x-prefixed EVM private key for a funded testnet account

env_vars.name: AH_PRIVATE_KEY
env_vars.description: 0x-prefixed EVM private key for Polkadot Hub TestNet deployment. Must be funded with testnet PAS. Edit .env directly — never paste in chat.

env_vars.name: LOCAL_PRIVATE_KEY
env_vars.description: 0x-prefixed EVM private key for local dev node testing. Can use the default Hardhat dev key (0xac0974bec39a17e36ba4a6b4d238ff944bacb478cbed5efcae784d7bf4f2ff80) for local testing only.

steps.description: Clone polkavm-hardhat-examples. Navigate to the uniswap-v2 example directory after cloning.

steps.action: Navigate to the Uniswap V2 example and install dependencies
steps.description: Install all dependencies, then add dotenv. dotenv replaces Hardhat's interactive vars system for private key management. If the uniswap-v2 directory does not exist, list the cloned repo top-level directories to locate the correct subdirectory name.

Create .env with placeholders for both keys. Stop and ask the user to edit .env directly:
- AH_PRIVATE_KEY=0x<testnet_key> (funded with PAS on Polkadot Hub TestNet)
- LOCAL_PRIVATE_KEY=0x<local_key> (for local dev node only; the Hardhat default key 0xac0974...f2ff80 works for local testing)
Do NOT ask for private keys in chat. Wait for user confirmation before proceeding.

steps.action: Update hardhat.config.ts for TestNet and security
steps.description:
Open hardhat.config.ts and apply ALL changes:
1. Add 'import "dotenv/config";' as the very first line.
2. Replace any vars.get('AH_PRIVATE_KEY') with process.env.AH_PRIVATE_KEY as string, and vars.get('LOCAL_PRIVATE_KEY') with process.env.LOCAL_PRIVATE_KEY as string.
3. Remove any 'import { vars } from "hardhat/config";' if present.
4. In the polkadotTestnet (or assetHub) network block, set url: 'https://services.polkadothub-rpc.com/testnet' and chainId: 420420417.
5. Add gasPrice: 5000000000000 to the polkadotTestnet network block (5000 gwei = 5x the 1000 gwei base fee).
6. Add or confirm evmVersion: 'cancun' in the Solidity compiler settings — required for OpenZeppelin v5.4.0+ (mcopy opcode).
7. Ensure requiredConfirmations is 1 in any ignition config block.
Save the file.
Compile the Uniswap V2 Factory and Pair contracts. If you see 'invalid opcode: MCOPY', add evmVersion: 'cancun' to the Solidity compiler settings and retry.

steps.action: Run the test suite against TestNet (optional)
steps.description: Optionally run the test suite against Polkadot Hub TestNet to verify the contracts behave correctly. This consumes testnet PAS for gas. If PAS balance is low, skip and proceed directly to deployment. Local testing against a dev node is also possible with 'npx hardhat test' (omit --network flag).

steps.action: Deploy contracts to Polkadot Hub TestNet
commands: npx hardhat ignition deploy ignition/modules/UniswapV2.ts --network polkadotTestnet
steps.description:
Deploy the Uniswap V2 Factory (and optionally Pair) contracts via Ignition. Answer 'yes' when prompted to confirm deployment. Save the Factory contract address from the output.

If the deployment script is not an Ignition module, check package.json for a deploy script and use: npx hardhat run scripts/deploy.ts --network polkadotTestnet

Recovery for stuck transactions:
- 'priority is too low': verify gasPrice: 5000000000000 in hardhat.config.ts, delete ignition/deployments/, and redeploy.
- IGN401: check deployed_addresses.json first; if Factory address is present, deployment succeeded.

After deployment completes, verify the Factory contract is live by calling eth_getCode at the deployed address via the TestNet RPC. Run: curl -s -X POST https://services.polkadothub-rpc.com/testnet -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"eth_getCode","params":["<FACTORY_ADDRESS>","latest"],"id":1}' and confirm the response contains a non-empty bytecode string (not '0x').

error_patterns.pattern: priority is too low
error_patterns.cause: Transaction gas price below the 1000 gwei base fee on Polkadot Hub TestNet.
error_patterns.resolution: Set gasPrice: 5000000000000 in the polkadotTestnet network block in hardhat.config.ts, delete ignition/deployments/, and redeploy.

error_patterns.pattern: invalid opcode: MCOPY
error_patterns.cause: Uniswap V2 contracts or their dependencies use the mcopy opcode (Cancun EVM), but the Solidity compiler defaults to an older EVM version.
error_patterns.resolution: Add evmVersion: 'cancun' to the Solidity compiler settings in hardhat.config.ts. Recompile with npx hardhat compile.

error_patterns.pattern: Transaction Already Imported
error_patterns.cause: A prior transaction is still pending in the mempool. Retrying at the same gas price produces this error.
error_patterns.resolution: Do not retry at the same gas price. Send a zero-value replacement transaction at the same nonce with gasPrice 2000000000000 (2x base fee). Once mined, delete ignition/deployments/ and redeploy.

error_patterns.pattern: Cannot find directory uniswap-v2 in cloned repo
error_patterns.cause: The example directory may be named differently in the reference repo.
error_patterns.resolution: List the root directories of polkavm-hardhat-examples with 'ls polkavm-hardhat-examples/' and navigate to the directory that corresponds to the Uniswap V2 example.

actions: Clone polkavm-hardhat-examples and navigate to the uniswap-v2 directory
Install dependencies and dotenv

examples.scenario: Edge case: transaction stuck, need to verify if Factory deployed
examples.user_says: Deployment failed with transaction dropped — did the Factory deploy?
actions: Check ignition/deployments/<chainId>/deployed_addresses.json for UniswapV2Factory address
If present: verify with eth_getCode at that address; if non-empty bytecode, deployment succeeded
If absent: delete ignition/deployments/, confirm gasPrice 5000000000000 is set, redeploy
examples.result: Factory address confirmed from state or clean redeployment completed

skills.title: Deploy an ERC-20 Token Using Remix IDE
skills.description: Deploys a Solidity ERC-20 token contract to Polkadot Hub TestNet entirely through the Remix browser IDE with MetaMask wallet injection. Use when you prefer a no-CLI workflow or are demonstrating contract deployment to non-developers. Trigger phrases: 'deploy ERC-20 remix', 'erc20 token remix polkadot', 'deploy token browser IDE'. Covers fetching contract code from revm-hardhat-examples, Remix compile settings (evmVersion cancun for OpenZeppelin v5), MetaMask TestNet configuration, and minting tokens via the Remix UI. No local environment required beyond a browser and MetaMask.

runtime: A modern web browser (Chrome or Firefox recommended)
network: Polkadot Hub TestNet (chainId 420420417) — configured in MetaMask
tokens: Testnet PAS tokens from https://faucet.polkadot.io/ — required to pay deployment gas
wallet: MetaMask browser extension installed and unlocked
An account funded with testnet PAS on Polkadot Hub TestNet (chainId 420420417, RPC: https://services.polkadothub-rpc.com/testnet)

steps.description:
Fetch the OpenZeppelin ERC-20 contract source from the reference repository. The contract file is at: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/polkadot-developers/revm-hardhat-examples/master/erc20-hardhat/contracts/MyToken.sol

Run: curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/polkadot-developers/revm-hardhat-examples/master/erc20-hardhat/contracts/MyToken.sol and display the contract source to the user. The user will paste this into Remix in the next step.

steps.action: Open Remix IDE and create the contract file
steps.description: Navigate to https://remix.ethereum.org in the browser. In the File Explorer panel (left sidebar), click the 'New File' icon and name it 'MyToken.sol'. Paste the full contract source fetched in step 1 into the editor. Save with Ctrl+S (or Cmd+S on Mac).

steps.action: Configure the Solidity compiler in Remix
steps.description:
Click the 'Solidity compiler' tab (shield icon) in the left sidebar. Set the following:
- Compiler version: select 0.8.20 or higher (match the pragma in the contract source)
- EVM version: select 'cancun' from the Advanced Configuration dropdown — required for OpenZeppelin v5.4.0+ (mcopy opcode)
Click 'Compile MyToken.sol'. A green checkmark indicates success. If compilation fails with 'Unknown key', verify the EVM version is set to 'cancun'.

steps.action: Add Polkadot Hub TestNet to MetaMask
steps.description:
If MetaMask does not already have Polkadot Hub TestNet configured, add it manually:
1. Open MetaMask and go to Settings > Networks > Add Network > Add a network manually.
2. Enter: Network Name: Polkadot Hub TestNet; RPC URL: https://services.polkadothub-rpc.com/testnet; Chain ID: 420420417; Currency Symbol: PAS.
3. Click Save and switch to the Polkadot Hub TestNet network.
4. Confirm the account shown in MetaMask has PAS tokens. If not, visit https://faucet.polkadot.io/ to request testnet PAS.

steps.action: Connect MetaMask to Remix and deploy
steps.description:
In Remix, click the 'Deploy & run transactions' tab (Ethereum icon) in the left sidebar. Set:
- Environment: select 'Injected Provider - MetaMask'
- MetaMask will pop up asking to connect — click Connect and approve.
- Confirm the Account field shows your MetaMask address and the network shows Polkadot Hub TestNet (chain ID 420420417).
- In the Contract dropdown, select 'MyToken'.
- Expand the Deploy section and fill in the constructor arguments: initialOwner = your MetaMask address.
- Click the orange 'Deploy' button.
- MetaMask will show a transaction confirmation — review the gas fee and click Confirm.
- Wait for the transaction to be mined (10–30 seconds on TestNet). The deployed contract address appears in the 'Deployed Contracts' section below.

steps.action: Verify the deployment and mint tokens
steps.description:
In the 'Deployed Contracts' section in Remix, expand the MyToken contract entry. Verify the deployment:
- Click 'name' to confirm it returns your token name.
- Click 'symbol' to confirm the token symbol.
- Click 'totalSupply' — should return 0 if the contract has no initial supply.

To mint tokens:
1. Expand the 'mint' function.
2. Enter: to = your MetaMask address, amount = 1000000000000000000 (1 token with 18 decimals).
3. Click 'transact' and confirm in MetaMask.
4. After mining, click 'balanceOf' and enter your address to verify the balance increased.

error_patterns.pattern: Remix compilation fails with 'Unknown key evmVersion cancun'
error_patterns.cause: Older Remix versions may not list 'cancun' in the EVM version dropdown.
error_patterns.resolution: Update to the latest Remix version by refreshing the page. The cancun option should appear in Advanced Configuration. Alternatively, use a custom EVM version by typing 'cancun' in the input field if available.

error_patterns.pattern: MetaMask shows wrong network after connecting to Remix
error_patterns.cause: MetaMask is on a different network than Polkadot Hub TestNet.
error_patterns.resolution: In MetaMask, switch to 'Polkadot Hub TestNet' (chainId 420420417) before deploying. Confirm the Remix environment section shows the correct chain ID.

error_patterns.pattern: Transaction fails with insufficient funds
error_patterns.cause: The MetaMask account does not have enough PAS tokens to pay for deployment gas.
error_patterns.resolution: Visit https://faucet.polkadot.io/, enter your MetaMask address, and request testnet PAS. Wait for the tokens to arrive (usually under 1 minute), then retry deployment.

error_patterns.pattern: Remix shows 'Gas estimation failed'
error_patterns.cause: Contract constructor arguments may be missing or invalid, or the MetaMask account has insufficient funds.
error_patterns.resolution: Verify the initialOwner address is filled in correctly in the constructor arguments. Check PAS balance in MetaMask and top up via faucet if needed.

pages.relevance: CLI-based ERC-20 deployment using Hardhat — use when the user prefers a terminal workflow over Remix.
