examples.scenario: Common scenario: deploy ERC-20 via Remix
examples.user_says: Deploy an ERC-20 token on Polkadot Hub using Remix IDE
actions: Fetch MyToken.sol from revm-hardhat-examples and display to user
Open https://remix.ethereum.org, create MyToken.sol, paste contract
In Remix Solidity compiler, set version 0.8.20+ and EVM version 'cancun'
Add Polkadot Hub TestNet to MetaMask (chainId 420420417, RPC testnet URL)
Connect Injected Provider in Remix Deploy tab, deploy with initialOwner set to wallet address
Verify deployment and mint tokens via Remix UI
examples.result: ERC-20 token deployed on Polkadot Hub TestNet; contract visible in Remix with working name, symbol, mint, and balanceOf functions

examples.scenario: Edge case: user has no testnet PAS tokens
examples.user_says: Deployment failed — gas fee transaction rejected
actions: Direct user to https://faucet.polkadot.io/ and ask them to enter their MetaMask address
Wait for confirmation that PAS tokens have arrived (check MetaMask balance)
Retry deployment from the Remix Deploy tab
examples.result: Account funded with testnet PAS; deployment proceeds successfully

skills.title: Deploy Contracts to Polkadot Hub with Ethers.js
skills.description: Sets up a Node.js project, compiles a Solidity contract with solc, deploys to Polkadot Hub TestNet using Ethers.js v6, and interacts with the deployed contract via read and write script calls. Use when integrating Ethers.js into a new or existing Node.js project targeting Polkadot Hub. Trigger phrases: 'deploy with ethers.js polkadot', 'ethers.js contract deployment', 'use ethers polkadot hub', 'ethersjs testnet'. Covers dotenv credential setup, custom Polkadot Hub provider configuration, and the compile-deploy-interact workflow. Produces a deployed contract with verified read/write interaction.

network: Polkadot Hub TestNet RPC: https://services.polkadothub-rpc.com/testnet (chain ID 420420417, base fee 1000 gwei)
tokens: Testnet PAS tokens from https://faucet.polkadot.io/ — required for deployment gas
wallet: 0x-prefixed EVM private key for a funded testnet account

env_vars.name: PRIVATE_KEY
env_vars.description: 0x-prefixed EVM private key of the deployer wallet. Must be funded with testnet PAS. Edit .env directly — never paste in chat.

env_vars.name: RPC_URL
env_vars.description: HTTP RPC endpoint for Polkadot Hub TestNet. Set to https://services.polkadothub-rpc.com/testnet.

steps.action: Create the project directory and initialize
steps.description: Create and initialize an ESM Node.js project. ESM is required for Ethers.js v6 imports.
Install ethers (v6), dotenv for credential management, solc for Solidity compilation, and TypeScript tooling.

Create .env with RPC_URL pre-filled and empty PRIVATE_KEY. Stop and ask the user to edit .env directly by adding their 0x-prefixed private key as PRIVATE_KEY=0x<their_key>. Do NOT ask for the key in chat. Wait for confirmation before continuing.

Fetch the Solidity compilation helper from polkadot-cookbook. Run:
curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/polkadot-developers/polkadot-cookbook/main/polkadot-docs/smart-contracts/libraries/ethers-js/compile.ts -o compile.ts

If the path returns 404, try without the compile.ts extension or check the available files at:
curl -s https://api.github.com/repos/polkadot-developers/polkadot-cookbook/contents/polkadot-docs/smart-contracts/libraries/ethers-js

Substitutions to apply in compile.ts: none — the file compiles a contract inline or from a local .sol file.

steps.action: Fetch the contract and deployment scripts
steps.description:
Fetch the main deployment script from polkadot-cookbook:
curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/polkadot-developers/polkadot-cookbook/main/polkadot-docs/smart-contracts/libraries/ethers-js/deploy.ts -o deploy.ts

Also fetch the interaction script:
curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/polkadot-developers/polkadot-cookbook/main/polkadot-docs/smart-contracts/libraries/ethers-js/interact.ts -o interact.ts

If a contract .sol file is referenced in the scripts, fetch it as well. Apply ALL of these substitutions across any fetched files:
1. Add 'import "dotenv/config";' as the first line of each script.
2. Replace INSERT_RPC_URL with process.env.RPC_URL.
3. Replace INSERT_CHAIN_ID or any hardcoded chain ID with 420420417.
4. Replace INSERT_PRIVATE_KEY, INSERT_MNEMONIC, or any hardcoded credential with process.env.PRIVATE_KEY.
5. For any provider construction, ensure it points to 'https://services.polkadothub-rpc.com/testnet'.
Run the compile script to generate the contract ABI and bytecode. If the output references a .json artifact file, confirm it was created before proceeding to deploy.

steps.action: Deploy the contract to Polkadot Hub TestNet
steps.description:
Run the deployment script. Save the deployed contract address from the output — needed for the interact script.

If deployment fails with 'insufficient funds': visit https://faucet.polkadot.io/ and fund the deployer account.
If the transaction is stuck or rejected: verify the RPC URL is correct and the account is funded, then retry.

Run the interact script. If it expects a CONTRACT_ADDRESS environment variable or argument, update interact.ts with the deployed address from step 7 before running. Verify the output shows successful read and write operations against the deployed contract.

error_patterns.pattern: Error: insufficient funds for intrinsic transaction cost
error_patterns.cause: The deployer account does not have enough PAS to pay for deployment gas.
error_patterns.resolution: Visit https://faucet.polkadot.io/, enter the deployer address, and request testnet PAS. Confirm balance in MetaMask or via eth_getBalance RPC call, then retry.

error_patterns.pattern: Error: could not detect network
error_patterns.cause: The RPC_URL in .env is incorrect or the endpoint is unreachable.
error_patterns.resolution: Verify RPC_URL=https://services.polkadothub-rpc.com/testnet in .env. Test connectivity: curl -s -X POST https://services.polkadothub-rpc.com/testnet -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"eth_blockNumber","params":[],"id":1}'

error_patterns.pattern: SyntaxError: Cannot use import statement in a module
error_patterns.cause: Project is not configured as ESM.
error_patterns.resolution: Run 'npm pkg set type=module' in the ethers-deploy directory to enable ES modules.

error_patterns.pattern: Error: transaction underpriced
error_patterns.cause: The gas price in the transaction is below the 1000 gwei TestNet base fee.
error_patterns.resolution: Add an explicit gasPrice to the transaction options: { gasPrice: ethers.parseUnits('5000', 'gwei') }. Polkadot Hub TestNet has a base fee of 1000 gwei.

examples.scenario: Common scenario: deploy and interact with a Storage contract
examples.user_says: Deploy a contract to Polkadot Hub using Ethers.js
actions: Create ethers-deploy ESM project and install ethers, dotenv, solc, tsx
Create .env with RPC_URL and empty PRIVATE_KEY (user fills in)
Fetch compile.ts, deploy.ts, interact.ts from polkadot-cookbook
Apply substitutions: dotenv import, process.env.RPC_URL, process.env.PRIVATE_KEY, chainId 420420417
Run npx tsx compile.ts to build ABI/bytecode
Run npx tsx deploy.ts and save deployed contract address
Run npx tsx interact.ts to verify read/write contract operations
examples.result: Solidity contract compiled, deployed to Polkadot Hub TestNet, and verified with read/write operations via Ethers.js scripts

examples.scenario: Edge case: transaction underpriced error during deployment
examples.user_says: Deploy failed with transaction underpriced
actions: Open deploy.ts and add gasPrice override to the deployment transaction options
Set gasPrice: ethers.parseUnits('5000', 'gwei') — 5x the 1000 gwei TestNet base fee
Retry with npx tsx deploy.ts
examples.result: Deployment succeeds with sufficient gas price for Polkadot Hub TestNet

skills.title: Deploy Contracts to Polkadot Hub with Viem
skills.description: Sets up a TypeScript project, compiles a Solidity contract, deploys to Polkadot Hub TestNet using Viem v2 walletClient.deployContract, and interacts with the deployed contract via publicClient and walletClient. Use when building a TypeScript project that needs direct Viem integration with Polkadot Hub without a Hardhat setup. Trigger phrases: 'deploy with viem polkadot', 'viem contract deployment', 'use viem polkadot hub', 'deploy viem typescript'. Covers custom Polkadot Hub chain definition, dotenv credential setup, and the compile-deploy-interact pattern. Companion to deploy-contracts-ethers-js.

network: Polkadot Hub TestNet RPC: https://services.polkadothub-rpc.com/testnet (chain ID 420420417, base fee 1000 gwei)
tokens: Testnet PAS tokens from https://faucet.polkadot.io/ — required for deployment gas
wallet: 0x-prefixed EVM private key for a funded testnet account

env_vars.name: PRIVATE_KEY
env_vars.description: 0x-prefixed EVM private key of the deployer wallet. Must be funded with testnet PAS. Edit .env directly — never paste in chat.

steps.action: Create the project directory and initialize
steps.description: Create and initialize an ESM Node.js project in a new 'viem-deploy' directory. ESM is required for Viem v2.
Install viem (v2), dotenv for credential management, solc for Solidity compilation, and TypeScript tooling.

Create .env with an empty PRIVATE_KEY placeholder. Stop and ask the user to edit .env directly by adding PRIVATE_KEY=0x<their_key>. Do NOT ask for the key in chat. Wait for confirmation before continuing.

Fetch the Polkadot Hub chain definition from polkadot-cookbook:
curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/polkadot-developers/polkadot-cookbook/main/polkadot-docs/smart-contracts/libraries/viem/chain.ts -o chain.ts

Apply substitutions in chain.ts:
1. Ensure the chain ID is 420420417.
2. Ensure the RPC URL is 'https://services.polkadothub-rpc.com/testnet'.
3. If the file uses INSERT_RPC_URL or INSERT_CHAIN_ID placeholders, replace them.
If chain.ts does not exist in the cookbook, create it manually with this content:
import { defineChain } from 'viem';
export const polkadotHubTestnet = defineChain({ id: 420420417, name: 'Polkadot Hub TestNet', nativeCurrency: { name: 'PAS', symbol: 'PAS', decimals: 18 }, rpcUrls: { default: { http: ['https://services.polkadothub-rpc.com/testnet'] } } });

steps.action: Fetch the compilation and deployment scripts
steps.description:
Fetch the deployment script from polkadot-cookbook:
curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/polkadot-developers/polkadot-cookbook/main/polkadot-docs/smart-contracts/libraries/viem/deploy.ts -o deploy.ts

Fetch the interaction script:
curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/polkadot-developers/polkadot-cookbook/main/polkadot-docs/smart-contracts/libraries/viem/read.ts -o read.ts
curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/polkadot-developers/polkadot-cookbook/main/polkadot-docs/smart-contracts/libraries/viem/write.ts -o write.ts

Apply ALL substitutions across fetched files:
1. Add 'import "dotenv/config";' as the first line of deploy.ts.
2. Replace INSERT_PRIVATE_KEY with process.env.PRIVATE_KEY as `0x${string}`.
3. Replace INSERT_RPC_URL with 'https://services.polkadothub-rpc.com/testnet'.
4. Replace INSERT_CHAIN_ID with 420420417n (BigInt for Viem chain ID fields) or 420420417.
5. Replace INSERT_CONTRACT_ADDRESS in read.ts/write.ts with the deployed address (available after step 6).
If any script is not found, check the cookbook directory listing: curl -s https://api.github.com/repos/polkadot-developers/polkadot-cookbook/contents/polkadot-docs/smart-contracts/libraries/viem

If the deployment scripts reference a .sol file or require a compiled ABI/bytecode JSON, fetch or create the contract file first:
curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/polkadot-developers/polkadot-cookbook/main/polkadot-docs/smart-contracts/libraries/viem/Storage.sol -o Storage.sol

Then compile with solc:
npx --yes solc@latest --combined-json abi,bin Storage.sol -o artifacts/

Alternatively, if the script includes a compile step, run it: npx tsx compile.ts

Verify ABI and bytecode artifacts exist in the artifacts/ directory before deploying.

steps.action: Deploy the contract to Polkadot Hub TestNet
steps.description:
Run the deployment script. Save the deployed contract address from the output.

If the transaction fails with 'insufficient funds': visit https://faucet.polkadot.io/ and fund the deployer account.
If the transaction fails with 'fee cap too low' or similar gas errors: add gasPrice override in deploy.ts:
const hash = await walletClient.deployContract({ ..., gasPrice: parseGwei('5000') });
Import parseGwei from 'viem'.

Update read.ts and write.ts with the deployed contract address from step 7 (replace INSERT_CONTRACT_ADDRESS). Then run each script:
npx tsx read.ts   — reads the current stored value from the contract
npx tsx write.ts  — writes a new value and waits for transaction receipt

Verify that the value returned by read.ts changes after write.ts has been executed.

error_patterns.pattern: Error: fee cap too low / transaction underpriced
error_patterns.cause: Viem's default gas estimation produces a gas price below the 1000 gwei TestNet base fee.
error_patterns.resolution: Add an explicit gasPrice override to the deployContract call: { gasPrice: parseGwei('5000') }. Import parseGwei from 'viem'. Polkadot Hub TestNet requires at least 1000 gwei; use 5000 gwei (5x) as a safe margin.

error_patterns.pattern: Error: Chain ID mismatch
error_patterns.cause: The Viem chain definition's id does not match the connected network's chain ID.
error_patterns.resolution: Verify the chain object has id: 420420417. Check with: const chainId = await publicClient.getChainId(); and confirm it returns 420420417.

error_patterns.pattern: Cannot find module 'viem'
error_patterns.cause: Viem package not installed.
error_patterns.resolution: Run 'npm install viem' in the viem-deploy directory.

error_patterns.pattern: Error: insufficient funds for intrinsic transaction cost
error_patterns.cause: The deployer account does not have enough PAS for gas.
error_patterns.resolution: Visit https://faucet.polkadot.io/ and request testnet PAS for the deployer address. Verify balance with: await publicClient.getBalance({ address: '0x...' })

examples.scenario: Common scenario: deploy and interact with a contract
examples.user_says: Deploy a smart contract to Polkadot Hub with Viem
actions: Create viem-deploy ESM project, install viem, dotenv, solc, tsx
Create .env with empty PRIVATE_KEY (user fills in)
Fetch chain.ts from polkadot-cookbook, verify chain ID 420420417 and TestNet RPC
Fetch deploy.ts, read.ts, write.ts; apply dotenv import and process.env.PRIVATE_KEY substitutions
Compile Solidity contract to ABI/bytecode artifacts
Run npx tsx deploy.ts and save deployed contract address
Update read.ts/write.ts with deployed address; run both to verify contract interaction
examples.result: Contract deployed to Polkadot Hub TestNet; read and write operations confirmed via Viem scripts

examples.scenario: Edge case: gas fee cap too low during deployment
examples.user_says: Deployment fails with fee cap too low error
actions: Open deploy.ts and locate the walletClient.deployContract call
Add gasPrice: parseGwei('5000') to the transaction options object
Import parseGwei from 'viem' at the top of the file
Retry with npx tsx deploy.ts
examples.result: Deployment succeeds with 5000 gwei gas price, above the 1000 gwei TestNet base fee

skills.title: Set Up a Mock Runtime for Pallet Unit Testing
skills.description: Creates a mock runtime module for FRAME pallet unit testing using construct_runtime! and derive_impl. Use when you need a self-contained test environment for a custom FRAME pallet before writing unit tests. Configures frame_system with test defaults, satisfies pallet Config traits, and provides genesis storage helpers for different initial states. Trigger phrases: 'mock runtime pallet', 'set up pallet testing', 'construct_runtime test', 'pallet test environment', 'derive_impl TestDefaultConfig'. Requires a completed FRAME pallet in pallets/pallet-custom. Do NOT use for full parachain runtime integration testing.

runtime: Completed the Make a Custom Pallet guide — the counter pallet must exist in pallets/pallet-custom

steps.action: Create mock.rs and add module declaration to lib.rs
steps.description:
Create the empty mock.rs file. Then open src/lib.rs and insert the following two lines immediately after the 'pub use pallet::*;' line:

#[cfg(test)]
mod mock;

The #[cfg(test)] attribute ensures this module is only compiled when running tests, keeping it out of production builds.

steps.action: Fetch and save the complete mock.rs
steps.description: Fetch the reference file and save it as 'mock.rs', replacing the empty placeholder. The file must contain: (1) imports including construct_runtime!, frame_system, and IdentityLookup; (2) Test runtime type and Block type alias via construct_runtime!; (3) #[derive_impl(frame_system::config_preludes::TestDefaultConfig)] for System with AccountId = u64, Lookup = IdentityLookup, Block = Block; (4) impl pallet_custom::Config for Test with type RuntimeEvent = RuntimeEvent and type CounterMaxValue = ConstU32<1000>; (5) new_test_ext() genesis helper; (6) new_test_ext_with_counter(val: u32) helper; (7) new_test_ext_with_interactions() helper accepting counter value and Vec of (AccountId, u32) interactions. If the AI fetch tool is blocked, run: curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/polkadot-developers/polkadot-cookbook/main/polkadot-docs/parachains/customize-runtime/pallet-development/mock-runtime/mock.rs -o mock.rs

Compile the test code including the new mock module. Two auto-generated tests will run: the construct_runtime integrity test and the genesis config build test. Both must pass. If compilation fails with 'missing field' errors, add the missing associated type to the impl pallet_custom::Config for Test block in mock.rs using a sensible default (e.g., type WeightInfo = ();).

files.description: Complete mock runtime: construct_runtime! with System + CustomPallet, frame_system TestDefaultConfig, pallet Config impl, and three genesis storage helpers (default, counter init, interactions init)

error_patterns.pattern: error[E0277]: the trait bound is not satisfied / missing associated type in Config impl
error_patterns.cause: The mock.rs Config implementation is missing an associated type that the pallet's Config trait declares (e.g., WeightInfo).
error_patterns.resolution: Add the missing type to the impl pallet_custom::Config for Test block in mock.rs. Use a placeholder — for WeightInfo: type WeightInfo = ();

error_patterns.pattern: error[E0412]: cannot find type in scope / unresolved import
error_patterns.cause: An import path in mock.rs does not match the current crate layout.
error_patterns.resolution: Verify that import paths in mock.rs match your actual pallet crate name and module structure. Check Cargo.toml for the crate name.

error_patterns.pattern: error: proc macro `construct_runtime` panicked
error_patterns.cause: A pallet in construct_runtime! has a misconfigured index or incompatible Config.
error_patterns.resolution: Verify the pallet name and module path match the actual module. Ensure all required Config associated types are implemented in the mock.

supplementary_context.description: Load when continuing pallet development after mock runtime setup.

pages.title: Unit Test Pallets
pages.relevance: Next step: write comprehensive unit tests using the mock runtime.

pages.title: Create a Custom Pallet
pages.relevance: Prerequisite: the custom counter pallet that this mock runtime is written for.

examples.scenario: Common scenario: create a test environment for the custom counter pallet
examples.user_says: Set up a mock runtime so I can write unit tests for my counter pallet
actions: Create pallets/pallet-custom/src/mock.rs
Add #[cfg(test)] mod mock; to lib.rs after pub use pallet::*;
Fetch the complete mock.rs from the polkadot-cookbook reference and save it
Run cargo test --package pallet-custom --lib to confirm auto-generated tests pass
examples.result: Two auto-generated tests pass: construct_runtime integrity test and genesis config build test. The mock runtime is ready for unit test authoring.

examples.scenario: Edge case: pallet Config has additional associated types not in the reference mock.rs
examples.user_says: Compilation fails with 'missing associated type WeightInfo in impl pallet_custom::Config for Test'
actions: Open src/mock.rs
Add type WeightInfo = (); inside the impl pallet_custom::Config for Test block
Re-run cargo test --package pallet-custom --lib
examples.result: Compilation succeeds with the () placeholder WeightInfo, appropriate for test environments.

skills.title: Write Unit Tests for a FRAME Pallet
skills.description: Writes a complete tests.rs module for a FRAME pallet using assert_ok!, assert_noop!, and System::assert_last_event macros. Use after completing the mock runtime guide to verify pallet logic in complete isolation. Covers all critical test categories: dispatchable success paths, arithmetic guard errors, root-only origin checks, event data correctness, user-interaction tracking, and genesis state loading. Trigger phrases: 'unit test FRAME pallet', 'test pallet logic', 'assert_noop assert_ok', 'pallet test suite', 'test dispatchable'. Requires completed create-a-pallet and set-up-pallet-mock-runtime guides. Do NOT use for integration or end-to-end testing of a full parachain.

runtime: Completed the Make a Custom Pallet guide — counter pallet in pallets/pallet-custom
Completed the Mock Your Runtime guide — mock.rs must exist in pallets/pallet-custom/src

steps.action: Create tests.rs and add module declaration to lib.rs
Write basic operation and event emission tests
steps.description:
Append these tests to src/tests.rs. Call System::set_block_number(1) inside execute_with before any event assertion — events are suppressed on block 0:

#[test]
fn increment_works() — uses new_test_ext(), signs with account 1, increments by 50, asserts CounterValue is 50 and CounterIncremented event with new_value:50/who:1/amount:50, asserts UserInteractions for account 1 is 1.

#[test]
fn set_counter_value_works() — uses new_test_ext(), sets block 1, calls set_counter_value(RuntimeOrigin::root(), 100), asserts CounterValue is 100 and CounterValueSet { new_value: 100 } event.

#[test]
fn decrement_works() — uses new_test_ext_with_counter(100), signs with account 2, decrements by 30, asserts CounterValue is 70 and CounterDecremented event.

steps.action: Write error condition and access control tests
steps.description:
Append these tests to src/tests.rs:

#[test]
fn increment_fails_on_overflow() — uses new_test_ext_with_counter(u32::MAX), asserts assert_noop!(increment(signed(1), 1), Error::<Test>::Overflow). assert_noop! verifies both the error AND that no storage was modified.

#[test]
fn decrement_fails_on_underflow() — uses new_test_ext_with_counter(10), asserts noop on decrement(signed(1), 11) with Error::Underflow.

#[test]
fn set_counter_value_requires_root() — uses new_test_ext(), asserts noop on set_counter_value(signed(alice), 100) with DispatchError::BadOrigin, then asserts ok with RuntimeOrigin::root().

#[test]
fn increment_respects_max_value() — uses new_test_ext_with_counter(950), asserts noop on increment(signed(1), 51) with Error::CounterMaxValueExceeded, then asserts ok on increment of 50.

steps.action: Write genesis configuration and interaction tracking tests
steps.description:
Append these final tests to src/tests.rs:

#[test]
fn genesis_config_works() — uses new_test_ext_with_interactions(42, vec![(1, 5), (2, 10)]), asserts CounterValue is 42, UserInteractions for account 1 is 5, for account 2 is 10.

#[test]
fn increment_tracks_multiple_interactions() — calls increment three times on same account, asserts UserInteractions count is 3.

#[test]
fn different_users_tracked_separately() — account 1 increments twice (total interactions: 2), account 2 decrements once (interactions: 1); asserts each account tracked independently.

steps.expected_output: test result: ok. 15 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored
steps.description: Run all tests. Expected output shows 15 passing tests: 13 pallet tests plus 2 auto-generated mock tests. If fewer appear, verify every function has #[test]. If an event test fails with 'left: []', confirm System::set_block_number(1) is the first call inside the execute_with closure.

error_patterns.pattern: left: [], right: [EventRecord { ... }] / assertion failed on event
error_patterns.cause: System::set_block_number(1) was not called before the dispatchable. Events are suppressed on block 0.
error_patterns.resolution: Add System::set_block_number(1); as the first line inside the execute_with closure for any test that checks events.

error_patterns.pattern: error: assert_noop! failed — storage was modified
error_patterns.cause: The dispatchable modified storage before returning an error, violating the no-op contract.
error_patterns.resolution: This is a pallet bug: all preconditions must be checked before any storage mutations. Fix the pallet extrinsic, not the test.

error_patterns.pattern: thread 'tests::...' panicked at 'called Result::unwrap() on Err'
error_patterns.cause: A dispatchable wrapped in assert_ok! returned an error. Usually wrong origin or incorrect genesis state.
error_patterns.resolution: Check that the genesis helper (new_test_ext vs new_test_ext_with_counter) matches expected initial state, and the origin type matches what the extrinsic requires.

supplementary_context.description: Load when extending pallet testing or proceeding to benchmarking.

pages.title: Benchmark Your Pallet
pages.relevance: Next step: benchmark extrinsics and generate production weight files.

pages.title: Mock Your Runtime
pages.relevance: Prerequisite: the mock runtime providing new_test_ext helpers and the Test runtime type.

examples.scenario: Common scenario: write a complete test suite for the counter pallet
examples.user_says: Write unit tests for my custom counter pallet covering increment, decrement, errors, and genesis config
actions: Create pallets/pallet-custom/src/tests.rs
Add #[cfg(test)] mod tests; to lib.rs
Write imports: use crate::{mock::*, Error, Event}; plus FRAME macros
Write increment_works, set_counter_value_works, decrement_works with System::set_block_number(1) for event checks
Write error tests: overflow, underflow, root-only access control
Write genesis_config_works and interaction tracking tests
Run cargo test --package pallet-custom — expect 15 passing tests
examples.result: All 15 tests pass including 2 auto-generated mock tests.

examples.scenario: Edge case: event assertion fails with empty event list
examples.user_says: My event test fails: 'left: [], right: [EventRecord {...}]'
actions: Open the failing test in tests.rs
Add System::set_block_number(1); as the first line inside the execute_with closure
Re-run cargo test --package pallet-custom
examples.result: The event test passes because events are recorded starting from block 1.

skills.title: Benchmark a FRAME Pallet and Generate Weight Files
skills.description: Implements full FRAME pallet benchmarking: adds benchmarking.rs with #[benchmark] functions, defines WeightInfo trait, wires it into pallet Config, configures Cargo.toml features, builds release WASM, installs frame-omni-bencher, downloads the weight template, and generates weights.rs. Use when a custom pallet needs accurate extrinsic weights before production deployment. Trigger phrases: 'benchmark pallet', 'frame-omni-bencher', 'generate weight file', 'WeightInfo', 'pallet weights production'. Requires a working parachain template with the custom pallet integrated. Do NOT use for estimating gas on EVM contracts.

runtime: A working parachain template with the custom pallet integrated (from create-a-pallet series)
The custom counter pallet in pallets/pallet-custom with mock runtime set up (from mock-runtime guide)

steps.action: Wire WeightInfo into pallet Config and extrinsic annotations
steps.description:
Edit src/lib.rs with four changes:
1. At the top add: pub mod weights;  (expose weights module)
2. After 'pub use pallet::*;' add: #[cfg(feature = "runtime-benchmarks")] mod benchmarking;
3. In #[pallet::config] trait add: type WeightInfo: weights::WeightInfo;
4. Replace hardcoded weight attributes on each extrinsic:
   - #[pallet::weight(T::WeightInfo::set_counter_value())]
   - #[pallet::weight(T::WeightInfo::increment())]
   - #[pallet::weight(T::WeightInfo::decrement())]

steps.action: Update pallet Cargo.toml with runtime-benchmarks feature
steps.description:
Open pallets/pallet-custom/Cargo.toml and add the runtime-benchmarks feature under [features]:

[features]
default = ["std"]
runtime-benchmarks = [
    "frame/runtime-benchmarks",
]
std = [
    "codec/std",
    "scale-info/std",
    "frame/std",
]

steps.action: Update mock.rs, runtime Cargo.toml, runtime config, and benchmarks.rs
steps.description:
Four integration edits required:
1. src/mock.rs: add type WeightInfo = (); to impl pallet_custom::Config for Test.
2. runtime/Cargo.toml: add 'pallet-custom/runtime-benchmarks' to the runtime-benchmarks feature list.
3. runtime/src/configs/mod.rs: add type WeightInfo = (); to impl pallet_custom::Config for Runtime (placeholder).
4. runtime/src/benchmarks.rs: add [pallet_custom, CustomPallet] inside the define_benchmarks! macro.

Run benchmark unit tests generated by impl_benchmark_test_suite!. All three benchmark functions must pass before building the full runtime. These tests verify the benchmark code compiles and the assertions hold.

steps.action: Build the release runtime with benchmarks enabled
steps.description: Compile the full runtime in release mode. Produces the WASM at target/release/wbuild/parachain-template-runtime/parachain_template_runtime.wasm. Build may take 15-30 minutes. This WASM is only for benchmarking — do not use for production deployment.

Install via cargo, or download a pre-built binary from the Polkadot SDK releases page. Replace VERSION with the SDK version tag used by the parachain template (e.g., polkadot-stable2412):
macOS: curl -L -o frame-omni-bencher https://github.com/paritytech/polkadot-sdk/releases/download/VERSION/frame-omni-bencher-aarch64-apple-darwin && chmod +x frame-omni-bencher && sudo mv frame-omni-bencher /usr/local/bin/
Ubuntu: curl -L -o frame-omni-bencher https://github.com/paritytech/polkadot-sdk/releases/download/VERSION/frame-omni-bencher && chmod +x frame-omni-bencher && sudo mv frame-omni-bencher /usr/local/bin/

Download the official weight template for your SDK version. Replace SDK_VERSION with the polkadot-sdk release tag from the parachain template's Cargo.toml (e.g., polkadot-stable2412):

curl -L https://raw.githubusercontent.com/paritytech/polkadot-sdk/refs/tags/SDK_VERSION/substrate/.maintain/frame-weight-template.hbs --output ./pallets/pallet-custom/frame-weight-template.hbs

Save this path — needed in step 10.

Run benchmarks against the WASM runtime and generate production weights.rs. This overwrites the placeholder weights.rs from step 2. The output contains a SubstrateWeight<T> struct implementing WeightInfo with actual measured values. For more precise measurements, add: --steps 50 --repeat 20

steps.action: Update runtime config to use generated weights

error_patterns.pattern: error[E0412]: cannot find type Weight / use of undeclared crate
error_patterns.cause: The weights.rs uses frame_support types with the wrong import path for the SDK version.
error_patterns.resolution: Use frame::prelude::Weight rather than frame_support::weights::Weight for SDK versions that use the unified frame crate.

error_patterns.pattern: Error: No benchmarks found for pallet_custom
error_patterns.cause: The pallet was not registered in define_benchmarks! in runtime/src/benchmarks.rs, or the pallet name is misspelled.
error_patterns.resolution: Add [pallet_custom, CustomPallet] to define_benchmarks! — use underscore form for the crate name (pallet_custom not pallet-custom) and the correct runtime type alias.

error_patterns.pattern: could not compile parachain-template-runtime / runtime-benchmarks feature error
error_patterns.cause: The pallet's runtime-benchmarks feature was not added to the runtime's feature cascade in runtime/Cargo.toml.
error_patterns.resolution: Add 'pallet-custom/runtime-benchmarks' to the runtime-benchmarks list in runtime/Cargo.toml.

supplementary_context.description: Load for context on pallet development prerequisites or parachain template.

pages.title: Create a Custom Pallet
pages.relevance: The custom counter pallet being benchmarked.

pages.title: Set Up the Polkadot SDK Parachain Template
pages.relevance: The parachain template runtime structure required for benchmark integration.

examples.scenario: Common scenario: benchmark the counter pallet and generate a production weight file
examples.user_says: Benchmark my counter pallet and generate weights.rs for production use
actions: Create benchmarking.rs with #[benchmark] functions for all three extrinsics
Create placeholder weights.rs with WeightInfo trait and () impl
Add WeightInfo to pallet Config and update #[pallet::weight] attributes in lib.rs
Update pallet Cargo.toml with runtime-benchmarks feature
Wire into runtime: mock.rs WeightInfo=(), runtime Cargo.toml, runtime Config, define_benchmarks!
Run cargo test -p pallet-custom --features runtime-benchmarks to verify benchmark compilation
cargo build --release --features runtime-benchmarks to build benchmark WASM
Install frame-omni-bencher, download frame-weight-template.hbs
Run frame-omni-bencher to generate weights.rs
Update runtime Config to use pallet_custom::weights::SubstrateWeight<Runtime>
examples.result: A measured weights.rs replaces the placeholder; runtime uses accurate production weights.

examples.scenario: Edge case: frame-omni-bencher reports 'No benchmarks found'
examples.user_says: frame-omni-bencher says No benchmarks found for pallet_custom
actions: Open runtime/src/benchmarks.rs
Verify define_benchmarks! includes [pallet_custom, CustomPallet]
If missing, add the entry with underscore crate name and correct runtime type alias
Rebuild with --features runtime-benchmarks and re-run frame-omni-bencher
examples.result: Benchmarks are discovered and weights.rs is generated successfully.

skills.title: Set Up a Local Development Node
skills.description: Builds and runs a local Polkadot SDK development node with smart contract support and an ETH-RPC adapter at http://localhost:8545. Use when you need a fee-free isolated environment for deploying and testing EVM smart contracts before using Polkadot Hub TestNet. Supports Ethereum tools (Hardhat, Remix, MetaMask, Ethers.js) via the ETH-RPC adapter. Trigger phrases: 'local dev node', 'run node locally', 'revive-dev-node', 'localhost:8545', 'local Polkadot blockchain'. Requires Rust and Polkadot SDK build dependencies installed. Do NOT use as a substitute for TestNet validation — the local node exposes a subset of the Ethereum JSON-RPC API.

runtime: Rust toolchain (stable + nightly via rustup) with wasm32-unknown-unknown target
Polkadot SDK build dependencies — complete the Install Polkadot SDK Dependencies guide first (Rust, clang, cmake, protobuf-compiler)
At least 20 GB free disk space for the release build

steps.description: Clone the official polkadot-sdk repository. The clone downloads several GB of source code. The repository contains both the revive-dev-node implementation and the ETH-RPC adapter.

Compile the Revive Dev node in release mode. Release builds are optimized but take significantly longer (up to 30 minutes). The binary will be at polkadot-sdk/target/release/revive-dev-node. If out of memory, limit parallelism by appending: -- -j2
Compile the ETH-RPC adapter. This translates Ethereum JSON-RPC calls into Substrate requests. Binary at polkadot-sdk/target/release/eth-rpc.

Start the local development node with --dev. This initializes a blockchain with pallet-revive for smart contracts and pre-funded development accounts. The node immediately produces blocks. Leave this terminal running — open a new terminal for step 5. For debug logging, prepend: RUST_LOG="error,evm=debug,sc_rpc_server=info,runtime::revive=debug"

steps.action: Start the ETH-RPC adapter in a new terminal
steps.description:
Open a new terminal, navigate to the polkadot-sdk directory, and start the adapter with --dev. It connects to the local node and exposes Ethereum JSON-RPC at http://localhost:8545. Success is indicated by log lines showing the adapter is ready. For debug logging, prepend: RUST_LOG="info,eth-rpc=debug"

Verify the environment is ready by connecting Hardhat, Remix, or MetaMask to http://localhost:8545.

error_patterns.pattern: error: linker not found / error while loading shared libraries / could not compile
error_patterns.cause: Missing system build dependencies such as clang, cmake, or protobuf-compiler.
error_patterns.resolution: Complete the Install Polkadot SDK Dependencies guide. On Ubuntu: sudo apt install -y cmake clang libclang-dev protobuf-compiler. On macOS: brew install cmake protobuf.

error_patterns.pattern: Connection refused at localhost:8545 / eth-rpc exits immediately
error_patterns.cause: The revive-dev-node is not running when eth-rpc starts, or it crashed before the adapter connected.
error_patterns.resolution: Ensure revive-dev-node --dev is running in a separate terminal and producing blocks before starting eth-rpc. The adapter connects via WebSocket to the node.

error_patterns.pattern: Method not found / eth_method not supported
error_patterns.cause: An Ethereum tool is calling a JSON-RPC method not implemented by the ETH-RPC adapter (e.g., anvil_* or debug_*).
error_patterns.resolution: The local node supports a subset of the Ethereum JSON-RPC API. Use Polkadot Hub TestNet for full API compatibility. Check the Polkadot EVM differences documentation for supported methods.

supplementary_context.description: Load for guidance on connecting Ethereum tools or deploying contracts.

pages.title: Install Polkadot SDK
pages.relevance: Prerequisite: installing Rust and Polkadot SDK build dependencies.

pages.title: Use Hardhat with Polkadot Hub
pages.relevance: Configure Hardhat to connect to http://localhost:8545 for local testing.

examples.scenario: Common scenario: start a local dev environment for smart contract development
examples.user_says: Set up a local Polkadot node for testing my smart contracts
actions: Clone polkadot-sdk from GitHub
Build revive-dev-node with cargo build --release in polkadot-sdk/
Build eth-rpc adapter with cargo build --release
Start revive-dev-node --dev in terminal 1
Start eth-rpc --dev in terminal 2
Confirm ETH-RPC is listening at http://localhost:8545
examples.result: Local node produces blocks; ETH-RPC adapter accepts standard Ethereum JSON-RPC at localhost:8545.

examples.scenario: Edge case: cargo build takes excessively long or runs out of memory
examples.user_says: The build has been running for over an hour and my machine is unresponsive
actions: Cancel the build with Ctrl+C
Check free disk space (df -h) — at least 20 GB required
Check free RAM — at least 8 GB recommended
Restart build with limited parallelism: cargo build -p revive-dev-node --bin revive-dev-node --release -- -j2
examples.result: Build completes more slowly with reduced CPU and memory usage.

skills.title: Set Up Prometheus, Grafana, and Alertmanager for a Validator Node
skills.description: Installs and configures Prometheus, Grafana, and Alertmanager as systemd services on a Linux validator host. Scrapes node metrics from port 9615, visualizes them in Grafana, and routes email alerts via Alertmanager. Use when operating a Polkadot validator node and needing visibility into performance and automated downtime alerting. Trigger phrases: 'monitor validator', 'Prometheus Grafana validator', 'validator alerts', 'node metrics', 'alertmanager email'. Requires a running Polkadot validator node on Ubuntu/Debian with systemd and sudo access. Do NOT use for EVM smart contract monitoring.

runtime: A running Polkadot validator node exposing Prometheus metrics on port 9615 (start with --prometheus-external flag)
network: Outbound HTTPS to download Prometheus and Alertmanager release binaries from GitHub
Server accessible on port 9090 (Prometheus UI) and port 3000 (Grafana UI) from the admin browser

steps.action: Create prometheus system user and directories
steps.description: Create a dedicated 'prometheus' system user with no home directory and no shell (minimal privilege). Create the config directory /etc/prometheus and data directory /var/lib/prometheus, then set ownership.

Go to https://github.com/prometheus/prometheus/releases and copy the tarball URL for your architecture (amd64 or arm64). Replace INSERT_RELEASE_URL with the full URL (e.g., https://github.com/prometheus/prometheus/releases/download/v3.0.0/prometheus-3.0.0.linux-amd64.tar.gz):

sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get upgrade -y
wget INSERT_RELEASE_URL
tar xfz prometheus-*.tar.gz
cd prometheus-*
sudo cp ./prometheus /usr/local/bin/
sudo cp ./promtool /usr/local/bin/
sudo cp -r ./consoles /etc/prometheus
sudo cp -r ./console_libraries /etc/prometheus
sudo chown -R prometheus:prometheus /etc/prometheus/consoles /etc/prometheus/console_libraries
cd /tmp && rm -r prometheus*

steps.action: Create prometheus.yml and start Prometheus as a systemd service
steps.description:
Create /etc/prometheus/prometheus.yml with this content:

global:
  scrape_interval: 15s
  evaluation_interval: 15s
rule_files:
  - rules.yml
alerting:
  alertmanagers:
    - static_configs:
        - targets:
          - localhost:9093
scrape_configs:
  - job_name: 'prometheus'
    scrape_interval: 5s
    static_configs:
      - targets: ['localhost:9090']
  - job_name: 'node_metrics'
    scrape_interval: 5s
    static_configs:
      - targets: ['localhost:9615']

Then: sudo chown prometheus:prometheus /etc/prometheus/prometheus.yml
Validate: promtool check config /etc/prometheus/prometheus.yml

Create /etc/systemd/system/prometheus.service:
[Unit]
Description=Prometheus
Wants=network-online.target
After=network-online.target
[Service]
User=prometheus
Group=prometheus
Type=simple
ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/prometheus --config.file /etc/prometheus/prometheus.yml --storage.tsdb.path /var/lib/prometheus/ --web.console.templates=/etc/prometheus/consoles --web.console.libraries=/etc/prometheus/console_libraries
[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target

Then:
sudo systemctl daemon-reload && sudo systemctl enable prometheus && sudo systemctl start prometheus

Verify at http://SERVER_IP:9090/

steps.action: Install Grafana and connect to Prometheus
commands: sudo apt-get install -y apt-transport-https software-properties-common wget
echo "deb [signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/grafana.gpg] https://apt.grafana.com stable main" | sudo tee -a /etc/apt/sources.list.d/grafana.list
sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y grafana
sudo systemctl daemon-reload && sudo systemctl enable grafana-server && sudo systemctl start grafana-server
steps.description:
Install Grafana from the official apt repository and start it as a systemd service. Navigate to http://SERVER_IP:3000/ and log in with admin/admin (change password on first login). To connect Grafana to Prometheus:
1. Settings (gear icon) > Data Sources > Add data source > Prometheus.
2. Set URL to http://localhost:9090 > Save & Test (confirm 'Data source is working').
3. Dashboards > Import > enter ID 21715 (Substrate Node Metrics) > select Prometheus > Import.

Go to https://github.com/prometheus/alertmanager/releases and copy the tarball URL for your architecture. Replace INSERT_RELEASE_URL:

wget INSERT_RELEASE_URL
tar -xvzf alertmanager*
cd alertmanager-*
sudo cp ./alertmanager /usr/local/bin/
sudo cp ./amtool /usr/local/bin/
sudo chown prometheus:prometheus /usr/local/bin/alertmanager /usr/local/bin/amtool
sudo mkdir /etc/alertmanager
sudo chown -R prometheus:prometheus /etc/alertmanager

Stop and ask the user to provide their Gmail address. Instruct them to generate a Gmail app password at https://support.google.com/accounts/answer/185833 (requires 2-Step Verification). Do NOT ask for the password in chat — the user should edit the file directly.

Create /etc/alertmanager/alertmanager.yml:

global:
  smtp_smarthost: 'smtp.gmail.com:587'
  smtp_from: 'YOUR_EMAIL@gmail.com'
  smtp_auth_username: 'YOUR_EMAIL@gmail.com'
  smtp_auth_password: 'YOUR_APP_PASSWORD'
route:
  receiver: 'email-notifications'
receivers:
  - name: 'email-notifications'
    email_configs:
      - to: 'YOUR_EMAIL@gmail.com'
        send_resolved: True

Replace YOUR_EMAIL and YOUR_APP_PASSWORD with the user's values. Then:
sudo chown -R prometheus:prometheus /etc/alertmanager

Wait for user confirmation that the file is filled before proceeding.

steps.action: Create Alertmanager systemd service, alert rules, and start everything
steps.description:
Create /etc/systemd/system/alertmanager.service:
[Unit]
Description=AlertManager
Wants=network-online.target
After=network-online.target
[Service]
User=prometheus
Group=prometheus
Type=simple
WorkingDirectory=/etc/alertmanager/
ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/alertmanager --config.file=/etc/alertmanager/alertmanager.yml
[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target

Enable and start:
sudo systemctl daemon-reload && sudo systemctl enable alertmanager && sudo systemctl start alertmanager
sudo systemctl status alertmanager  # Active field should show 'active (running)'

Create /etc/prometheus/rules.yml:
groups:
  - name: alert_rules
    rules:
      - alert: InstanceDown
        expr: up == 0
        for: 5m
        labels:
          severity: critical
        annotations:
          summary: 'Instance {{ $labels.instance }} down'
          description: '{{ $labels.instance }} has been down for more than 5 minutes.'

Finalize:
sudo chown prometheus:prometheus /etc/prometheus/rules.yml
sudo -u prometheus promtool check rules /etc/prometheus/rules.yml
sudo systemctl restart prometheus && sudo systemctl restart alertmanager

Email alerts will fire if the validator node goes offline for more than 5 minutes.

error_patterns.pattern: promtool check config fails / YAML parse error in prometheus.yml
error_patterns.cause: Indentation error or incorrect syntax in prometheus.yml.
error_patterns.resolution: YAML is whitespace-sensitive. Verify that scrape_configs uses 2-space indentation consistently. No tabs allowed. Re-run: promtool check config /etc/prometheus/prometheus.yml

error_patterns.pattern: Grafana shows 'Data source is not working' / ECONNREFUSED on port 9090
error_patterns.cause: Prometheus is not running or the data source URL is incorrect.
error_patterns.resolution: Check: sudo systemctl status prometheus. If stopped, check prometheus.yml for YAML errors. Verify port 9090 is reachable from the Grafana host. Use http://localhost:9090 if both services are on the same server.

error_patterns.pattern: node_metrics target shows as DOWN in Prometheus Status > Targets
error_patterns.cause: The Polkadot node is not exposing metrics on port 9615, or the validator is not running.
error_patterns.resolution: Ensure the Polkadot node is started with the --prometheus-external flag. Test the endpoint directly: curl http://localhost:9615/metrics. If the node is on a different IP, update the targets value in prometheus.yml.

error_patterns.pattern: Alertmanager: error sending email / authentication failed
error_patterns.cause: Invalid Gmail app password, or 2-Step Verification not enabled on the Gmail account.
error_patterns.resolution: Generate a new app password at https://support.google.com/accounts/answer/185833. Ensure 2-Step Verification is enabled on the Gmail account. Update /etc/alertmanager/alertmanager.yml and restart alertmanager.

supplementary_context.description: Load for context on validator node setup or key management.

pages.title: Set Up a Validator
pages.relevance: Validator node setup — prerequisite for having a running node to monitor.

pages.title: Offenses and Slashes
pages.relevance: Slashing conditions — understanding which downtime events trigger slash penalties.

examples.scenario: Common scenario: set up complete monitoring for a new validator
examples.user_says: Set up Prometheus and Grafana to monitor my Polkadot validator
actions: Create prometheus user and /etc/prometheus, /var/lib/prometheus directories
Download and install Prometheus binaries; create prometheus.yml scraping port 9615
Create prometheus.service and start with systemctl
Install Grafana from apt; start service; log in at port 3000
Add Prometheus as Grafana data source (http://localhost:9090); import dashboard ID 21715
Download and install Alertmanager; ask user to fill in email credentials in alertmanager.yml
Create alertmanager.service and start
Create rules.yml with InstanceDown alert; restart Prometheus and Alertmanager
examples.result: Prometheus scrapes validator metrics every 5s; Grafana shows real-time dashboard; email alert fires if node is offline for 5+ minutes.

examples.scenario: Edge case: no node metrics visible in Grafana despite Prometheus running
examples.user_says: Grafana shows no data and Prometheus shows node_metrics target as DOWN
actions: Check if the Polkadot node process is running: systemctl status polkadot
Verify the node was started with --prometheus-external flag
Test metrics endpoint: curl http://localhost:9615/metrics
If the node is on a different IP, update the targets value in /etc/prometheus/prometheus.yml and restart Prometheus
examples.result: Node metrics appear in Grafana once the Prometheus scrape target is reachable.

skills.title: Interact with Polkadot Chains Using Dedot
skills.description: Sets up a lightweight Dedot TypeScript client to read chain state and send transactions on any Polkadot SDK blockchain. Use when you need a modern, tree-shakable alternative to Polkadot.js for querying storage, subscribing to events, calling runtime APIs, or submitting signed extrinsics. Trigger phrases: 'use Dedot', 'query chain with Dedot', 'Dedot client', 'interact with chain TypeScript'. Output: account balance, runtime constants, and submitted transaction hash. No testnet tokens required for read-only steps.

network: WebSocket RPC endpoint for the target chain (e.g., wss://rpc.polkadot.io for Polkadot mainnet)

commands: npm init -y && npm pkg set type=module
steps.description: Create a new directory named 'dedot-example' and initialize it as an ESM Node.js project. The 'type=module' setting is required because Dedot uses ES module imports.

Install the 'dedot' runtime package, '@dedot/chaintypes' for per-chain type definitions and IntelliSense, and 'tsx' plus 'typescript' as dev tooling. The '@dedot/chaintypes' package enables auto-completion for chain-specific pallets and storage entries.

steps.action: Initialize a DedotClient and query account balance

steps.expected_output: Free balance: <bigint value representing the account's free balance in planck>
steps.description: Execute main.ts. The output prints the free balance of the queried address in the chain's smallest unit. A zero balance is valid for unfunded accounts — it will not throw an error.

To send a balance transfer, create 'send-tx.ts'. The pattern uses @polkadot/keyring for signing. Replace 'INSERT_DEST_ADDRESS' with the recipient SS58 address and '2_000_000_000_000n' with the desired amount in planck (1 DOT = 10^10 planck).

Fetch the template:
curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/polkadot-developers/polkadot-docs/master/.snippets/code/reference/tools/dedot/sign-and-send-tx-with-keyring.ts -o send-tx.ts

Then install the signer dependency:
npm install @polkadot/keyring @polkadot/util-crypto

Substitutions required in send-tx.ts:
- Replace '//Alice' with the sender's mnemonic URI (use '//Alice' only on local dev nodes).
- Replace 'INSERT_DEST_ADDRESS' with the recipient SS58 address.
- Replace '2_000_000_000_000n' with the desired transfer amount in planck.

Do NOT hardcode a real private key in the file. For testnet use, add 'import "dotenv/config"' and load the seed phrase from SEED_PHRASE in a .env file. Add .env to .gitignore before proceeding.

Run: npx tsx send-tx.ts

error_patterns.pattern: Error: Cannot find package 'dedot'
error_patterns.cause: Dependencies not installed or node_modules missing.
error_patterns.resolution: Run 'npm install dedot' in the dedot-example directory.

error_patterns.pattern: SyntaxError: Cannot use import statement in a module
error_patterns.cause: Project is not configured as ESM.
error_patterns.resolution: Run 'npm pkg set type=module' in the project directory.

error_patterns.pattern: WebSocket connection failed / ECONNREFUSED
error_patterns.cause: Wrong WebSocket endpoint URL or the node is unreachable.
error_patterns.resolution: Verify the WebSocket URL is correct and the node is online. For Polkadot mainnet use wss://rpc.polkadot.io. For Polkadot Hub TestNet use wss://asset-hub-paseo.dotters.network.

error_patterns.pattern: TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'free')
error_patterns.cause: The queried account does not exist on chain (never had a balance).
error_patterns.resolution: An account with no on-chain history returns a default AccountInfo with all balances at 0. Check that the ADDRESS constant is a valid SS58 address for the target chain. Use a funded account or a known validator address for initial testing.

supplementary_context.description: Load these pages when the user asks about connecting to specific chains or using alternative clients.

pages.relevance: Multi-SDK tutorial showing how to query account balance using PAPI, Polkadot.js, Dedot, and other clients side-by-side.

examples.scenario: Common scenario: query account balance
examples.user_says: Use Dedot to check the balance of a Polkadot account
actions: Scaffold 'dedot-example/' as an ESM Node.js project
Install dedot, @dedot/chaintypes, and tsx
Create main.ts with DedotClient connected to wss://rpc.polkadot.io
Query client.query.system.account for the target address
Run npx tsx main.ts and read the printed free balance
examples.result: Free balance of the queried address printed to console in planck units.

examples.user_says: Connect Dedot to my parachain and query a custom pallet
actions: Generate a ChainApi interface for the custom chain: npx dedot chaintypes -w wss://your-node.example.com
Import the generated ChainApi and pass it as the type parameter to DedotClient.new()
Access custom pallet storage via client.query.<customPallet>.<storageItem>()
examples.result: Type-safe access to custom pallet storage with IntelliSense for the target chain's specific APIs.

skills.title: Run a Parachain Node with Polkadot Omni Node
skills.description: Installs the polkadot-omni-node pre-built binary and runs a full node for any compatible Polkadot parachain using an external chain spec file. Use when you need to run a parachain node without maintaining a custom node codebase, or when testing a runtime against a live network. Trigger phrases: 'run a parachain node', 'polkadot-omni-node', 'spin up a parachain full node', 'start omni node'. Output: syncing parachain node with WebSocket RPC at ws://localhost:9944. Requires a chain spec JSON file for the target parachain.

runtime: macOS (Apple Silicon or Intel) or Linux (Ubuntu/Debian) for the pre-built binary path
Rust and Cargo (via rustup) if building from source — see docs.polkadot.com/parachains/install-polkadot-sdk/ for system dependencies
network: Internet access to download the binary and to sync the parachain

steps.description:
Download the pre-built binary for your platform from the Polkadot SDK releases page: https://github.com/paritytech/polkadot-sdk/releases

Find the latest stable release tag (e.g., polkadot-stable2409-2 or similar). Look for the asset named:
- macOS Apple Silicon: polkadot-omni-node-aarch64-apple-darwin
- macOS Intel: polkadot-omni-node-x86_64-apple-darwin
- Linux x86_64: polkadot-omni-node (no suffix)

Replace INSERT_RELEASE_TAG with the full tag name (e.g., polkadot-stable2409-2):

For macOS:
```
curl -L -o polkadot-omni-node https://github.com/paritytech/polkadot-sdk/releases/download/INSERT_RELEASE_TAG/polkadot-omni-node-aarch64-apple-darwin
chmod +x polkadot-omni-node
sudo mv polkadot-omni-node /usr/local/bin/
```

For Ubuntu/Linux:
```
curl -L -o polkadot-omni-node https://github.com/paritytech/polkadot-sdk/releases/download/INSERT_RELEASE_TAG/polkadot-omni-node
chmod +x polkadot-omni-node
sudo mv polkadot-omni-node /usr/local/bin/
```

Alternatively, install from source (requires Rust — see prerequisites). Replace INSERT_VERSION with the crate version listed on https://crates.io/crates/polkadot-omni-node:
```
cargo install --locked polkadot-omni-node@INSERT_VERSION
```

Confirm the binary is installed and accessible from PATH. You should see the installed version number. If the command is not found, ensure /usr/local/bin is in your PATH, or use the full path to the binary.

The polkadot-omni-node requires a chain spec JSON file. The official source is https://github.com/paritytech/chainspecs or the browsable UI at https://paritytech.github.io/chainspecs/

1. Visit https://paritytech.github.io/chainspecs/ and find the parachain you want to run.
2. Click the chain spec entry to open the JSON.
3. Save the full JSON content locally as a file, e.g., 'chain_spec.json'.

Note the exact file path — you will pass it to --chain in the next step. For testing with a custom parachain, you can export the chain spec from a Polkadot SDK node using 'polkadot-omni-node build-spec' or use chain-spec-builder from the Polkadot SDK.

Launch the node with warp sync enabled. Replace './chain_spec.json' with the actual path to your saved chain spec file:

To see all available flags before running:
```
polkadot-omni-node --help
```

To start the node:
```
polkadot-omni-node --chain ./chain_spec.json --sync warp
```

The --chain flag points to the chain spec; --sync warp enables fast catch-up to the latest finalized state (historical blocks sync in the background).

You will see log lines like 'Syncing' and 'Imported #NNNN' as the node connects to peers and downloads state. The node exposes a WebSocket RPC endpoint at ws://localhost:9944 by default.

To stop the node, press Ctrl+C. For long-running production use, configure a systemd service or a process manager.

error_patterns.pattern: polkadot-omni-node: command not found
error_patterns.cause: Binary not on PATH or installation failed.
error_patterns.resolution: Verify the binary was moved to /usr/local/bin/ and that directory is in $PATH. Run 'which polkadot-omni-node' to check. If missing, repeat the installation step.

error_patterns.pattern: Error: The chain spec is not compatible / missing required runtime API
error_patterns.cause: The parachain runtime does not implement all APIs required by polkadot-omni-node (e.g., GetParachainInfo, AuraApi, or required pallets).
error_patterns.resolution: Check the runtime compatibility requirements at docs.polkadot.com/reference/tools/omninode/. The runtime must implement GetParachainInfo, AuraApi, and include the System, ParachainSystem, Aura, and ParachainInfo pallets. See the parachain template at github.com/paritytech/polkadot-sdk-parachain-template for a reference implementation.

error_patterns.pattern: No peers found / node stuck at block 0
error_patterns.cause: Network connectivity issue, firewall blocking P2P ports, or incorrect chain spec.
error_patterns.resolution: Ensure outbound TCP on ports 30333/30334 is not blocked. Verify the chain spec corresponds to an active network. Check the Polkadot Discord for any network outages.

Verify: polkadot-omni-node --version

skills.title: Interact with a Polkadot Chain Using Polkadart
skills.description: Sets up Polkadart — the native Dart/Flutter SDK for Polkadot — to generate type-safe chain bindings, query on-chain storage, subscribe to new blocks, and sign and send a balance transfer. Use when building Dart or Flutter applications that interact with Polkadot or any Polkadot SDK blockchain. Trigger phrases: 'Polkadart', 'Dart Polkadot SDK', 'Flutter blockchain', 'query Polkadot in Dart', 'Dart dApp'. Output: account balance, block subscription, and submitted transaction result.

runtime: Dart SDK v2.17+ or Flutter SDK (includes Dart)
network: WebSocket RPC endpoint for the target Polkadot SDK chain (e.g., wss://rpc.polkadot.io)

steps.action: Create a Dart project and add Polkadart dependencies
commands: dart pub add polkadart polkadart_cli polkadart_keyring polkadart_scale_codec ss58 substrate_metadata
steps.description: Create a new Dart console project. The core packages are: polkadart (main SDK), polkadart_cli (type generator), polkadart_keyring (key management), polkadart_scale_codec (SCALE encoding), ss58 (address encoding), substrate_metadata (metadata parsing). For a minimal install use only 'polkadart polkadart_cli polkadart_keyring'.

Open pubspec.yaml and add the following top-level section (at the same level as 'name:' and 'dependencies:'). Replace 'wss://rpc.polkadot.io' with the WebSocket URL of the chain you want to target. The 'output_dir' is where generated Dart classes will be written; 'polkadot' is the chain identifier used in generated imports.

Add to pubspec.yaml:
```yaml
polkadart:
  output_dir: lib/generated
  chains:
    polkadot: wss://rpc.polkadot.io
```

If targeting a different chain, replace 'polkadot' with a short identifier (e.g., 'assethub') and update the WSS URL accordingly. Save the file before proceeding.

Run the code generator. It connects to the chain, downloads metadata, and generates Dart classes for all pallets, storage items, and extrinsics. The output is placed in 'lib/generated/'. This step requires an active internet connection to the chain's WebSocket endpoint. The -v flag shows verbose progress. If the connection times out, verify the WSS URL in pubspec.yaml.

steps.action: Create main.dart: connect and query account balance

steps.description: Execute the program. It connects to the chain, queries the account balance, prints the free balance in planck, and disconnects. Ensure the address in step 4 is a valid SS58 address for the Polkadot network.

steps.action: Sign and send a balance transfer (optional)
steps.description:
To send a transaction, create a new file 'bin/send_transfer.dart'. This step requires a funded account on the target network. The example below uses the dev account '//Alice', which is only valid on local development nodes. For testnet, replace KeyPair.sr25519.fromUri('//Alice') with a keypair loaded from a mnemonic stored securely in a .env file (use the dotenv package). Do NOT hardcode real mnemonics in source files.

The full send pattern:
1. Load keypair: final wallet = await KeyPair.sr25519.fromUri('//Alice');
2. Get runtime version: final runtimeVersion = await polkadot.rpc.state.getRuntimeVersion();
3. Get current block number and hash for the mortality period.
4. Get genesis hash and account nonce.
5. Build the transfer call: polkadot.tx.balances.transferKeepAlive(dest: multiAddress, value: BigInt.from(1000000000000)).
6. Construct SigningPayload, sign it with wallet.sign(), and build ExtrinsicPayload.
7. Submit and await the result.

See the full working example in the Polkadot docs at docs.polkadot.com/reference/tools/polkadart/. For a production app, store the mnemonic in secure storage — never in source code.

error_patterns.pattern: Could not find package 'polkadart' / pub get failed
error_patterns.cause: Package not added to pubspec.yaml dependencies or pub cache issue.
error_patterns.resolution: Run 'dart pub add polkadart polkadart_cli polkadart_keyring' in the project directory, then 'dart pub get'.

error_patterns.pattern: dart run polkadart_cli:generate fails / WebSocket connection refused
error_patterns.cause: The chain WebSocket URL in pubspec.yaml is unreachable.
error_patterns.resolution: Verify the WSS URL under the polkadart.chains key in pubspec.yaml. Test connectivity by opening the URL in a browser with a WebSocket client. Ensure outbound WebSocket connections are not blocked by a firewall.

error_patterns.pattern: Generated class 'Polkadot' not found / missing import
error_patterns.cause: Type generation has not been run, or output_dir does not match the import path.
error_patterns.resolution: Run 'dart run polkadart_cli:generate -v' and check that files appear in 'lib/generated/'. Update the import path to match the generated package structure (e.g., 'package:polkadart_example/generated/polkadot/polkadot.dart').

examples.user_says: Use Polkadart to check the DOT balance of an account in my Flutter app
actions: Add Polkadart packages to pubspec.yaml
Configure type generation targeting wss://rpc.polkadot.io
Run dart run polkadart_cli:generate -v to generate Polkadot class
Create Provider.fromUri() and Polkadot() instance
Call polkadot.query.system.account() with the decoded address pubkey
Print accountInfo.data.free to console
examples.result: Free balance of the specified account printed as a planck integer.

Run dart run polkadart_cli:generate -v to generate chain-specific Dart classes
Import the generated class and construct an instance with Provider.fromUri()
examples.result: Type-safe access to custom parachain pallets and storage from Dart.

skills.title: Interact with a Polkadot Node Using Python Substrate Interface
skills.description: Sets up the Python Substrate Interface library to connect to any Polkadot SDK blockchain, query on-chain storage (account balance, nonce), and submit signed extrinsics. Use when building Python scripts, data pipelines, or backend services that need to read or write chain state without a JavaScript runtime. Trigger phrases: 'Python Substrate Interface', 'py-substrate-interface', 'query Polkadot with Python', 'Substrate Python', 'substrate-interface pip'. Output: printed account balance details and transaction receipt with extrinsic and block hash.

network: WebSocket RPC endpoint for the target Polkadot SDK node (e.g., wss://rpc.polkadot.io for Polkadot mainnet, or ws://127.0.0.1:9944 for a local node)

env_vars.name: MNEMONIC
env_vars.description: The 12- or 24-word mnemonic phrase for the signing account. Required only for transaction submission steps. Stop and ask the user to add this to a .env file — do not ask for it in chat.

steps.action: Create a virtual environment and install the library
steps.description: Create an isolated virtual environment named 'py-substrate-example' and install the substrate-interface library. On Windows, replace 'source bin/activate' with '.\Scripts\activate'. After activation, the prompt shows '(py-substrate-example)'. All subsequent commands must be run in this activated environment.

Create 'connect.py' with the following content. Replace 'INSERT_WS_URL' with the WebSocket endpoint for your target node (e.g., 'wss://rpc.polkadot.io' for Polkadot mainnet or 'ws://127.0.0.1:9944' for a local dev node).

Fetch the template:
curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/polkadot-developers/polkadot-docs/master/.snippets/code/reference/tools/py-substrate-interface/substrate_interface.py -o connect.py

Then replace INSERT_WS_URL in connect.py with the target endpoint. Run to verify the connection:
python3 connect.py

Expected output: 'Connected to chain: Polkadot' (or your target chain name). If the chain name is wrong or absent, the URL may be incorrect.

Create 'read_state.py'. Fetch the template:
curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/polkadot-developers/polkadot-docs/master/.snippets/code/reference/tools/py-substrate-interface/read_state.py -o read_state.py

Substitutions required:
- At the top of the file, add the SubstrateInterface connection (same as connect.py, replacing INSERT_WS_URL with the endpoint).
- Replace 'INSERT_ADDRESS' with the SS58 address to query.

Run:
python3 read_state.py

Expected output:
  Account Details:
  - Free Balance: <planck amount>
  - Reserved: <planck amount>
  - Nonce: <integer>

The values are in planck (smallest unit). Divide by 10^10 for DOT values on Polkadot mainnet.

Create '.env' file:
MNEMONIC=

Add '.env' to .gitignore:
echo '.env' >> .gitignore

Stop and ask the user to fill in their mnemonic phrase in the .env file. Do NOT ask for the mnemonic phrase in chat. Wait for confirmation before continuing.

Install dotenv:
pip install python-dotenv

Create 'send_tx.py'. Fetch the template:
curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/polkadot-developers/polkadot-docs/master/.snippets/code/reference/tools/py-substrate-interface/send_tx.py -o send_tx.py

At the top of send_tx.py, add:
```python
import os
from dotenv import load_dotenv
load_dotenv()
from substrateinterface import SubstrateInterface, Keypair

substrate = SubstrateInterface(url='INSERT_WS_URL')
keypair = Keypair.create_from_mnemonic(os.environ['MNEMONIC'])
```

Then add the template content below. Substitutions in send_tx.py:
- Replace INSERT_WS_URL with the WebSocket endpoint.
- Replace 'INSERT_ADDRESS' (in call_params 'dest') with the recipient SS58 address.
- Replace 'INSERT_VALUE' with the transfer amount in planck (e.g., 1000000000000 for 0.1 DOT).

Run:
python3 send_tx.py

Expected output:
  Transaction successful:
  - Extrinsic Hash: 0x...
  - Block Hash: 0x...

error_patterns.pattern: ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'substrateinterface'
error_patterns.cause: Library not installed or virtual environment not activated.
error_patterns.resolution: Ensure the virtual environment is activated (run 'source bin/activate' on macOS/Linux or '.\Scripts\activate' on Windows), then run 'pip install substrate-interface'.

error_patterns.pattern: ConnectionError / WebSocket connection refused
error_patterns.cause: Wrong WebSocket URL or the node is not running.
error_patterns.resolution: Verify the URL in SubstrateInterface(url=...). For a local node ensure it's running with --rpc-external or uses the default port 9944. For public endpoints, test with wss://rpc.polkadot.io.

error_patterns.pattern: ValueError: Invalid address format
error_patterns.cause: The address passed to the storage query is not a valid SS58-encoded address.
error_patterns.resolution: Verify the account address is in SS58 format and matches the network's prefix. For Polkadot mainnet, addresses start with '1'; for testnet (Paseo) addresses start with a different prefix. Use polkadot.js.org/apps to validate the address.

error_patterns.pattern: ExtrinsicFailed: Token.FundsUnavailable
error_patterns.cause: The sender account does not have enough balance to pay the transfer amount plus transaction fees.
error_patterns.resolution: Check the account balance with read_state.py before submitting. Ensure the 'value' parameter in call_params covers both the transfer amount and transaction fees.

pages.relevance: Multi-SDK account query tutorial including a Python Substrate Interface example alongside PAPI and Polkadot.js.

pages.title: Send Transactions with SDKs

examples.scenario: Common scenario: query account balance from Python
examples.user_says: Use Python to check the balance of a Polkadot account
actions: Create virtual environment and install substrate-interface
Create connect.py with SubstrateInterface(url='wss://rpc.polkadot.io') and verify connection
Create read_state.py with substrate.query(module='System', storage_function='Account', params=[ADDRESS])
Print free_balance, reserved, and nonce from account_info.value
examples.result: Account balance details printed with free balance in planck.

examples.scenario: Edge case: transaction fails with ExtrinsicFailed
examples.user_says: My Python transfer script runs but the extrinsic failed
actions: Check receipt.is_success — if False, print receipt.error_message for the specific error
If FundsUnavailable: verify sender balance covers transfer amount plus fees using read_state.py
If BadOrigin: verify the keypair matches the sender address in the call_params 'dest' field
examples.result: Root cause identified from receipt.error_message; transaction corrected and resubmitted.

skills.title: Interact with a Polkadot Node Using Subxt (Rust)
skills.description: Scaffolds a Rust project with the Subxt library to interact with any Polkadot SDK blockchain using compile-time type safety. Use when building Rust services, CLI tools, or backend applications that need to query chain state, call runtime APIs, or submit transactions without an external RPC framework. Trigger phrases: 'Subxt', 'Rust Polkadot client', 'interact with Polkadot in Rust', 'subxt-cli metadata', 'type-safe Polkadot Rust'. Output: existential deposit constant, account info, and confirmed balance transfer event.

runtime: Rust and Cargo installed via rustup (https://rustup.rs/)
A Rust project directory (cargo new my_project)

env_vars.name: SECRET_PHRASE
env_vars.description: 12- or 24-word BIP39 mnemonic for the signing account. Required for the transaction submission step. Stop and ask the user to add this to a .env file — do not ask for it in chat.

steps.description: Create a new Rust binary project named 'subxt-example'. All subsequent steps run from within this directory.

Install the subxt-cli command-line tool used to download chain metadata. Check https://crates.io/crates/subxt-cli for the latest version and replace INSERT_SUBXT_CLI_VERSION below:

cargo install subxt-cli@INSERT_SUBXT_CLI_VERSION

As of the current reference files, the version is 0.50.0, so the command would be:
cargo install subxt-cli@0.50.0

This installs the 'subxt' binary globally. Installation may take several minutes as it compiles from source.

Open Cargo.toml and replace the [dependencies] section with the following. Replace version numbers with the latest from crates.io if needed (reference: subxt 0.50.0, subxt-signer 0.50.0, tokio 1.44.2):

Fetch the reference Cargo.toml:
curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/polkadot-developers/polkadot-docs/master/.snippets/code/reference/tools/subxt/Cargo.toml -o Cargo.toml

This Cargo.toml sets:
- subxt = "0.50.0" — the main RPC and codec library
- subxt-signer = "0.50.0" — provides Keypair for signing transactions
- tokio = { version = "1.44.2", features = ["rt", "macros"] } — async runtime

The file also sets the binary name to 'subxt' pointing to 'subxt.rs'. Keep the [[bin]] section as-is.

Use subxt-cli to fetch the chain's runtime metadata. Replace INSERT_NODE_URL with the WebSocket URL of the node (e.g., wss://rpc.polkadot.io for Polkadot mainnet):

subxt metadata --url INSERT_NODE_URL > polkadot_metadata.scale

This saves the SCALE-encoded metadata to 'polkadot_metadata.scale' in the project root. The #[subxt::subxt] macro references this file at compile time. If the command hangs, verify the node URL is reachable and the node is running.

steps.action: Create the main Rust source file
steps.description:
Fetch the reference subxt.rs file:
curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/polkadot-developers/polkadot-docs/master/.snippets/code/reference/tools/subxt/subxt.rs -o subxt.rs

Substitutions required in subxt.rs — each INSERT_* placeholder must be replaced before building:
- INSERT_NODE_URL: WebSocket URL (same as used in step 4, e.g., 'wss://rpc.polkadot.io').
- INSERT_ADDRESS: SS58 address to query for account info (e.g., a known validator address).
- INSERT_DEST_ADDRESS: SS58 recipient address for the balance transfer.
- INSERT_AMOUNT: Transfer amount as a u128 integer in planck (e.g., 1000000000000 for 0.1 DOT on Polkadot).
- INSERT_SECRET_PHRASE: Sender's BIP39 mnemonic. Do NOT put a real mnemonic in the file — instead, store it in a .env file, add .env to .gitignore, and load it with std::env::var("SECRET_PHRASE") at runtime.

For the SECRET_PHRASE substitution: create .env with SECRET_PHRASE= and stop to ask the user to fill it in before proceeding. Wait for confirmation.

steps.expected_output:
Existential deposit: <planck value>
Account info: <AccountInfo struct>
Balance transfer successful: <Transfer event details>
steps.description: Build and run the project. The first build will take several minutes as dependencies compile. Subsequent builds are faster. The program: (1) reads the existential deposit constant, (2) fetches account info for INSERT_ADDRESS, (3) submits a balance transfer and waits for finalization. If the transfer step fails with an error, check that the account has sufficient balance (at least INSERT_AMOUNT + gas fees) and that INSERT_SECRET_PHRASE belongs to the sender account.

error_patterns.pattern: error[E0425]: cannot find value / type in scope — after changing metadata
error_patterns.cause: The generated types from #[subxt::subxt] changed after re-downloading metadata from a different chain or after a runtime upgrade.
error_patterns.resolution: Re-download metadata (step 4) and rebuild. If querying a different network than the metadata was originally fetched for, the type paths (polkadot::storage(), polkadot::constants(), etc.) may differ — check the Subxt docs for the new paths.

error_patterns.pattern: Error: Rpc error: target url is not valid
error_patterns.cause: INSERT_NODE_URL was not replaced or the URL format is incorrect.
error_patterns.resolution: Ensure NODE_URL in subxt.rs is a full WebSocket URL starting with ws:// or wss:// (e.g., wss://rpc.polkadot.io). HTTP URLs are not supported.

error_patterns.pattern: invalid phrase: mnemonic / BIP39 error
error_patterns.cause: INSERT_SECRET_PHRASE was not replaced or is malformed.
error_patterns.resolution: Verify the mnemonic is 12 or 24 BIP39 words separated by spaces. Load it from the SECRET_PHRASE env variable rather than hardcoding. Ensure there are no leading/trailing spaces in the .env value.

error_patterns.pattern: Module 'Balances' / ExtrinsicFailed
error_patterns.cause: Insufficient balance, wrong destination address, or node RPC error.
error_patterns.resolution: Check that the sender account has enough balance to cover the transfer amount plus transaction fees. Verify INSERT_DEST_ADDRESS is a valid SS58 address for the target network.
