actions: Compare SCALE-encoded message bytes against the source page example
Call weighMessage first to confirm the message decodes correctly
Check that maxWeight is at least the refTime returned by weighMessage
If revert persists, reload IXcm.sol ABI from source page to confirm interface matches precompile
examples.result: Root cause identified (malformed encoding or wrong interface); corrected message executes successfully

skills.title: Manage Validator Session Keys and Node Key
skills.description: Walks through generating new session keys on a running Polkadot validator via the local RPC endpoint, recording the output hex, and submitting the set_keys extrinsic on-chain via Polkadot.js Apps. Also covers generating a static node key for a consistent P2P peer ID. Use after setting up a validator node (see set-up-polkadot-validator-node skill) or when rotating keys for security maintenance. Trigger phrases: 'rotate session keys', 'set_keys validator', 'generate node key', 'validator key management', 'author_rotateKeys'. Prerequisite: a fully synced validator node with RPC port 9944 bound to localhost.

runtime: Polkadot validator node fully synced and running with RPC port 9944 bound to localhost
curl installed on the validator server
Docker installed (for optional node key generation with subkey)
network: Polkadot mainnet or Paseo TestNet (for practice)
SSH access to the validator server
wallet: Polkadot.js browser extension (https://polkadot.js.org/extension/)
Controller account with a small DOT balance for the set_keys extrinsic fee

steps.action: Verify the validator node RPC is accessible and fully synced
steps.description: Run this command on the validator server via SSH. A healthy response contains 'isSyncing': false and 'peers' > 0. If isSyncing is true, wait for the node to finish syncing before proceeding — generating keys on an unsynced node and submitting them on-chain wastes an extrinsic fee.

steps.action: Generate new session keys via the rotate_keys RPC
steps.description: This generates a fresh set of session keys (BABE, GRANDPA, ImOnline, AuthorityDiscovery) in the node's keystore and returns the concatenated public keys as a single hex string. Save the hex value from the 'result' field — it will be submitted on-chain in step 3. The hex is a public key bundle, not a secret, but losing it means re-running this step before the next session.

steps.action: Submit set_keys on-chain via Polkadot.js Apps
steps.description: Delegate to the user: Open https://polkadot.js.org/apps/ and connect to Polkadot mainnet. Navigate to Developer > Extrinsics. Select the controller account in the 'using the selected account' dropdown. Choose 'session' module and 'setKeys(keys, proof)' call. Paste the hex from step 2 into the 'keys' field. Set 'proof' to '0x00'. Click 'Submit Transaction', sign with the Polkadot.js extension, and wait for finalization. The new keys take effect at the next session boundary (1–6 hours depending on era length).
steps.expected_output: Transaction finalized; session.KeysChanged event visible in the block explorer

steps.action: (Optional) Generate a static node key for a consistent peer ID
steps.description: If you want the same P2P peer ID across node restarts, generate a static node key using the subkey Docker image. The command writes a 32-byte key to 'node-key.dat' in the current directory. The peer ID is printed to stdout — save it as NODE_PEER_ID. Keep 'node-key.dat' secure; losing it means the peer ID changes on next restart. To use it, add '--node-key-file /path/to/node-key.dat' to the polkadot startup command.

error_patterns.pattern: curl: (7) Failed to connect to localhost port 9944
error_patterns.cause: The validator node is not running or the RPC port is not bound to localhost.
error_patterns.resolution: Verify the node is running: 'systemctl status polkadot'. Check startup flags include '--rpc-port 9944'. Ensure no firewall rule blocks local connections to 9944.

error_patterns.pattern: author_rotateKeys error: 'Method not found'
error_patterns.cause: The node was started without '--rpc-methods=Unsafe', which is required for author_* methods.
error_patterns.resolution: Restart the node with '--rpc-methods=Unsafe'. Only bind this flag to localhost (never expose unsafe RPC externally).

error_patterns.pattern: set_keys fails with 'NoAssociatedValidatorId'
error_patterns.cause: The controller account is not bonded to a stash account that is configured as a validator.
error_patterns.resolution: Ensure the stash account has bonded DOT and the controller is set. The set_keys extrinsic must be submitted from the controller of an active or intending validator stash.

pages.relevance: Installing the validator binary and starting the node — prerequisite for this skill.

pages.title: Start Validating
pages.relevance: Bonding DOT and activating the validator after session keys are registered.

pages.relevance: Hardware requirements and minimum stake constraints.

examples.scenario: Common scenario: initial session key setup for a new validator
examples.user_says: I've set up my Polkadot validator node — how do I set the session keys?
actions: Verify node is fully synced via system_health RPC
Call author_rotateKeys and record the hex output
Submit session.setKeys extrinsic via Polkadot.js Apps with the hex and proof 0x00
examples.result: Session keys registered on-chain; validator is ready to be activated at the next era boundary

examples.scenario: Edge case: rotating keys after a security incident
examples.user_says: I need to rotate my validator session keys immediately
actions: Call author_rotateKeys on the running node to generate a new key bundle
Record the new hex string
Submit session.setKeys with the new hex — old keys are replaced at the next session change
examples.result: New session keys queued on-chain; old keys phased out at the next session boundary

skills.title: Upgrade a Validator Node Using a Backup Server
skills.description: Guides through the zero-downtime validator upgrade process using two servers (Validator A and Validator B). Session keys are rotated to Validator B in session N, take effect in session N+2, and Validator A is taken offline in session N+3 for maintenance. Use when upgrading the Polkadot binary, patching the OS, or replacing hardware on an active validator without missing blocks. Trigger phrases: 'upgrade validator', 'validator maintenance', 'rotate validator backup', 'zero-downtime validator upgrade'. Requires two fully configured validator servers and an active on-chain validator stash.

runtime: Validator A: currently active, fully synced Polkadot validator node
Validator B: second server with same or newer Polkadot binary, fully synced, RPC port 9944 accessible on localhost
network: Both servers: TCP port 30333 open for P2P peer discovery
SSH access to both servers
wallet: Polkadot.js browser extension (https://polkadot.js.org/extension/)
Controller account with a small DOT balance for the set_keys extrinsic

steps.action: Confirm Validator B is fully synced
steps.description: Run this command on Validator B via SSH. Confirm 'isSyncing': false and peers > 0. Validator B must be fully synced before rotating keys to it — rotating to an unsynced node causes missed blocks in the target session.

steps.action: Generate session keys on Validator B
steps.description: Run on Validator B via SSH. Record the hex string from the 'result' field — this is Validator B's session key bundle. Do NOT stop Validator A yet. Save this hex as VALIDATOR_B_KEYS — needed in step 3.

steps.action: Submit set_keys on-chain with Validator B's keys (session N)
steps.description: Delegate to the user: Open https://polkadot.js.org/apps/ > Developer > Extrinsics. Select the controller account. Choose 'session > setKeys(keys, proof)'. Paste VALIDATOR_B_KEYS from step 2 into 'keys'. Set 'proof' to '0x00'. Submit and sign. IMPORTANT: Keep Validator A running — both nodes will coexist until the session change. This does NOT cause an equivocation slash because only the currently-registered keys produce blocks in a given session.

steps.action: Wait for session N+2: confirm Validator B's keys are active
steps.description: Delegate to the user: Key changes are queued in session N and activated in session N+2 (one session queued, one session applied). In Polkadot.js Apps, navigate to Network > Staking > Overview and find your validator. Wait until the 'next session keys' indicator updates to Validator B's key hash and the 'session keys' indicator shows the new keys as active. Session length on Polkadot mainnet is approximately 4 hours. Do NOT shut down Validator A until the keys are confirmed active on B.

Once Validator B is confirmed active (step 4), SSH into Validator A and perform the required maintenance (binary upgrade, OS patch, hardware replacement, etc.). After maintenance, restart Validator A and allow it to re-sync. Validator A is now the standby server. To reverse the rotation in the future, repeat from step 2 using Validator A.

error_patterns.pattern: Validator B not producing blocks after session change
error_patterns.cause: Validator B was not fully synced when keys were submitted, or the node was not running with --validator flag.
error_patterns.resolution: Check Validator B logs for 'Prepared block for proposing'. Verify health via system_health (step 1). Ensure the node was started with the '--validator' flag. If B was not synced, generate new keys on B after sync completes and re-submit set_keys.

error_patterns.pattern: Equivocation slash warning in validator logs
error_patterns.cause: Validator A's keystore was copied to Validator B instead of generating fresh keys.
error_patterns.resolution: NEVER copy the keystore directory from one server to another. Always generate fresh keys on each server using author_rotateKeys. If equivocation occurred, chill the validator immediately and investigate.

error_patterns.pattern: Session keys did not update after set_keys submission
error_patterns.cause: The extrinsic was submitted from the wrong account or did not finalize.
error_patterns.resolution: Verify the extrinsic was signed by the controller account (not the stash). Check the transaction in the block explorer. Re-submit if it did not finalize.

pages.title: Validator Key Management
pages.relevance: Detailed key generation and set_keys submission steps — use for the RPC commands in steps 2-3.

pages.title: Set Up a Validator
pages.relevance: Binary installation guide — use when setting up Validator B from scratch.

pages.title: Staking Operator Proxy on Polkadot
pages.relevance: Using a staking operator proxy to submit set_keys without exposing the controller key.

examples.scenario: Common scenario: binary upgrade to the latest Polkadot release
examples.user_says: I need to upgrade my Polkadot validator binary without missing blocks
actions: Install the new binary on Validator B; confirm it is synced
Generate session keys on Validator B
Submit set_keys on-chain from B's key bundle
Wait for session N+2 confirmation that B is active
Upgrade binary on Validator A; restart and verify sync
examples.result: Validator upgraded with zero missed blocks; B handles block production during A maintenance

examples.scenario: Edge case: Validator B not yet synced when maintenance is urgent
examples.user_says: Validator A is degraded — I need to switch to B but B is still syncing
actions: Monitor B's sync progress via system_health (isSyncing field)
Wait for isSyncing: false before submitting set_keys
Accept that some blocks may be missed during B's sync period
Submit set_keys once B is synced; wait for session N+2 activation
examples.result: Validator B takes over after the next session boundary; some rewards may be lost during sync

skills.title: Deploy a Parachain to the Polkadot TestNet (Paseo)
skills.description: Covers the end-to-end parachain launch workflow on Paseo TestNet: obtain PAS tokens, reserve a para ID via Polkadot.js Apps, generate collator keys with subkey (Docker), build plain and raw chain specs with chain-spec-builder, export genesis wasm and state with polkadot-omni-node, register a parathread on-chain, generate a node key, start the collator, and insert session keys via RPC. Use this skill after completing the set-up-parachain-template skill. Trigger phrases: 'deploy parachain', 'register parachain Paseo', 'launch parachain TestNet', 'reserve para ID', 'parachain registration'. Part of the three-page launch series: set-up-the-parachain-template to deploy-to-polkadot to obtain-coretime.

runtime: Polkadot SDK parachain template built in release mode (see set-up-parachain-template skill)
network: Paseo TestNet — wss://rpc.ibp.network/paseo
Server with TCP port 30333 open for P2P collator connections
tokens: PAS tokens from https://faucet.polkadot.io/ — required for para ID reservation and parathread registration fees
wallet: Polkadot.js browser extension (https://polkadot.js.org/extension/) with a PAS-funded account

steps.action: Obtain PAS tokens from the faucet
steps.description: Delegate to the user: Open https://faucet.polkadot.io/, select the 'Paseo' network, paste your SS58 account address, and submit. The faucet delivers 500 PAS per request. Verify the balance in Polkadot.js Apps before proceeding.

steps.action: Reserve a para ID on Paseo TestNet
steps.description: Delegate to the user: Open https://polkadot.js.org/apps/ and connect to Paseo TestNet (wss://rpc.ibp.network/paseo). Navigate to Network > Parachains > Parathreads. Click '+ Parathread'. Select your PAS-funded account as manager and submit. After finalization, the UI shows your assigned para ID — record it as PARA_ID (needed in all subsequent steps).
steps.expected_output: Para ID assigned and visible in the Parathreads tab

steps.action: Generate collator session keys using subkey (Docker)
steps.description: Run both commands. The first produces an SR25519 key (used for Aura block production); the second produces an ED25519 key (used for GRANDPA finality). Record both seed phrases and Account IDs securely. Never share the seed phrases. Save SR25519 Account ID as AURA_KEY and ED25519 Account ID as GRANDPA_KEY.

commands: chain-spec-builder create --relay-chain paseo --para-id INSERT_PARA_ID --runtime ./target/release/wbuild/parachain-template-runtime/parachain_template_runtime.compact.compressed.wasm named-preset development
steps.description: Replace INSERT_PARA_ID with the value of PARA_ID from step 2 (e.g., 2000). This produces 'chain_spec.json'. Then open 'chain_spec.json' and make these edits: (1) Set the 'id' field to a unique string (e.g., 'my-parachain-paseo'). (2) Insert AURA_KEY and GRANDPA_KEY (from step 3) into the 'aura' and 'grandpa' genesis sections. (3) Confirm 'para_id' equals PARA_ID. Save the file.
Converts the human-readable chain spec to SCALE-encoded raw format. Verify 'chain_spec_raw.json' was created and is non-empty.
Produces 'genesis_wasm' (runtime Wasm bytecode) and 'genesis_state' (initial chain state). Both files are required for the on-chain registration in step 7.

Delegate to the user: In Polkadot.js Apps (connected to Paseo), navigate to Network > Parachains > Parathreads. Find your reserved PARA_ID and click 'Register'. In the modal, upload 'genesis_wasm' for 'Genesis WASM' and 'genesis_state' for 'Initial state'. Submit and sign. After finalization, the status changes from 'Reserved' to 'Onboarding'. Registration takes effect at the next epoch (up to 600 blocks on Paseo).
steps.expected_output: Parathread status changes to 'Onboarding' in the Parathreads tab

steps.action: Generate a P2P node key for the collator
steps.description: Writes a P2P node key to 'node-key.dat' and prints the peer ID to stdout. Save the peer ID as NODE_PEER_ID. Keep 'node-key.dat' secure — losing it changes the peer ID on next restart.

The '--' separator splits collator arguments from the embedded relay chain (Paseo) arguments. '--sync warp' enables fast warp sync for Paseo. The collator first syncs the relay chain (several minutes). Wait for log messages containing 'Parachain synced' before inserting session keys in step 10.

steps.action: Insert session keys into the collator keystore
steps.description: Make these substitutions before running: INSERT_AURA_SEED_PHRASE -> SR25519 seed phrase from step 3; INSERT_AURA_PUBLIC_KEY_HEX -> SR25519 Account ID (0x-prefixed) from step 3; INSERT_GRANDPA_SEED_PHRASE -> ED25519 seed phrase from step 3; INSERT_GRANDPA_PUBLIC_KEY_HEX -> ED25519 Account ID (0x-prefixed) from step 3. Each successful command returns: {"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":null,"id":1}. Do NOT include these commands in chat history — they contain seed phrases. Run them directly in the server terminal.

error_patterns.pattern: chain-spec-builder: cannot find runtime Wasm file
error_patterns.cause: The parachain was not built in release mode or the Wasm path is incorrect.
error_patterns.resolution: Run 'cargo build --release' in the parachain-template directory. The Wasm artifact is at: target/release/wbuild/parachain-template-runtime/parachain_template_runtime.compact.compressed.wasm

error_patterns.pattern: Parathread registration fails: 'ParaAlreadyExists' or 'NotRegistrar'
error_patterns.cause: The para ID was not properly reserved, or the registration account differs from the manager.
error_patterns.resolution: Verify that PARA_ID was successfully reserved in step 2 using the correct account. Use the same account for the registration in step 7.

error_patterns.pattern: Collator log: 'Relay chain does not contain our parachain'
error_patterns.cause: Parathread registration has not finalized on-chain, or chain_spec_raw.json has the wrong para_id.
error_patterns.resolution: Verify the Parathreads tab shows 'Onboarding' for PARA_ID. Confirm chain_spec_raw.json contains the correct para_id. Registration takes up to one epoch (600 blocks on Paseo).

error_patterns.pattern: author_insertKey: 'Method not found'
error_patterns.cause: The collator was started without '--rpc-methods=Unsafe'.
error_patterns.resolution: Stop the collator, add '--rpc-methods=Unsafe' to the startup command, and restart. Never expose unsafe RPC methods to external network interfaces.

pages.relevance: Required prerequisite: build the parachain template locally before deploying to TestNet.

examples.scenario: Common scenario: first deployment to Paseo after building the template
examples.user_says: I built the parachain template — how do I deploy it to Paseo TestNet?
actions: Get PAS tokens from faucet
Reserve a para ID via Polkadot.js Apps
Generate Aura (SR25519) and GRANDPA (ED25519) collator keys with subkey Docker
Build plain chain spec, embed keys, build raw chain spec, export genesis data
Register parathread on-chain via Polkadot.js Apps
Generate node key, start collator, insert session keys via RPC
examples.result: Parachain registered on Paseo TestNet with status 'Onboarding'; collator is running and syncing

examples.scenario: Edge case: collator shows 0 peers after launch
examples.user_says: My collator started but shows 0 peers and is not syncing the relay chain

Confirm the startup command includes the '-- --chain paseo' relay chain section
Add a known Paseo bootnode with '--bootnodes /dns/...' to the startup flags
Wait 10 minutes for peer discovery — initial warp sync connection can be slow

skills.title: Obtain Coretime for a Parachain
skills.description: Guides through obtaining Polkadot coretime for a registered parathread: placing on-demand coretime orders via the Polkadot.js Apps onDemandAssignmentProvider extrinsic, or purchasing and assigning bulk coretime via the RegionX marketplace. Use after the parachain is registered on TestNet (see deploy-parachain-to-polkadot-testnet skill). Trigger phrases: 'obtain coretime', 'on-demand coretime', 'bulk coretime', 'parachain block production', 'RegionX', 'placeOrderAllowDeath'. All steps are GUI-driven. workflow_pattern: conditional-branch — choose on-demand (step 2) or bulk (steps 3-4).

runtime: Parachain registered on Paseo TestNet with status 'Onboarding' or 'Parathread' (see deploy-parachain-to-polkadot-testnet skill)
network: Paseo TestNet — wss://rpc.ibp.network/paseo
RegionX marketplace at https://app.regionx.tech (for bulk coretime option)
tokens: PAS tokens from https://faucet.polkadot.io/ — required for on-demand orders and bulk coretime purchases
wallet: Polkadot.js browser extension with a PAS-funded account

steps.description: Delegate to the user: Open https://polkadot.js.org/apps/ and connect to Paseo TestNet (wss://rpc.ibp.network/paseo). Navigate to Network > Parachains > Parathreads. Locate your para ID and confirm the status is 'Onboarding' or 'Parathread'. If still 'Reserved', the genesis registration has not finalized — wait for the next epoch. Decide: on-demand coretime (step 2, cheaper, intermittent) or bulk coretime (steps 3-4, dedicated, continuous).

steps.action: Option A: Place an on-demand coretime order
steps.description: Delegate to the user (choose this option for testing or low-frequency block production): In Polkadot.js Apps connected to Paseo, navigate to Developer > Extrinsics. Select your funded account. Choose the 'onDemandAssignmentProvider' module and 'placeOrderAllowDeath(maxAmount, paraId)'. Set 'maxAmount' to the maximum PAS you are willing to pay per block (e.g., 1_000_000_000_000 for 1 PAS). Set 'paraId' to your para ID from step 1. Submit and sign. Each order buys one block slot; use placeOrderKeepAlive to prevent account reaping if you place many orders.

steps.action: Option B: Purchase bulk coretime on RegionX
steps.description: Delegate to the user (choose this option for continuous block production): Open https://app.regionx.tech and connect your Polkadot.js wallet. Switch to the Paseo Coretime chain in RegionX. Browse available coretime regions in the marketplace. Select a region, verify the price and duration, and click 'Purchase'. Sign the purchase transaction. After purchase, a coretime region NFT appears in your account — proceed to step 4.

steps.action: Option B only: Assign bulk coretime region to your parachain
steps.description: Delegate to the user: In RegionX, navigate to 'My Regions'. Select the purchased coretime region. Click 'Assign'. Enter your para ID and select 'Finality' mode (for production) or 'Provisional' (for testing). Confirm the assignment. Block production begins at the next relay chain session boundary.

Delegate to the user: In Polkadot.js Apps > Network > Parachains > Overview, find your para ID. Confirm the 'best' and 'finalized' block numbers are incrementing. If they are not incrementing: check the collator logs for 'Prepared block for proposing' messages, and verify the on-demand order or coretime region assignment is correct.
steps.expected_output: Parachain block numbers incrementing in the Parachains > Overview tab

error_patterns.pattern: placeOrderAllowDeath fails: 'InsufficientFunds'
error_patterns.cause: The account does not have enough PAS to cover maxAmount plus gas.
error_patterns.resolution: Obtain more PAS from https://faucet.polkadot.io/. Reduce maxAmount to 1_000_000_000_000 (1 PAS) as an upper bound for Paseo.

error_patterns.pattern: Parachain block numbers not incrementing after coretime assignment
error_patterns.cause: Collator is offline, not synced, or session keys were not inserted.
error_patterns.resolution: Check collator logs for 'Prepared block for proposing'. Verify session keys were inserted (see deploy-parachain-to-polkadot-testnet step 10). Ensure the collator process is running.

error_patterns.pattern: RegionX: cannot connect wallet or Paseo Coretime chain not available
error_patterns.cause: Polkadot.js extension not authorized on app.regionx.tech, or network config has changed.
error_patterns.resolution: Allow app.regionx.tech in the Polkadot.js extension. Check RegionX documentation for the current Paseo Coretime chain endpoint.

examples.user_says: I deployed my parachain to Paseo — how do I get it producing blocks?

Confirm block numbers incrementing in Parachains > Overview

Wait for next session boundary for the assignment to activate
Verify continuous block production in Parachains > Overview

skills.title: Get TestNet Tokens from the Polkadot Faucet
skills.description: Guides the user through the three-step Polkadot Hub TestNet faucet workflow: navigate to the faucet site, enter an EVM address, and submit the token request. Use when a deployment or contract tutorial requires a funded account and the user does not yet have testnet tokens. Trigger phrases: 'get test tokens', 'fund my wallet', 'need PAS tokens', 'Polkadot faucet', 'testnet tokens'. Output: the specified EVM address is credited with PAS tokens on Polkadot Hub TestNet (chain ID 420420417). Note: this is a browser UI workflow that must be delegated to the user.

wallet: MetaMask or any EVM-compatible wallet with an address (only the 0x-prefixed address is needed)
Copy your EVM address (0x-prefixed, 42 characters)
network: Internet access to https://faucet.polkadot.io/

steps.description: Open https://faucet.polkadot.io/ in a browser. The faucet dispenses PAS tokens on Polkadot Hub TestNet (chain ID 420420417) and other test networks. Confirm the page loads — if unavailable, check the Polkadot Discord #faucet channel. This step must be delegated to the user.

steps.action: Enter EVM address and select Polkadot Hub TestNet
steps.description: In the faucet UI, paste the 0x-prefixed EVM address into the address field. From the network selector, choose 'Polkadot Hub TestNet' (chain ID 420420417). The faucet has a rate limit of approximately 500 PAS per 24 hours per address. This step must be delegated to the user.

steps.action: Submit the faucet request and confirm receipt
steps.description: Click the 'Get Tokens' (or similar) button. The faucet processes the request and returns a transaction hash. Verify receipt: curl -s -X POST https://services.polkadothub-rpc.com/testnet -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"eth_getBalance","params":["YOUR_ADDRESS","latest"],"id":1}' — a non-zero hex value confirms the account is funded. Replace YOUR_ADDRESS with the 0x-prefixed address. This step must be delegated to the user.

error_patterns.pattern: Faucet shows 'rate limit exceeded' or 'already requested'
error_patterns.cause: The address has already received tokens within the 24-hour rate limit window.
error_patterns.resolution: Wait 24 hours before requesting again, or use a different development wallet address. For local testing without rate limits, consider forking TestNet using Chopsticks.

error_patterns.pattern: Balance remains zero after successful faucet transaction
error_patterns.cause: The faucet transaction may still be pending due to TestNet latency, or Polkadot Hub TestNet is not added to MetaMask.
error_patterns.resolution: Wait 1-2 minutes and recheck the balance. Add Polkadot Hub TestNet to MetaMask: chain ID 420420417, RPC https://services.polkadothub-rpc.com/testnet, symbol PAS.

skills.title: Renew Coretime for a Parachain
skills.description: Guides operators through renewing an existing bulk coretime lease: manually via the broker.renew extrinsic, or automatically via the enable_auto_renew XCM call from the parachain to the Coretime chain. Use when a parachain's coretime region is approaching its end block and the operator wants to maintain continuous block production. Trigger phrases: 'renew coretime', 'coretime expiry', 'auto-renew parachain', 'broker.renew', 'enable_auto_renew'. Prerequisites: existing parachain with active bulk coretime; DOT/PAS for fees; Polkadot.js Apps with connected operator account. Note: entirely GUI-driven via Polkadot.js Apps — all steps must be delegated to the user.

runtime: Polkadot.js Apps (https://polkadot.js.org/apps/) connected to the Coretime parachain (or relay chain)
network: RPC access to Polkadot or Paseo relay chain and Coretime parachain
tokens: DOT (mainnet) or PAS (Paseo testnet) in the operator account for the renewal fee
For auto-renewal: tokens in the parachain sovereign account on the Coretime chain to cover ongoing XCM execution fees
wallet: Polkadot.js extension (https://polkadot.js.org/extension/) with the operator account that holds the coretime region

steps.description: In Polkadot.js Apps connected to the Coretime chain, navigate to Developer > Chain State. Select broker > regions() to see the current coretime region for your para ID — note the region core and end block. Compare the end block to the current block to determine time remaining. This step must be delegated to the user.

In Polkadot.js Apps connected to the Coretime chain, navigate to Developer > Extrinsics. Select the operator account, choose broker > renew, and enter the core index for the para ID. Submit and sign the transaction. The new region starts from the end of the current region. Important: broker.renew must be submitted during the Renewals phase of the bulk sale cycle — submitting outside this window fails. Check the current phase via broker > saleInfo() in Chain State. This step must be delegated to the user.

steps.action: (Optional) Enable auto-renewal via XCM from the parachain
steps.description: To automate future renewals, the parachain sends an XCM message to the Coretime chain calling broker > enable_auto_renew(core, task, maybe_task_address). In Polkadot.js Apps connected to the parachain, navigate to Developer > Extrinsics, select sudo > sudo(call), and craft a polkadotXcm > send call targeting the Coretime chain with the encoded enable_auto_renew extrinsic. Prerequisite: an HRMP channel must be open between the parachain and the Coretime chain, and the parachain sovereign account on the Coretime chain must have sufficient tokens for ongoing renewals. This step must be delegated to the user.

After transaction confirmation, query broker > regions() on the Coretime chain to verify the region shows an updated end block. For auto-renewal, query broker > autoRenewals() to confirm the entry is present. This step must be delegated to the user.

error_patterns.pattern: Transaction rejected: 'Cannot renew outside Renewals phase'
error_patterns.cause: The broker.renew extrinsic was submitted outside the Renewals phase of the bulk coretime sale cycle.
error_patterns.resolution: Check the current sale phase via broker > saleInfo() in Chain State. Wait for the next Renewals phase — visible as saleInfo.renewalsEnd block.

error_patterns.pattern: XCM for enable_auto_renew fails with 'Barrier' or 'TooExpensive'
error_patterns.cause: The parachain sovereign account on the Coretime chain has insufficient tokens, or the HRMP channel is not open.
error_patterns.resolution: Fund the sovereign account on the Coretime chain via a standard XCM transfer. Verify the HRMP channel between the parachain and Coretime chain is open and bidirectional.

error_patterns.pattern: Region not visible after renewal
error_patterns.cause: Transaction may still be pending, or the core was assigned to a different para ID.
error_patterns.resolution: Wait for finalization and re-query broker > regions(). Filter results by your para ID.

actions: Query broker > regions() on the Coretime chain to find the current region end block
Wait for the Renewals phase of the bulk sale cycle
Submit broker.renew with the core index in Polkadot.js Apps
Verify the updated end block in broker > regions()
examples.result: Parachain coretime renewed for another bulk period; continuous block production maintained

Fund parachain sovereign account on the Coretime chain

Confirm the entry in broker > autoRenewals()
examples.result: Auto-renewal configured; coretime renews each cycle without manual intervention

skills.title: Unlock a Parachain via XCM
skills.description: Walks operators through unlocking a locked parachain: verify lock status via Chain State, encode the registrar.removeLock extrinsic, fund the sovereign account, and submit the XCM message via sudo. Use when a parachain is stuck in a locked state after initial onboarding and needs to transition to self-managing. Trigger phrases: 'unlock parachain', 'remove parachain lock', 'registrar.removeLock', 'parachain locked', 'parachain stuck'. Prerequisites: running parachain with sudo access; sovereign account funded on relay chain; Polkadot.js Apps. Note: entirely GUI-driven via Polkadot.js Apps — all steps must be delegated to the user.

runtime: Polkadot.js Apps (https://polkadot.js.org/apps/) connected to both the parachain and the relay chain
sudo pallet active on the parachain
network: RPC access to the parachain and the relay chain (Polkadot, Paseo, or Westend)
tokens: DOT/PAS in the parachain sovereign account on the relay chain to cover XCM execution fees (minimum ~1 DOT recommended)
Small DOT/PAS balance in the sudo account for extrinsic submission fees
wallet: Polkadot.js extension with the sudo account for the parachain

steps.description: In Polkadot.js Apps connected to the relay chain, navigate to Developer > Chain State. Select registrar > paras(paraId) and enter the para ID. If the result shows 'manager: Some(account)', the parachain is locked. Note the manager account address — the XCM must originate from this account's parachain. This step must be delegated to the user.

In Polkadot.js Apps connected to the relay chain, navigate to Developer > Extrinsics. Select registrar > removeLock, enter the para ID, and use the 'Encode call data' option (do NOT submit). Copy the resulting hex-encoded call data — this will be used as the XCM payload in the next step. This step must be delegated to the user.

steps.action: Fund the parachain sovereign account on the relay chain
steps.description: Compute the sovereign account address for the parachain on the relay chain (deterministic from the para ID — use the subkey utility or Polkadot.js Apps > Developer > Utilities > Derive account). In Polkadot.js Apps on the relay chain, go to Accounts and verify the sovereign account has at least 1 DOT/PAS. If not, transfer tokens from a funded relay chain account. The sovereign account pays for XCM execution on the relay chain. This step must be delegated to the user.

steps.action: Submit the XCM unlock message via sudo
steps.description: In Polkadot.js Apps connected to the parachain, navigate to Developer > Extrinsics. Select sudo > sudo(call), then choose polkadotXcm > send. Set the destination to the relay chain (V3 MultiLocation: parent = 1, interior = Here). Construct the XCM message with: WithdrawAsset (from sovereign account), BuyExecution (weight: Unlimited), Transact (call = encoded registrar.removeLock from Step 2). Sign and submit with the sudo account. This step must be delegated to the user.

After relay chain finalization (30-60 seconds), re-query registrar > paras(paraId) in Chain State on the relay chain. The 'manager' field should now be 'None', confirming the lock is removed. This step must be delegated to the user.

error_patterns.pattern: XCM fails with 'Barrier' error on relay chain
error_patterns.cause: The XCM message lacks a valid BuyExecution instruction, or the sovereign account has insufficient tokens to pay for execution.
error_patterns.resolution: Ensure the sovereign account has sufficient DOT/PAS (minimum ~1 DOT). Verify the XCM includes WithdrawAsset followed by BuyExecution with WeightLimit::Unlimited.

error_patterns.pattern: registrar.removeLock fails: 'NotOwner' or 'NotRegistered'
error_patterns.cause: The account sending the XCM is not the manager recorded in registrar > paras(), or the para ID is not registered.
error_patterns.resolution: Confirm the manager account matches registrar > paras(paraId). The Transact call must originate from the correct parachain's sovereign account on the relay chain.

error_patterns.pattern: Para still shows lock after XCM sent
error_patterns.cause: The XCM message may still be queued or the relay chain has not finalized the XCM execution block.
error_patterns.resolution: Wait for relay chain finalization. Check XCMP or DMP message queues in Chain State. If the message failed, inspect the relay chain event log for XCM execution errors.

examples.scenario: Common scenario: unlock parachain after initial deployment
examples.user_says: My parachain is locked after deployment — how do I remove the lock?
actions: Verify lock status via registrar > paras() in Polkadot.js Apps on the relay chain
Encode registrar.removeLock call data without submitting
Fund sovereign account on relay chain with at least 1 DOT/PAS
Submit XCM via sudo > polkadotXcm > send from the parachain
Confirm lock removed by re-querying registrar > paras()
examples.result: Parachain lock removed; parachain is now self-managing and can operate independently

examples.scenario: Edge case: sovereign account insufficient funds
examples.user_says: The XCM failed with a Barrier error — sovereign account doesn't have enough tokens
actions: Derive the sovereign account address from the para ID
Transfer at least 1 DOT/PAS from a funded relay chain account to the sovereign account
Retry the XCM send with BuyExecution WeightLimit::Unlimited
examples.result: Sovereign account funded; XCM retry succeeds and parachain lock is removed

skills.title: Connect to Polkadot Hub and Get Test Tokens
skills.description: Provides Polkadot Hub TestNet and MainNet network parameters and walks through adding the network to MetaMask and requesting PAS test tokens from the faucet. Use when setting up a new smart contract development environment or when a skill needs TestNet connection reference data (RPC URLs, WSS endpoints, chain IDs). Trigger phrases: 'connect to Polkadot Hub', 'add TestNet to MetaMask', 'Polkadot Hub network config', 'chain ID 420420417', 'configure RPC endpoint'. Output: MetaMask configured with Polkadot Hub TestNet (chain ID 420420417) and account funded with PAS. This skill is primarily a network reference — consult it for endpoint URLs and chain IDs needed by other skills.

wallet: MetaMask browser extension installed (https://metamask.io/download/)
network: Internet access to Polkadot Hub TestNet RPC: https://services.polkadothub-rpc.com/testnet
Internet access to the faucet: https://faucet.polkadot.io/

steps.action: Add Polkadot Hub TestNet to MetaMask
steps.description: In MetaMask, open Settings > Networks > Add a network > Add a network manually. Enter these parameters: Network name: 'Polkadot Hub TestNet'; New RPC URL: 'https://services.polkadothub-rpc.com/testnet'; Chain ID: '420420417'; Currency symbol: 'PAS'. For the Block Explorer URL, check https://docs.polkadot.com/smart-contracts/connect/ for the current explorer URL, as it may change. Click Save and switch MetaMask to this network. This step must be delegated to the user.

steps.action: Request PAS test tokens from the faucet
steps.description: Navigate to https://faucet.polkadot.io/. Select 'Polkadot Hub TestNet', paste your 0x-prefixed EVM address, and click the request button. The faucet credits PAS tokens within seconds. Rate limit: approximately 500 PAS per 24 hours per address. This step must be delegated to the user.

In MetaMask, confirm the PAS balance is non-zero on the Polkadot Hub TestNet network. To verify programmatically: curl -s -X POST https://services.polkadothub-rpc.com/testnet -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"eth_getBalance","params":["YOUR_ADDRESS","latest"],"id":1}'. Replace YOUR_ADDRESS with the 0x-prefixed address. A non-zero hex result confirms the account is funded.

error_patterns.pattern: MetaMask: 'Invalid chain ID' or cannot connect to network
error_patterns.cause: Incorrect chain ID or RPC URL entered in MetaMask network settings.
error_patterns.resolution: Verify the chain ID is exactly 420420417 (decimal) and the RPC URL is https://services.polkadothub-rpc.com/testnet. Remove and re-add the network if the error persists.

error_patterns.pattern: Faucet rate limit: 'Already requested recently'
error_patterns.cause: The address already received tokens within the 24-hour rate limit window.
error_patterns.resolution: Wait 24 hours, or use a different development wallet address.

error_patterns.pattern: RPC endpoint returns errors or is unreachable
error_patterns.cause: TestNet may be under maintenance, or the public RPC endpoint URL has changed.
error_patterns.resolution: Check https://docs.polkadot.com/smart-contracts/connect/ for the latest endpoint URLs. The WSS alternative is wss://services.polkadothub-rpc.com/testnet.

skills.title: Start Validating on Polkadot
skills.description: Syncs a Polkadot validator node with the relay chain, bonds DOT tokens via Polkadot.js Apps, activates the validator, and configures it as a persistent systemd service. Use after completing node setup and session key registration (see set-up-polkadot-validator-node and manage-validator-session-keys). Key capabilities: full and warp sync options with progress monitoring; GUI bonding and activation steps delegated to the user; systemd unit file creation for auto-restarting operation; recovery guidance for zero-peer and stuck-waiting scenarios. Trigger phrases: 'start validating', 'bond DOT validator', 'activate my validator', 'run validator systemd', 'sync validator node', 'validator waiting set'.

runtime: 64-bit Linux server (Ubuntu 22.04+ recommended) with polkadot binary, polkadot-execute-worker, and polkadot-prepare-worker installed in /usr/local/bin/ (see set-up-polkadot-validator-node)
network: TCP port 30333 open for P2P peer discovery
Stable internet connection for chain sync (Polkadot full sync requires 1+ TB NVMe SSD)
tokens: DOT tokens for bonding: minimum self-stake of 10,000 DOT required as of March 2026 runtime upgrade; additional DOT to meet the current minimum active nomination threshold (check https://wiki.polkadot.com/general/chain-state-values/)
Small DOT balance in stash account for extrinsic fees
wallet: Polkadot.js browser extension (https://polkadot.js.org/extension/) with stash account imported
Stash account funded with bonding amount plus fees

steps.description: Replace MY_VALIDATOR_NAME with your chosen validator display name (visible on-chain and in Telemetry — choose a unique name). Network options: use --chain polkadot for Polkadot mainnet, --chain kusama for Kusama, or --chain westend for Westend testnet. Sync options: --sync warp downloads only finality proofs and latest state first (fastest, hours), then fetches historical blocks in background; omit --sync to use full sync (downloads entire chain history from genesis — requires more time and disk). The node must be fully synced before bonding and activation. This is the manual run before systemd is configured; stop it (pkill polkadot) once systemd is set up in step 5.

Poll until currentBlock equals highestBlock. Run this command repeatedly — full sync can take hours or days depending on sync mode and hardware. You can also check Telemetry at https://telemetry.polkadot.io/ and search for your validator name (MY_VALIDATOR_NAME). Fully synced nodes appear white; gray nodes are still syncing. Do NOT proceed to bonding until sync is complete.

steps.action: Bond DOT via Polkadot.js Apps (delegate to user)
steps.description:
This step requires the Polkadot.js Apps GUI — delegate to the user:

1. Navigate to https://polkadot.js.org/apps/#/staking/actions
2. Click the Accounts tab, then '+ Stash'
3. Select your stash account (the account holding your DOT)
4. Set value bonded: at least 10,000 DOT self-stake (minimum as of March 2026) plus enough to exceed the current minimum active nomination threshold
5. Set payment destination (reward recipient address — do NOT use an exchange address)
6. Click Bond and sign with your Polkadot.js extension
7. Wait for ExtrinsicSuccess confirmation

Note: unbonding period is 28 days on Polkadot, 7 days on Kusama. Stop and confirm with the user before proceeding to step 4.

steps.action: Activate validator via Polkadot.js Apps (delegate to user)
steps.description:
This step requires the Polkadot.js Apps GUI — delegate to the user:

1. In Polkadot.js Apps, go to Network > Staking > Accounts tab
2. Click '+ Validator'
3. Set bond amount and click Next
4. Paste the session key hex blob from author_rotateKeys (obtained during key-management — see manage-validator-session-keys)
5. Set commission to at least 10% (minimum enforced on-chain as of March 2026; below 10% is permissionlessly chill-able via staking.chillOther)
6. Set blocked nominations preference
7. Click Bond & Validate and sign with your Polkadot.js extension

After confirmation, your validator appears in the 'waiting' set. It will be selected for the active set when a slot is available in a future era (eras are 24 hours on Polkadot, 6 hours on Kusama). Stop and confirm with the user before proceeding to step 5.

Fetch the official Polkadot systemd unit file from polkadot-sdk. If the fetch is blocked, create the file manually at /etc/systemd/system/polkadot-validator.service with the following content:

[Unit]
Description=Polkadot Validator Node
After=network.target

[Service]
User=polkadot
ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/polkadot \
  --base-path=/var/lib/polkadot \
  --chain=polkadot \
  --validator \
  --name=MY_VALIDATOR_NAME \
  --node-key-file=/var/lib/polkadot/node-key \
  --rpc-port=9944 \
  --rpc-addr=127.0.0.1
Restart=always
RestartSec=10
LimitNOFILE=65536

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target

Substitutions: replace MY_VALIDATOR_NAME with your validator display name; replace --chain=polkadot with --chain=kusama or --chain=westend for other networks. The RestartSec=10 delay is critical — do not reduce it. If the node crashes and GRANDPA votes were not persisted to disk, restarting immediately risks equivocation (a slashable offense).

steps.action: Stop manual node and start systemd service
steps.description: Stop the manually-started node from step 1 (pkill polkadot — '|| true' prevents failure if the node is already stopped), reload systemd to pick up the new unit file, enable the service to start on boot, then start it now.

Verify the systemd service is active (running) and the node has peers and is not syncing. If peers is 0, check that TCP port 30333 is open (sudo ufw allow 30333/tcp; for cloud VMs also update the security group). For ongoing log monitoring: sudo journalctl -fu polkadot-validator. Your validator is now in the waiting set and will be selected for the active set in a future era when a slot is available and your stake meets the minimum threshold.

error_patterns.pattern: Node reports 0 peers after 15 minutes
error_patterns.cause: TCP port 30333 is blocked by a firewall or cloud security group.
error_patterns.resolution: Open port 30333: sudo ufw allow 30333/tcp. For cloud VMs, add an inbound TCP rule for port 30333 in your security group or firewall settings. Mainnet bootnodes are embedded in the chain spec — no manual bootnode configuration is needed.

error_patterns.pattern: Validator appears in waiting set but never activates after multiple eras
error_patterns.cause: Bonded amount is below the minimum active nomination threshold for the current era, or session keys were not submitted correctly on-chain.
error_patterns.resolution: Verify session keys are registered: Polkadot.js Apps > Developer > Chain State > session.nextKeys(YOUR_STASH_ADDRESS) — should return the key blob from author_rotateKeys. Check the current minimum active nomination threshold at https://wiki.polkadot.com/general/chain-state-values/ or Subscan. Bond more DOT if your stake is below the threshold.

error_patterns.pattern: systemd service fails to start (ExecStart path not found)
error_patterns.cause: Polkadot binary not installed at /usr/local/bin/polkadot, or worker executables missing.
error_patterns.resolution: Verify: which polkadot && which polkadot-execute-worker && which polkadot-prepare-worker. If missing, reinstall all three binaries from the same polkadot-sdk release tag and place them in the same directory.

error_patterns.pattern: ExtrinsicFailed or InsufficientBalance during bonding
error_patterns.cause: Stash account does not hold enough DOT to cover the bonding amount plus transaction fees.
error_patterns.resolution: Check stash account balance in Polkadot.js Apps. Ensure the account has more DOT than the intended bond amount (reserve at least 0.1 DOT for fees). Fund the stash account before retrying.

examples.scenario: Common scenario: complete the final phase of validator setup
examples.user_says: I've installed the polkadot binary and generated session keys. How do I start validating?
actions: Start the node with --sync warp to catch up to the chain head quickly
Poll system_syncState until currentBlock equals highestBlock
Delegate bonding to user: Polkadot.js Apps > Network > Staking > Accounts > + Stash
Delegate activation to user: Bond & Validate with session key hex and commission at least 10%
Fetch and save polkadot-validator.service systemd unit file
Stop manual node, daemon-reload, enable and start systemd service
Verify health via system_health RPC — confirm peers > 0 and isSyncing false
examples.result: Validator running as a systemd service and appearing in the waiting set, ready to be selected for active validation in a future era

examples.scenario: Edge case: validator stuck at 0 peers after service start
examples.user_says: My validator started but shows 0 peers and isSyncing is stuck
actions: Check firewall: sudo ufw status — verify port 30333 is allowed
For cloud VMs, check security group inbound rules for TCP 30333
Run: sudo journalctl -fu polkadot-validator | grep -i peer
If port is blocked: sudo ufw allow 30333/tcp && sudo systemctl restart polkadot-validator
examples.result: Node connects to peers and resumes syncing; peer count increases over the following minutes
